nodes.name: Button
nodes.description: Represents an action, action hotspot, or button on a form or document.

events.name: Click
events.description: Occurs when the user selects the action, action hotspot, or button.

events.name: ObjectExecute
events.description: Occurs when an action, button, or hotspot is activated by an OLE2 server that is FX/Notesflow enabled.

events.name: QueryClick
events.description: Occurs before the Click event fires, allowing it to be cancelled.

nodes.name: Field
nodes.description: Represents a field on a form.

events.name: Entering
events.description: Occurs when the user enters a field in Edit mode.

events.name: Exiting
events.description: Occurs when the user exits a field in Edit mode.

events.name: OnBlur
events.description: Occurs when the user exits a field in Edit mode.

events.name: OnChange
events.description: Occurs when the user changes a field in Edit mode.

events.name: OnFocus
events.description: Occurs when the user enters a field in Edit mode.

nodes.name: Navigator
nodes.description: Represents a navigator button, hotspot, or other navigator object.

events.name: Click
events.description: Occurs when the user selects a navigator object.

nodes.name: NotesACL
nodes.description: Represents the access control list (ACL) of a database.

props.name: AdministrationServer
props.description: Read-write. The name of the administration server for a database.
props.syntax: To get: serverName$ = notesACL.AdministrationServer To set: notesACL.AdministrationServer = serverName$

props.name: InternetLevel
props.description: Read-write. Indicates the maximum Internet access level for this database.
props.syntax: To get: level% = notesACL.InternetLevel To set: notesACL.InternetLevel = level%

props.name: IsAdminNames
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether the administration server for the database can modify all Names fields in a database.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesACL.IsAdminNames To set: notesACL.IsAdminNames = flag

props.name: IsAdminReaderAuthor
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether the administration server for the database can modify Readers and Authors fields in a database.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesACL.IsAdminReaderAuthor To set: notesACL.IsAdminReaderAuthor = flag

props.name: IsExtendedAccess
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether extended access is enabled.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesACL.IsExtendedAccess To set: notesACL.IsExtendedAccess = flag

props.name: Parent
props.description: Read-only. The database that owns an ACL.

props.name: Roles
props.description: Read-only. All the roles defined in an access control list.

props.name: UniformAccess
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether uniform access is enforced.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesACL.UniformAccess To set: notesACL.UniformAccess = flag

methods.name: AddRole

params.name: name$
params.description: The name of the new role. The recommended style is to use just the name, with no parentheses or brackets around it. If brackets surround the name, they will be stripped and the enclosed name used.

methods.description: Adds a role to an ACL.

methods.name: CreateACLEntry

params.name: name$
params.description: The name of the person, group, or server for whom you want to create an entry in the ACL.

params.name: level%
params.description: The level you want to assign to this person, group, or server in the ACL. May be any of the following:

params.name: Level
params.description: Description

params.name: ACLLEVEL_NOACCESS (0)
params.description: No access

params.name: ACLLEVEL_DEPOSITOR (1)
params.description: Depositor access

params.name: ACLLEVEL_READER (2)
params.description: Reader access

params.name: ACLLEVEL_AUTHOR (3)
params.description: Author access

params.name: ACLLEVEL_EDITOR (4)
params.description: Editor access

params.name: ACLLEVEL_DESIGNER (5)
params.description: Designer access

params.name: ACLLEVEL_MANAGER (6)
params.description: Manager access

returns.name: notesACLEntry
returns.description: NotesACLEntry. The newly created entry.

methods.description: You must call Save on the ACL if you want the modified ACL to be saved to disk. For more information, see the "NotesACLEntry class."

methods.name: DeleteRole

params.name: name$
params.description: The name of the role to remove. The recommended style is to use just the name, with no parentheses or brackets around it. If brackets surround the name, they will be stripped and the enclosed name used.

methods.description: If the role you specified does not exist in the ACL, Notes® raises the error "Role name not found."

methods.name: GetEntry

params.name: name$
params.description: The name for which you want to find the ACL entry. The name must be an exact match, including case.

returns.name: notesACLEntry
returns.description: NotesACLEntry. The ACL entry that matches name$. If name$ is not in the ACL, it returns Nothing.

methods.description: GetEntry can find people, groups, or servers in an ACL. If a person is not listed explicitly in the ACL, but is a member of a group that's listed in the ACL, GetEntry does not find that person's name.

methods.name: GetFirstEntry

returns.name: notesACLEntry
returns.description: NotesACLEntry. The first entry in the ACL.

methods.description: Returns the first entry in an ACL, usually the Default entry.

methods.name: GetNextEntry

params.name: entry
params.description: Any entry in the ACL. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesACLEntry
returns.description: NotesACLEntry. The entry following the specified entry in the ACL. Returns Nothing if there are no more entries.

methods.description: The order of the ACL entries is unspecified. The order is not alphabetical and does not correspond to UI displays.

methods.name: RemoveACLEntry

params.name: name$
params.description: The name of the person, group, or server for which you want to remove the entry. You must supply the complete name, but hierarchical names can be in abbreviated format. Case is not significant.

methods.description: You must call Save on the ACL if you want the modified ACL to be saved to disk.

methods.name: RenameRole

params.name: oldName$
params.description: The current name of the role. The recommended style is to use just the name, with no parentheses or brackets around it. If brackets surround the name, they will be stripped and the enclosed name used.

params.name: newName$
params.description: The new name you want to give to the role. The recommended style is to use just the name, with no parentheses or square brackets around it. If square brackets surround the name, they will be stripped and the enclosed name used.

methods.description: When you rename a role, any entries in the ACL that had the old role get the new role.

methods.name: Save
methods.description: Saves the changes you made to the ACL. If you don't call Save before closing a database, the changes you made to its ACL will be lost.

nodes.name: NotesACLEntry
nodes.description: Represents a single entry in an access control list. An entry may be for a person, a group, or a server.

props.name: CanCreateDocuments
props.description: Read-write. For an entry with Author access to a database, indicates whether the entry is allowed to create new documents.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesACLEntry .CanCreateDocuments To set: notesACLEntry .CanCreateDocuments = flag

props.name: CanCreateLSOrJavaAgent
props.description: Read-write. For an entry with Reader access, indicates whether the entry is allowed to create LotusScript® or Java™ agents.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesACLEntry .CanCreateLSOrJavaAgent To set: notesACLEntry .CanCreateLSOrJavaAgent = flag

props.name: CanCreatePersonalAgent
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether an entry can create private agents in a database.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesACLEntry .CanCreatePersonalAgent To set: notesACLEntry .CanCreatePersonalAgent = flag

props.name: CanCreatePersonalFolder
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether an entry can create personal folders in a database.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesACLEntry .CanCreatePersonalFolder To set: notesACLEntry .CanCreatePersonalFolder = flag

props.name: CanCreateSharedFolder
props.description: Read-write. For an entry with Editor access to the database, indicates whether the entry can create shared folders.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesACLEntry .CanCreateSharedFolder To set: notesACLEntry .CanCreateSharedFolder = flag

props.name: CanDeleteDocuments
props.description: Read-write. For an entry with Author access or higher to a database, indicates whether an entry can delete documents.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesACLEntry .CanDeleteDocuments To set: notesACLEntry .CanDeleteDocuments = flag

props.name: CanReplicateOrCopyDocuments
props.description: Read-write. For an entry with Reader access or higher to a database, indicates whether an entry can replicate or copy documents.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesACLEntry .CanReplicateOrCopyDocuments To set: notesACLEntry .CanReplicateOrCopyDocuments = flag

props.name: IsAdminReaderAuthor
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether an entry that is an administration server can modify Readers and Authors fields in a database.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesACLEntry .IsAdminReaderAuthor To set: notesACLEntry .IsAdminReaderAuthor = flag

props.name: IsAdminServer
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether this entry is the administration server for a database.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesACLEntry .IsAdminServer To set: notesACLEntry .IsAdminServer = flag

props.name: IsGroup
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether this is a group entry.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesACLEntry .IsGroup To set: notesACLEntry .IsGroup = flag

props.name: IsPerson
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether this is a person entry.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesACLEntry .IsPerson To set: notesACLEntry .IsPerson = flag

props.name: IsPublicReader
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether the current entry is a public reader of the database.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesACLEntry .IsPublicReader To set: notesACLEntry .IsPublicReader = flag

props.name: IsPublicWriter
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether the current entry is a public writer of the database.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesACLEntry .IsPublicWriter To set: notesACLEntry .IsPublicWriter = flag

props.name: IsServer
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether this is a server entry.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesACLEntry .IsServer To set: notesACLEntry .IsServer = flag

props.name: Level
props.description: Read-write. The access level this entry has for this database.
props.syntax: To get: level% = notesACLEntry .Level To set: notesACLEntry .Level = level%

props.name: Name
props.description: Read-write. The name of an entry.
props.syntax: To get: name$ = notesACLEntry .Name To set: notesACLEntry .Name = name$

props.name: NameObject
props.description: Read-only. The name of an entry.

props.name: Parent
props.description: Read-only. The access control list that contains an entry.

props.name: Roles
props.description: Read-only. The roles that are enabled for an entry.

props.name: UserType
props.description: Read-write. Indicates the type of user for a particular entry.
props.syntax: To get: usertype% = notesACLEntry .UserType To set: notesACLEntry .UserType = usertype%

methods.name: DisableRole

params.name: name$
params.description: The name of the role to disable. The recommended style is to use just the name, with no parentheses or brackets around it. If brackets surround the name, they will be stripped and the enclosed name used.

methods.description: If the role does not exist in the access control list, this method raises the error "Role name not found." If the role exists in the ACL, but is already disabled for the entry, this method does nothing.

methods.name: EnableRole

params.name: name$
params.description: The name of the role to enable. The recommended style is to use just the name, with no parentheses or brackets around it. If brackets surround the name, they will be stripped and the enclosed name used.

methods.description: If the role does not exist in the access control list, this method raises the error "Role name not found." If the role exists in the ACL, and is already enabled for the entry, this method does nothing.

methods.name: IsRoleEnabled

params.name: name$
params.description: The name of the role. The recommended style is to use just the name, with no parentheses or brackets around it. If brackets surround the name, they will be stripped and the enclosed name used.

methods.description: Given a role, indicates whether the role is enabled for an entry.

methods.name: New

params.name: notesACL
params.description: The ACL that contains the entry you're creating.

params.name: name$
params.description: The name of the person, group, or server for whom you want to create an entry in the ACL.

params.name: level%
params.description: The level you want to assign to this person, group, or server in the ACL. May be any of the following:

methods.description: New creates an entry in an ACL with the name and level that you specify.
methods.syntax: Dim variableName As New NotesACLEntry( notesACL , name$ , level% ) Set notesACLEntry = New NotesACLEntry( notesACL , name$ , level% )

methods.name: Remove
methods.description: Once an entry is removed from an ACL, that person, server, or group may still access the database at the level permitted by the Default entry.

params.name: group$
params.description: The name of the group.

params.name: members
params.description: The names of the members in the group.

returns.name: String
returns.description: String. The note ID of an entry created in the Administration Requests database.

methods.description: This method enters the following administration request: Add or Modify Group in Domino® Directory. If the group exists, the members are added to that group. If the group does not exist, a new multi-purpose group is created containing the members.

methods.name: AddInternetCertificateToUser

params.name: user$
params.description: The full hierarchical name (can be abbreviated) of the user.

params.name: keyringfile$
params.description: The pathname of the key ring file containing the Internet certificate.

params.name: keyringpassword$
params.description: The password of the key ring file.

params.name: expiration
params.description: The expiration date for the certificate.

returns.name: String
returns.description: String. The note ID of an entry created in the Administration Requests database.

methods.description: The administration process is a program that automates routine administrative tasks. For more information, see "Add Internet Certificate to Person Record" in Administration Help. If this method is used with the certifier ID process, it requires an Internet certificate in a key ring file. See "Creating a server key ring file" in Administration Help. If this method is used with the CA process, no server key ring file is needed, the keyringname$ and keyringpassword$ parameters should be called with null text values "", and the NotesAdministrationProcess object's UseCertificateAuthority property must be set to True prior to calling the method.
methods.syntax: noteID$ = notesAdministrationProcess .AddInternetCertificateToUser( user$ , keyringfile$ , keyringpassword$ [ , expiration ] )

methods.description: If the cluster does not exist, a new cluster is created and the server added to it.

methods.name: ConfigureMailAgent

params.name: username$
params.description: The full hierarchical name (can be abbreviated) of the user.

params.name: agentname$
params.description: The name of the agent.

params.name: activatable%
params.description: True (default) to make the agent activatable.

params.name: enable%
params.description: True (default) to enable the agent.

returns.name: String
returns.description: String. The note ID of an entry created in the Administration Requests database.

methods.description: This method specifies username as the value of "Run on behalf of" under the security tab in the Agent Properties box. This is useful to allow scheduled agents on a mail database to run under the authority of the mail user. If activatable is True, users with Editor access can enable and disable the agent without resigning it. This method must be executed by the mail file owner, and was designed to allow mail file owners with Editor access to enable and disable scheduled agents in their own mail files. It will not allow a user to enable a scheduled agent in a mail file that is owned by someone else. After the agent is configured, you can use NotesAgent to reset activatable and enable.
methods.syntax: noteID$ = notesAdministrationProcess .ConfigureMailAgent( username$ , agentname$ [ , activatable ] [ , enable ] )

methods.name: CreateReplica

params.name: sourceserver$
params.description: The full hierarchical name (can be abbreviated) of the server containing the database being replicated. An empty string ("") means the local server.

params.name: sourcedbfile$
params.description: The pathname of the database being replicated relative to the data directory.

params.name: destserver$
params.description: The full hierarchical name (can be abbreviated) of the server containing the replica. An empty string ("") means the local server.

params.name: destdbfile$
params.description: Optional. The pathname of the new database relative to the data directory. Defaults to the same pathname as the source file.

params.name: copyacl
params.description: Optional.

params.name: createftindex
params.description: Optional.

returns.name: String
returns.description: String. The note ID of an entry created in the Administration Requests database.

methods.description: This method triggers "Check access" and "Create replica" administration process requests for unclustered servers, or clustered servers in different clusters. This method triggers "Check access" and "Accelerated create replica" administration process requests for servers in the same cluster.
methods.syntax: noteID$ = notesAdministrationProcess .CreateReplica( sourceserver$ , sourcedbfile$ [ , destserver$ ] [ , destdbfile$ ] [ , copyacl ] [ , createftindex ] )

methods.name: MoveMailUser

params.name: username$
params.description: The full hierarchical name (can be abbreviated) of the user.

params.name: newhomeserver$
params.description: The full hierarchical name (can be abbreviated) of the new home server for the user's mail file.

params.name: newhomeservermailpath$
params.description: The path name of the new directory or folder for the user's mail file relative to the server's data directory. The path name does not include the file name.

params.name: usescos
params.description: Optional. If True, the new mail file makes use of the shared mail feature. Defaults to False.

params.name: newclusterreplicaarray$
params.description: Optional. Replicas of the mail file in the same cluster as the new home server. The array must consist of pairs of elements. The first element of the pair specifies the name of a server in the same cluster as the new home server. The second element specifies the path in the same form as the new home server mail path.

params.name: deleteoldclusterreplicas$
params.description: Optional. If True, deletes existing replicas of the mail file on servers in the same cluster as the existing home server. Defaults to False. Used only if the user is being moved off a cluster.

returns.name: String
returns.description: String. The note ID of an entry created in the Administration Requests database.

methods.description: Enters a request in the Administration Requests database to move a user's mail file.
methods.syntax: noteID$ = notesAdministrationProcess .MoveMailUser( username$ , newhomeserver$ , newhomeservermailpath$ [ , usescos ] [ , newclusterreplicaarray$ ] [ , deleteoldclusterreplicas ] )

The Domino® user's net user name, if defined, or short name is used as the Windows™ user name. The new values of lastname, firstname, and middleinitial (the common name in Domino®) are used to specify the the Window user's new full name. You must specify at least one of these parameters; unspecified parameters default to the user entry in the Domino® Directory. Defaults to False.

nodes.name: NotesAgent
nodes.description: Represents an agent. The agent may be shared or private, and may be a Release 3 macro.

props.name: Comment
props.description: Read-only. The comment that describes an agent, as entered by the agent's designer.

props.name: CommonOwner
props.description: Read-only. The name of the person who last modified and saved an agent (the owner of the agent). If the owner has a hierarchical name, only the common name is returned.

props.name: HasRunSinceModified
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether an agent has run since it was created or last modified.

props.name: HttpURL
props.description: Read-only. The Domino® URL of an agent when HTTP protocols are in effect.

props.name: IsActivatable
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether user activation is in effect when enabling or disabling a scheduled agent.

props.name: IsEnabled
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether an agent is able to run or not.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesAgent.IsEnabled To set: notesAgent.IsEnabled = flag

props.name: IsNotesAgent
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether an agent can run in the Notes® client environment.

props.name: IsPublic
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether an agent is shared or private.

props.name: IsWebAgent
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether an agent can run in a Web browser environment.

props.name: LastRun
props.description: Read-only. The date that an agent last ran.

props.name: LockHolders
props.description: Read-only. The names of the holders of a lock.

props.name: Name
props.description: Read-only. The name of an agent. Within a database, the name of an agent may not be unique.

props.name: NotesURL
props.description: Read-only. The Domino® URL of an agent when Notes® protocols are in effect.

props.name: OnBehalfOf
props.description: Read-only. Name of the user under whose identity the agent runs.

props.name: Owner
props.description: Read-only. The name of the person who last modified and saved an agent (the owner of the agent).

props.name: ParameterDocID
props.description: Use GetDocumentByID method in NotesDatabase to retrieve a document with its note ID.

props.name: Parent
props.description: Read-only. The database that contains an agent.

props.name: ProhibitDesignUpdate
props.description: If you change this property, you must call Save to commit the change. You must have the appropriate access privileges to both the database and to the agent. You cannot change the current agent.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesAgent.ProhibitDesignUpdate To set: notesAgent.ProhibitDesignUpdate = flag

props.name: ServerName
props.description: Read-write. The name of the server on which an agent is set to run. This is a property of the design element, not of the current run of the agent.
props.syntax: To get: server$ = notesAgent.ServerName To set: notesAgent.ServerName = server$

props.name: Target
props.type: Boolean or Constant of type integer
props.description: Read-only. Indicates which documents on which this agent acts.

props.name: Trigger
props.type: Boolean or Constant of type integer
props.description: Read-only. Indicates when this agent runs.

methods.name: GetPerformanceDocument

returns.name: notesDocument
returns.description: NotesDocument. The profiling results. This object is considered read-only; results are undefined if you write to it. Returns Nothing if no profiling results are available.

methods.description: Use of the returned NotesDocument object as the second parameter of EditDocument in NotesUIWorkspace opens the profiling results as a UI document. Profiling results are presented in a document based on the hidden form $BEProfile. The result document contains a heading listing the name of the agent and the creation time. The Body item of the result document contains a table with a row for each Domino® Objects method called and five columns: This method fails to find the $BEProfile form when it runs in the Notes® client if no profile results were previously viewed for any agent in the database. This is because the Notes® cache does not contain the newly-created form and cannot be refreshed. The user must close and reopen the database, and if this fails, close and reopen Notes®. Alternatively, you can first view profiling results for an agent in the database from the Designer UI.

methods.name: Lock

params.name: name
params.description: Optional. The names of the lock holders. Each lock holder must be a user or group. Defaults to one lock holder: the effective user. The empty string ("") is not permitted in the array.

params.name: provisionalOK
params.description: Optional.

returns.name: flag
returns.description: Boolean.

methods.description: IsDesignLockingEnabled in NotesDatabase must be True or this method raises an error. This method: The following actions occur depending on the current lock status:

methods.name: LockProvisional

params.name: name
params.description: Optional. The names of the lock holders. Each lock holder must be a user or group. Defaults to one lock holder: the effective user. The empty string ("") is not permitted in the array.

returns.name: flag
returns.description: Boolean.

methods.description: IsDesignLockingEnabled in NotesDatabase must be True or this method raises an error. The following actions occur depending on the current lock status: If the agent is modified by another user before the lock can be placed, this method raises an error.

methods.name: Remove
methods.description: After you call this method, the NotesAgent object is Nothing, and you can no longer use its methods or properties. It is possible to use this method to delete the agent that's currently running. Use this feature with care. You must refresh the Agents view in the user interface to see that the agent has been removed.

methods.name: Run

params.name: noteID$
params.description: Optional. The note ID of a document. The value is passed to the ParameterDocID property of the called agent.

returns.name: status
returns.description: Integer. Status of the operation where 0 indicates success.

methods.description: This method runs any agent regardless of source language (simple action, formula, LotusScript®, Java™). You cannot run an agent recursively (cannot call it from itself). The user cannot interact directly with a called agent. User output goes to the Domino® log.

methods.name: RunOnServer

params.name: noteID$
params.description: Optional. The note ID of a document. This value is passed to the ParameterDocID property of the called agent.

returns.name: status
returns.description: Integer. Status of the operation where 0 indicates success.

methods.description: This method runs any agent regardless of source language (simple action, formula, LotusScript®, Java™). You cannot run an agent recursively (cannot call it from itself). The rules governing the access level required to run an agent using the RunOnServer method are the same as for any other server-based agent. For information regarding agent security, see Setting up agent security.

methods.name: Save
methods.description: If you change the IsEnabled property or the ServerName property in an agent, you must call the Save method to commit the change to disk. You must have the appropriate access privileges to both the database and to the agent. Saving the agent changes the owner immediately. However, if you subsequently get the owner within the same NotesSession, the previous owner's name is returned. The ownership change is not reflected in the properties until the next time a Session is obtained. To save an agent from an agent that runs on a server, the executing agent must either: be signed by someone listed under "Run unrestricted methods and operations" in the saved agent's Server document in the Domino® Directory; be signed by someone listed under "Sign agents to run on behalf of someone else"; or must have the same effective user as the saved agent. The effective user may be the signer of an agent, the user listed under "Run on behalf of" in the agent properties, or the user invoking the agent if the agent is run from the Web the "Run as web user" agent property is in effect.

methods.name: UnLock
methods.description: IsDesignLockingEnabled in NotesDatabase must be True or this method raises an error. This method raises an error if the current user is not one of the lock holders and does not have lock breaking authority.

nodes.name: NotesCalendar
nodes.description: Represents a Domino® calendar.

props.name: AutoSendNotices
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether to automatically send information to participants when creating and updating meetings.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesCalendar.AutoSendNotices To set: notesCalendar.AutoSendNotices = flag

props.name: EntriesProcessed
props.description: Read-only. Number of entries processed by GetEntries and ReadRange operations.

props.name: ReadRangeMask1
props.description: Read-write. Mask that controls the property display for a readRange operation.
props.syntax: To get: mask& = notesCalendar.ReadRangeMask1 To set: notesCalendar.ReadRangeMask1 = mask&

props.name: ReadRangeMask2
props.description: Read-write. Mask that controls the optional property display for a readRange operation.
props.syntax: To get: mask& = notesCalendar.ReadRangeMask2 To set: notesCalendar.ReadRangeMask2 = mask&

props.name: ReadXLotusPropsOutputLevel
props.description: Read-write. Controls the return of X-LOTUS properties when reading a calendar entry or notice.
props.syntax: To get: setting% = notesCalendar.ReadXLotusPropsOutputLevel To set: notesCalendar.ReadXLotusPropsOutputLevel = setting%

props.name: UntilTime
props.description: Read-only. Specifies the time of the last invitation processed by getNewInvitations.

methods.name: CreateEntry

params.name: icalentry
params.description: Input for one calendar entry in iCalendar format.

params.name: flags
params.description: Write flag. CS_WRITE_DISABLE_IMPLICIT_SCHEDULING (2) disables the automatic sending of notices to participants. Setting this flag is the same as setting AutoSendNotices to false before calling this method.

returns.name: NotesCalendarEntry
returns.description: The calendar entry.

methods.description: If problems persist, try running with the following notes.ini variable: CSDebugAPI=1. When the exception occurs, check the console log for details. For a meeting, if AutoSendNoticesis not set to false and CS_WRITE_DISABLE_IMPLICIT_SCHEDULING is not set, notices are automatically sent to participants. If icalentry contains a recurrence rule (RRULE item), this method creates a calendar event for each recurrence with its own identifier (RECURRENCE-ID item). The format of a recurrence identifier is the time of the event in UTC format, for example, 20120913T160000Z. However, if you later change the time of the event, the identifier does not change.

methods.name: GetEntries

params.name: start
params.description: The start time of the entries.

params.name: end
params.description: The end time of the entries. An exception occurs if the end time is not greater than the start time.

params.name: skipcount
params.description: The number of entries to skip before starting the get operation. Defaults to 0.

params.name: maxreturn
params.description: The maximum number of entries to return. If skipcount and maxreturn are not specified, all entries in the range are returned.

returns.name: Variant
returns.description: The calendar entries in the range, or an empty for no entries. Each array element is of type NotesCalendarEntry.

methods.description: Gets calendar entries within a given time range.

methods.name: GetEntry

params.name: uid
params.description: The iCalendar identifier (UID item) of the entry.

returns.name: NotesCalendarEntry
returns.description: The calendar entry. No exception occurs for this method if the identifier is invalid. However, when you attempt to use the returned NotesCalendarEntry object, the following exception occurs: Entry not found in index.

methods.description: Gets a calendar entry.

methods.name: GetEntryByNoteID

params.name: noteid
params.description: The note identifier of the Domino® document containing the entry.

returns.name: NotesCalendarEntry
returns.description: The calendar entry. An exception occurs if the identifier is invalid.

methods.description: Gets a calendar entry given its note identifier.

methods.name: GetEntryByUNID

params.name: unid
params.description: The universal identifier (UNID) of the Domino® document containing the entry.

returns.name: NotesCalendarEntry
returns.description: The calendar entry. An exception occurs if the identifier is invalid.

methods.description: Gets a calendar entry given its universal identifier (UNID).

methods.name: GetNewInvitations

params.name: start
params.description: The start time for meetings to which any new invitations apply. Defaults to all meetings.

params.name: since
params.description: The since time for any new invitations. Defaults to all new invitations. Use this parameter in conjunction with UntilTime to get invitations posted since the last call.

returns.name: Variant
returns.description: The new invitations, or an empty array for no invitations. Each array element is of type NotesCalendarNotice.

methods.description: Gets calendar entries that are new invitations.

methods.name: GetNoticeByUNID

params.name: unid
params.description: The universal identifier (UNID) of the Domino® document containing the notice.

returns.name: NotesCalendarEntry
returns.description: The calendar notice. An exception occurs if the identifier is invalid.

methods.description: Gets a calendar notice given its universal identifier (UNID).

methods.name: GetRecurrenceID

params.name: datetime
params.description: The date and time range to search for a recurrence id instance in a calendar entry recurrence set (for example, for a recurring meeting).

returns.name: recurrenceID
returns.description: String. The recurrence id of an instance in a calendar entry recurrence set.

methods.description: Obtains a recurrence id string for specific recurrence entry in the given date/time range of a recurrence set (for example, for a recurring meeting).

methods.name: ReadRange

params.name: start
params.description: The start time of the range.

params.name: end
params.description: The end time of the range. An exception occurs if the end time is not greater than the start time.

params.name: skipcount
params.description: The number of entries to skip from the beginning of the range. This parameter can be used in conjunction with EntriesProcessed to read the entries in a series of calls.

params.name: maxread
params.description: The maximum number of entries to read.

returns.name: String
returns.description: A summary in iCalendar format of the entries from the start date to the end date, inclusive. An exception occurs if the range contains no entries.

methods.description: Each entry contains a UID item whose value can be used to get the corresponding NotesCalendarEntry object. Inclusion in a date range is determined by the start time of a meeting. The content of the return value is modified by any values set for ReadRangeMask1 and ReadRangeMask2.

nodes.name: NotesCalendarEntry

props.name: NoteID
props.description: Read-only. Notes ID of the calendar entry or notice.

props.name: UID

params.name: scope

params.name: recurid
params.type: String. The recurrence identifier (RECURRENCE-ID item) for a recurring calendar event. The format of a recurrence identifier is a time in UTC format, for example, 20120913T160000Z

params.name: Possible exception (lsxbeerr.lss)

params.name: lsERR_NOTES_ERR_RECURID_NOTFOUND

params.name: lsERR_NOTES_ERR_IDNOTFOUND

methods.name: Update

params.name: icalentry
params.type: String. The new value of the entry in iCalendar format

params.name: comments
params.type: String. Comments regarding a meeting change

params.name: flags
params.type: Long. Write flags. Combine values by adding them. CS_WRITE_DISABLE_IMPLICIT_SCHEDULING (2) disables the automatic sending of notices to participants. Setting this flag is the same as setting AutoSendNotices to false before calling this method. CS_WRITE_MODIFY_LITERAL (1 completely overwrites the original entry and using only the icalentry input. By default, the update preserves body attachments if they are not supplied with the input and preserves custom fields not present on the icalentry input

params.description: Newer version exists

params.name: lsERR_NOTES_ERR_UNSUPPORTEDACTION
params.description: Unsupported action

params.name: lsERR_NOTES_ERR_IDNOTFOUND
params.description: Identifier not found

methods.description: For a recurring entry, you must specify recurid. The iCalendar input must contain a single VEVENT and a UID.

nodes.name: NotesColorObject
nodes.description: Represents a color.

props.name: Blue
props.description: Read-only. The blue component of a color's RGB value.

props.name: Green
props.description: Read-only. The green component of the color's RGB value.

props.name: Hue
props.description: Read-only. The hue component of the color's HSL value.

props.name: Luminance
props.description: Read-only. The luminance component of the color's HSL value.

props.name: NotesColor
props.description: Read-write. The color's Domino® value.
props.syntax: To get: notesColor% = notesColorObject.NotesColor To set: notesColorObject.NotesColor = notesColor%

props.name: Red
props.description: Read-only. The red component of the color's RGB value.

props.name: Saturation
props.description: Read-only. The saturation component of the color's HSL value.

methods.name: SetHSL

params.name: hue%
params.description: Hue component of the HSL value. Must be in the range 0-240.

params.name: saturation%
params.description: Saturation component of the HSL value. Must be in the range 0-240.

params.name: luminance%
params.description: Luminance component of the HSL value. Must be in the range 0-240.

returns.name: Integer
returns.description: Integer. Closest Domino® color to the HSL value.

methods.description: SetHSL sets NotesColor, in addition to the return value, to the closest Domino® color. SetHSL sets the HSL properties to the specifications of this method. SetHSL sets the RGB properties to values that match the Domino® color.

methods.name: SetRGB

params.name: red%
params.description: Red component of the RGB value. Must be in the range 0-255.

params.name: green%
params.description: Green component of the RGB value. Must be in the range 0-255.

params.name: blue%
params.description: Blue component of the RGB value. Must be in the range 0-255.

returns.name: Integer
returns.description: Integer. Closest Domino® color to the RGB value.

methods.description: SetRGB sets NotesColor, in addition to the return value, to the closest Domino® color. SetRGB sets the RGB properties to the specifications of this method. SetRGB sets the HSL properties to values that match the Domino® color.

nodes.name: NotesDatabase
nodes.description: Represents a Notes® database.

props.name: ACL
props.description: Read-only. The access control list for a database.

props.name: ACLActivityLog
props.description: Read-only. The log from the access control list for a database.

props.name: Agents
props.description: Read-only. All of the agents in a database.

props.name: AllDocuments
props.description: Read-only. All the documents in a database.

props.name: Categories
props.description: Read-write. The categories under which a database appears in a database catalog.
props.syntax: To get: categoryList = notesDatabase .Categories To set: notesDatabase .Categories = categoryList

props.name: Created
props.description: Read-only. The date/time that a database was created.

props.name: CurrentAccessLevel
props.description: Read-only. The current user's access level to a database.

props.name: DelayUpdates
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether updates to a server are delayed (batched) for better performance.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesDatabase .DelayUpdates To set: notesDatabase .DelayUpdates = flag

props.name: DesignTemplateName
props.description: Read-only. The name of the design template from which a database inherits its design. If the database does not inherit its design from a design template, it returns an empty string ("").

props.name: EncryptionStrength
props.description: The encryption strength of a database.

props.name: FileFormat
props.description: Read-only. The ODS (on-disk structure) version of a database.

props.name: FileName
props.description: Read-only. The full file name of a database, including the extension but excluding the path.

props.name: FilePath
props.description: Read-only. The path and file name of a database. The file name includes the NSF extension.

props.name: FolderReferencesEnabled
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether this database maintains folder references for documents.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesDatabase .FolderReferencesEnabled To set: notesDatabase .FolderReferencesEnabled = flag

props.name: Forms
props.description: Read-only. All the forms in a database.

props.name: FTIndexFrequency
props.description: Read-write. Update frequency of a database's full-text index.
props.syntax: To get: frequency% = notesDatabase .FTIndexFrequency To set: notesDatabase .FTIndexFrequency = frequency%

props.name: HttpURL
props.description: Read-only. The Domino® URL of a database when HTTP protocols are in effect.

props.name: IsClusterReplication
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether cluster replication is in effect for a database on a server in a cluster.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesDatabase .IsClusterReplication To set: notesDatabase .IsClusterReplication = flag

props.name: IsConfigurationDirectory
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether a database is a Configuration Directory database.

props.name: IsCurrentAccessPublicReader
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether the current user has public reader access in a database.

props.name: IsCurrentAccessPublicWriter
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether the current user has public writer access in a database.

props.name: IsDesignLockingEnabled
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether design locking is enabled for a database.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesDatabase .IsDesignLockingEnabled To set: notesDatabase .IsDesignLockingEnabled = flag

props.name: IsDirectoryCatalog
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether a database is a Directory Catalog database, also known as the Light Weight NAB, or the Enterprise Address Book.

props.name: IsDocumentLockingEnabled
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether document locking is enabled for a database.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesDatabase .IsDocumentLockingEnabled To set: notesDatabase .IsDocumentLockingEnabled = flag

props.name: IsFTIndexed
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether or not a database has a full-text index.

props.name: IsInMultiDbIndexing
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether a database can be included in multi-database indexing.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesDatabase .IsInMultiDbIndexing To set: notesDatabase .IsInMultiDbIndexing = flag

props.name: IsInService
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether a database on a server in a cluster is accessible.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesDatabase .IsInService To set: notesDatabase .IsInService = flag

props.name: IsLink

methods.name: Compact

returns.name: Long
returns.description: Long. The difference in bytes between the size of the database before and after compacting.

methods.description: For more capabilities, see CompactWithOptions. This method must be run where the database is located, or it will produce an error. If the database is on the server, or accessible to that server via a mapped drive, the method must be run from the server. If the database resides on the Notes® client, the method must be run in the Notes® client. The method can not be run on one server to compact databases on another server. A script cannot compact the current database (the database in which the script is running) or the desktop.dsk file.

methods.name: CompactWithOptions

params.name: options$
params.description: One or more command-line options supported by the Compact server task without the minus signs. Spaces are insignificant except that a space cannot be placed in the S option between the number and the final K, k, M, or m. Options are processed in their order of specification.

params.name: options&
params.description: One or more of the following constants. Combine constants by adding.

params.name: spaceThreshold$
params.description: Optional. The value of the S option (compact if specified percent or amount of unused space) without the S, for example, "10" for 10 percent, "10K" for 10 kilobytes, or "10M" for 10 megabytes.

returns.name: Long
returns.description: Long. The difference in bytes between the size of the database before and after compacting.

methods.description: This method must be run where the database is located, or it will produce an error. If the database is on the server, or accessible to that server via a mapped drive, the method must be run from the server. If the database resides on the Notes® client, the method must be run in the Notes® client. The method can not be run on one server to compact databases on another server. A script cannot compact the current database (the database in which the script is running) or the desktop.dsk file. The options are those that you can use with the Compact server task. For more information, see "Compact options" in Administration Help.
methods.syntax: sizeDelta& = notesDatabase .CompactWithOptions( options$ ) sizeDelta& = notesDatabase .CompactWithOptions( options& [, spaceThreshold$ ] )

methods.name: Create

params.name: server$
params.description: The name of the server where you want the new database to reside. Use an empty string ("") to create a database on the current computer.

params.name: dbfile$
params.description: The file name of the new database.

params.name: openFlag
params.description: Indicates whether you want to open the database. Specify True if you want to open the database and False if you do not. A database must be open in order for a script to access its properties and methods. If you choose not to open the database when it's created, you can open it later with the Open or OpenIfModified method.

params.name: maxsize%
params.description: Optional. The maximum size (in gigabytes) that you would like to assign to the new database. This parameter applies only to Release 4 databases or those created on a server that has not been upgraded to Release 5. Entering an integer greater than 4 generates a run-time error that reads "Maximum size for database is 4 (gigabytes)," and the script fails.

methods.description: Both server$ and dbfile$ can be empty strings if the NotesDatabase object was already instantiated with a server and a file name (for example, using New). Because the new database is not based on a template, it's blank and does not contain any forms or views.
methods.syntax: Call notesDatabase .Create( server$ , dbfile$ , openFlag [, maxsize% ] )

methods.name: CreateCopy

params.name: newServer$
params.description: The name of the server where the new database resides. Use an empty string ("") to create a copy on the current computer.

params.name: newDbFile$
params.description: The file name of the new copy.

params.name: maxsize%
params.description: Optional. The maximum size (in gigabytes) that you would like to assign to the new database. This parameter applies only to Release 4 databases or those created on a server that has not been upgraded to Release 5. Entering an integer greater than 4, generates a run-time error that reads "Maximum size for database is 4 (gigabytes)," and the script fails.

returns.name: notesDatabaseNew
returns.description: NotesDatabase. The new copy.

methods.description: If a database with the specified file name already exists on the newServer$, error number 4005 occurs ("File already exists"). The copy contains the design elements of the current database, an identical access control list, and an identical title. It does not contain any documents. The copy is not a replica.
methods.syntax: Set notesDatabaseNew = notesDatabase .CreateCopy( newServer$ , newDbFile$ [, maxsize% ] )

methods.name: CreateFromTemplate

params.name: newServer$
params.description: The name of the server where the new database resides. Use an empty string ("") to create a database on the current computer.

params.name: newDbFile$
params.description: The file name of the new database.

params.name: inheritFlag
params.description: Specify True if you want the new database to inherit future design changes from the template; otherwise, specify False.

params.name: maxsize%
params.description: Optional. The maximum size (in gigabytes) that you would like to assign to the new database. This parameter applies only to Release 4 databases or those created on a server that has not been upgraded to Release 5. Entering an integer greater than 4 generates a run-time error that reads "Maximum size for database is 4 (gigabytes)," and the script fails.

returns.name: notesDatabaseNew
returns.description: NotesDatabase. The new database, which contains the forms, subforms, fields, views, folders, navigators, agents, and documents of the template.

methods.description: If a database with the specified file name already exists on the newServer$, an error is raised. The new database has the design elements and documents of the existing database. If "Database file is a master template" is set in the current database, the ACL of the new database takes the following entries:
methods.syntax: Set notesDatabaseNew = notesDatabase .CreateFromTemplate( newServer$ , newDbFile$ , inheritFlag [, maxsize% ] )

methods.name: FTDomainSearch

params.name: query$
params.description: The full-text query.

params.name: maxDocs%
params.description: The maximum number of documents you want returned from the query. Set this parameter to 0 to receive all matching documents.

params.name: sortoptions%
params.description: Optional. Sorting options. Combine options by adding.

params.name: otheroptions%
params.description: Optional. Search options. Combine options by adding.

params.name: start&
params.description: Optional. The starting page to return.

params.name: count%
params.description: Optional. The number of pages to return.

params.name: entryform$
params.description: The name of the search form in the domain catalog.

returns.name: notesDocument
returns.description: A NotesDocument with a rich text field named "Body" that contains a table of matching document titles.

methods.description: The databases you want included in the search must have the property "Include in multi-database indexing" set to TRUE. The current NotesDatabase object must represent a Domain Catalog. If you don't specify any sort options, you get the documents sorted by relevance score. If you ask for a sort by date, you don't get relevance scores.
methods.syntax: To search for a word or phrase, enter the word or phrase as is, except for search keywords must be enclosed in quotes. Remember to escape quotes if you are inside a literal. Wildcards, operators, and other syntax are permitted. For the complete syntax rules, see "Refining a search query using operators" in Notes® Help. Search for "query syntax" in the Domino® Designer Eclipse help system or information center (for example, FTSearch (NotesDatabase - JavaScript)), both of which include Notes®.

methods.name: FTSearch

params.name: query$
params.description: The full-text query. See the following for the syntax.

params.name: maxdocs%
params.description: The maximum number of documents you want returned from the query. Set this parameter to 0 to retrieve all documents (up to 5,000 - see the Usage section for more details) that meet the search criteria.

params.name: sortoptions%
params.description: Optional. Sorting options. Choose one.

params.name: otheroptions%
params.description: Optional. Search options. Combine options by adding.

returns.name: notesDocumentCollection
returns.description: NotesDocumentCollection. A collection of documents that match the full-text query, sorted by the selected option.

methods.description: This method is the same as FTSearchRange less the start parameter. If the database is not full-text indexed, this method works, but less efficiently. To test for an index, use the IsFTIndexed property in NotesDatabase. If the full-text is not updated after changes are made, it does not fully reflect the content of the database. To create or update an index on a local database, use the UpdateFTIndex method. This method returns a maximum of 5,000 documents by default. The Notes®.ini variable FT_MAX_SEARCH_RESULTS overrides this limit for indexed databases or databases that are not indexed but that are running in an agent on the client. For a database that is not indexed and is running in an agent on the server, you must set the TEMP_INDEX_MAX_DOC Notes®.ini variable as well. The absolute maximum is 2,147,483,647.
methods.syntax: To search for a word or phrase, enter the word or phrase as is, except that search keywords must be enclosed in quotes. Remember to escape quotes if you are inside a literal. Wildcards, operators, and other syntax are permitted. For the complete syntax rules, see "Refining a search query using operators" in Notes® Help. Search for "query syntax" in the Domino® Designer Eclipse help system or information center (for example, FTSearch (NotesDatabase - JavaScript)), both of which include Notes®.

methods.name: FTSearchRange

params.name: query$
params.description: The full-text query. See the following for the syntax.

params.name: maxdocs%
params.description: The maximum number of documents you want returned from the query. Set this parameter to 0 to retrieve all documents (up to 5,000; see the Usage section) that match the search criteria.

params.name: sortoptions%
params.description: Optional. Sorting options. Choose one.

params.name: otheroptions%
params.description: Optional. Search options. Combine options by adding.

params.name: start&
params.description: Optional. The starting document to return.

returns.name: notesDocumentCollection
returns.description: NotesDocumentCollection. A collection of documents that match the full-text query, sorted by the selected option.

methods.description: This method is the same as FTSearch plus the start parameter. If the database is not full-text indexed, this method works, but less efficiently. To test for an index, use the IsFTIndexed property in NotesDatabase. If the full-text is not updated after changes are made, it does not fully reflect the content of the database. To create or update an index on a local database, use the UpdateFTIndex method. This method returns a maximum of 5,000 documents by default. The Notes®.ini variable FT_MAX_SEARCH_RESULTS overrides this limit for indexed databases or databases that are not indexed but that are running in an agent on the client. For a database that is not indexed and is running in an agent on the server, you must set the TEMP_INDEX_MAX_DOC Notes®.ini variable as well. The absolute maximum is 2,147,483,647.
methods.syntax: To search for a word or phrase, enter the word or phrase as is, except that search keywords must be enclosed in quotes. Remember to escape quotes if you are inside a literal. Wildcards, operators, and other syntax are permitted. For the complete syntax rules, see "Refining a search query using operators" in Notes® Help. Search for "query syntax" in the Domino® Designer Eclipse help system or information center (for example, FTSearch (NotesDatabase - JavaScript)), both of which include Notes®.

methods.name: GetDocumentByUNID

params.description: The universal ID of a document.

returns.name: notesDocument
returns.description: NotesDocument. The document with the specified universal ID.

methods.description: For an explanation of universal IDs, see the UniversalID property in NotesDocument. You can obtain the universal ID from the UniversalID property or ParentDocumentUNID property in NotesDocument. Not matching the UNID to a document in the database raises lsERR_NOTES_BAD_UNID (4091).

methods.name: GetDocumentByURL

params.name: URL$
params.description: The desired uniform resource locator (URL), for example, http://www.acme.com. You can enter a maximum string length of 15K.

params.name: reload
params.description: Optional. Enter 1 (True) to reload the page from its Internet server. Enter 0 (False) to load the page from the Internet only if it is not already in the Web Navigator database. Enter 2 to reload the page only if it has been modified on its Internet server. The default value is 0.

params.name: urllist
params.description: Optional. Web pages can contain URL links to other Web pages. You can specify whether to save the URLs in a field called URLLinksn in the Notes® document. (The Web Navigator creates a new URLLinksn field each time the field size reaches 64K. For example, the first URLLinks field would be URLLinks1, the second would be URLLinks2, and so on.)

params.name: charset$
params.description: Optional. Enter the MIME character set (for example, ISO-2022-JP for Japanese or ISO-8859-1 for United States) that you want the Web Navigator to use when processing the Web page. Only use this parameter when the Web Navigator misdetects the correct MIME character set of the URL contents.

params.name: webusername$
params.description: Optional. Some Internet servers require you to obtain a username before you can access their pages. This parameter allows you to enter the username that you previously obtained from the Internet server.

params.name: webpassword$
params.description: Optional. Some Internet servers require you to obtain a password before you can access their pages. This parameter allows you to enter the password that you previously obtained from the Internet server.

params.name: proxywebusername$
params.description: Optional. Some proxy servers require that you specify a user name in order to connect through them. This parameter allows you to enter the user name for the proxy server. See your administrator for the user name required by the proxy.

params.name: proxywebpassword$
params.description: Optional. Some proxy servers require that you specify a password in order to connect through them. This parameter allows you to enter the password for the proxy server. See your administrator for the password required by the proxy.

params.name: returnimmediately
params.description: Optional. Specify True to return immediately and not wait for completion of the retrieval. Specify False or omit this parameter to wait for completion of the retrieval. If you specify True, GetDocumentByURL does not return the NotesDocument object representing the URL document. This parameter is useful for retrieving a URL document for offline storage purposes; in this case, you do not need the NotesDocument object and do not need to wait for completion of the operation. This parameter is ignored and False is forced if the database being opened is not local to the execution context.

returns.name: notesDocument
returns.description: The NotesDocument that represents the URL document you specified. The return value is Null if returnimmediately is True.

methods.description: Instantiates a document in the database on which it is called and returns a NotesDocument object for it. This method is typically used for either the Server Web Navigator or Personal Web Navigator database, but can be called on any database object.
methods.syntax: Set notesDocument = notesDatabase .GetDocumentByURL( URL$ [, reload ] [, urllist ] [, charset$ ] [, webusername$ ] [, webpassword$ ] [, proxywebusername$ ] [, proxywebpassword$ ] [, returnimmediately ] )

methods.name: GetModifiedDocuments

params.name: since
params.description: The start time for collecting the modified documents. Defaults to the creation time of the database effectively returning all documents.

params.name: noteClass%
params.description: Constant. One of the following to specify the type or types of document collected. You can combine types with a logical or. Defaults to DBMOD_DOC_DATA which collects only data documents.

returns.name: noteDocumentCollection
returns.description: NotesDocumentCollection. A collection containing the modified documents.

methods.description: The end time for the collection is the current database time, which is posted to the UntilTime property of the returned NotesDocumentCollection object. This time should be specified as the "since" time in a subsequent call to GetModifiedDocuments where you want to get all modified documents since the most recent call. Do not rely on the system time, which may differ from the database time. This method raises lsERR_NOTES_NOSUCH_DBGETMODDOCS (4698) "Not a valid GetModifiedDocuments constant" if the noteClass parameter is invalid.

params.name: Modifieduntil
params.description: The time until the document is modified.

returns.name: NotesNoteCollection

methods.description: Lets you retrieve all modified documents meeting matches for specified options.
methods.syntax: Set NotesNoteCollection = NotesDatabase .GetModifiedDocumentsWithOptions( Modifiedsince , Modifieduntil , Options)

params.name: name
params.description: An optional parameter that returns a collection of named documents of a specified name; there can be many such documents if they were created with UserName qualifiers.

methods.name: GetNamedDocument

params.name: name username
params.description: Specifies the name of a named document. Can be followed by an optional user name to associate a user with the named document.

methods.description: Returns a named document, a type of document that has a name rather than a NoteID and does not appear in the Notes client or in any view. A named document is designed for programmatic access and is a functional replacement for profile documents used previously. If a named document of the specified name is not found, it is created. The Document property IsNewNote returns True if the document is created or False if it existed previously. An optional username qualifier can be added to be used as a data store for that user.
methods.syntax: .GetNamedDocument( String Name, Optional String username )As NotesDocument
