methods.name: GetURLHeaderInfo

params.name: URL$
params.description: The URL for the Web page for which you want information , for example, http://www.acme.com/. You can enter a maximum string length of 15K.

params.name: headername$
params.description: Enter a header string of the URL header value that you want returned. The acceptable header strings are documented in the HTTP specification (available at various locations on the Internet, such as http://www.w3.org/) and are subject to change based on updated versions of the specification.

params.name: Webusername$
params.description: Optional. Some Internet servers require you to obtain a user name before you can access their pages. This parameter allows you to enter the user name that you previously obtained from the Internet server.

params.name: Webpassword$
params.description: Optional. Some Internet servers require you to obtain a password before you can access their pages. This parameter allows you to enter the password that you previously obtained from the Internet server.

params.name: Proxywebusername$
params.description: Optional. Some proxy servers require that you specify a user name in order to connect through them. This parameter allows you to enter the user name for the proxy server. See your administrator for the user name required by the proxy.

params.name: Proxywebpassword$
params.description: Optional. Some proxy servers require that you specify a password in order to connect through them. This parameter allows you to enter the password for the proxy server. See your administrator for the password required by the proxy.

returns.name: String
returns.description: String. The requested header. If a null value is returned, the header value that you requested was not found in the header of the URL, or the URL could not be found.

methods.description: All HTTP (and META) Header names are converted so that any dashes they contain became underscores (so they can be used in Views, etc.). Previously, this required all LotusScript® users to convert all dashes to underscores manually. Now, with Release 5, all dashes are automatically converted to underscores.
methods.syntax: header$ = notesDatabase .GetURLHeaderInfo( URL$ , headername$ [, Webusername$ ] [, Webpassword$ ] [, Proxywebusername$ ] [, Proxywebpassword$ ] )

methods.name: GetView

params.name: viewName$
params.description: The name of a view or folder in a database. Case insensitive. Use either the entire name of the view or folder (including backslashes for cascading views and folders), or an alias.

returns.name: notesView
returns.description: NotesView. The view or folder for which the name or alias matches viewName$. Returns Nothing if no match is found.

methods.description: Using getView returns public views and folders and private views and folders that are owned by the effective id running the agent. Private views stored in the desktop are not returned. When specifying the viewName$ parameter, do not use the view name and its alias together: for example, specifying "By Author|AuthorView" does not work. Use either the view name ("By Author") or its alias ("AuthorView"). When the view or folder name contains underscores to indicate menu accelerators, you have the option of either including or excluding the underscores. The method works more efficiently, however, if you include the underscores.

methods.name: GrantAccess

params.name: name$
params.description: The name of the person, group, or server for which you want to provide or change the access level.

params.name: level%
params.description: The level of access you're granting. This parameter is one of the following constants:

params.name: Level%
params.description: Meaning

params.name: ACLLEVEL_NOACCESS (0)
params.description: No access

params.name: ACLLEVEL_DEPOSITOR (1)
params.description: Depositor access

params.name: ACLLEVEL_READER (2)
params.description: Reader access

params.name: ACLLEVEL_AUTHOR (3)
params.description: Author access

params.name: ACLLEVEL_EDITOR (4)
params.description: Editor access

params.name: ACLLEVEL_DESIGNER (5)
params.description: Designer access

params.name: ACLLEVEL_MANAGER (6)
params.description: Manager access

methods.description: This method changes any existing NotesACL or NotesACLEntry object associated with the database. If the name$ already exists in the ACL, this method updates it with the access level%. Otherwise, the name is added to the ACL with level%. You can also use this method to deny access to a person, group, or server by assigning ACLLEVEL_NOACCESS.

methods.name: MarkForDelete
methods.description: Once a database is marked for deletion, it does not accept any new database open requests. After all active users are finished with it, the Cluster Manager pushes all changes to another replica (if there is another replica) and then deletes the database. Use this method if you want to remove a database that is obsolete or if you are copying a database from one server to another and want to delete the database from the original server. If you want to delete a database and all its replicas from a cluster, each database on each server must be marked for deletion. This method cannot be undone. You cannot remove a mark for deletion from a database once this method is used.

methods.name: Open

params.name: server$
params.description: The name of the server on which the database resides. Use an empty string ("") to indicate a database on the current computer, or for a NotesDatabase that's already assigned to a database.

params.name: dbfile$
params.description: The file name of the database to open. Use an empty string for a NotesDatabase that's already assigned to a database.

methods.description: Use the Open method as follows: The script returns an error if you try to reassign its server or file name using Open.
methods.syntax: flag = notesDatabase .Open( server$ , dbfile$ ) Call notesDatabase .Open()

methods.name: OpenByReplicaID

params.name: server$
params.description: The name of the server on which the database resides. Use an empty string ("") to indicate a database on the current computer.

params.name: replicaID$
params.description: The replica ID of the database that you want to open.

methods.description: Given a server name and a replica ID, opens the specified database, if it exists.

methods.name: OpenIfModified

params.name: server$
params.description: The name of the server on which the database resides. Use an empty string ("") to indicate a database on the current computer.

params.name: dbfile$
params.description: The file name of the database.

params.name: notesDateTime
params.description: If one or more documents in the database has been modified since this date, the database is opened; if not, it is not opened.

methods.description: Given a date, opens the specified database if it has been modified since that date.

methods.name: OpenMail
methods.description: If the script runs on a workstation, OpenMail finds the current user's mail server and database in the notes.ini (or Notes® Preferences) file. If the script runs on a server, the current user is considered to be the last person who modified the agent (the agent's owner), and OpenMail finds the user's mail server and database in the Domino® Directory on the server.

methods.name: OpenURLDb
methods.description: This method returns a pointer to the location of the Web Navigator database specified by the Notes® client.

methods.name: OpenWithFailover

params.name: server$
params.description: The name of the primary server on which the database resides.

params.name: dbfile$
params.description: The file name of the database to open.

methods.description: The object Server and FilePath properties reflect the actual server on which the database is opened. A script needs at least Reader access to a database in order to call Open. An error is returned if the script does not have access. If the database can't be opened on the specific server but the server belongs to a cluster, OpenWithFailover automatically looks for a replica of the specified server on the same cluster. If the method finds a replica, that database is opened instead, and the server property adjusts accordingly.

methods.name: QueryAccess

params.name: name$
params.description: The name of the person, group, or server.

returns.name: Integer
returns.description: Integer constant. Indicates the current access level. One of the following:

methods.description: If the name you specify is listed explicitly in the ACL, QueryAccess returns the access level for that ACL entry and does not check groups. If the name you specify is not listed explicitly in the ACL, QueryAccess checks to see if the name is a member of a group in the primary address book where the program is running: on a workstation the Personal Address Book; on a server the Domino® Directory. QueryAccess returns the highest access level among those groups. If the name you specify is not listed in the ACL either individually or as part of a group, QueryAccess returns the default access level for the ACL.

methods.name: QueryAccessPrivileges

params.name: name$
params.description: The name of the person, group, or server. For a hierarchical name, the full name must be specified but can be in abbreviated format.

returns.name: Long
returns.description: Long constant. The current access privileges, a combination of the following:

methods.description: If the name you specify is listed explicitly in the ACL, then QueryAccessPrivileges returns the privileges for that ACL entry and does not check groups. If the name you specify is not listed explicitly in the ACL, QueryAccessPrivileges checks to see if the name is a member of a group in the primary address book where the program is running: on a workstation the Personal Address Book; on a server the Domino® Directory.

methods.name: QueryAccessRoles

params.name: name$
params.description: The name of the person, group, or server.

returns.name: roles
returns.description: String array.

methods.description: If the name you specify is listed explicitly in the ACL, then QueryAccessRoles returns the roles for that ACL entry and does not check groups. If the name you specify is not listed explicitly in the ACL, QueryAccess checks to see if the name is a member of a group in the primary address book where the program is running: on a workstation the Personal Address Book; on a server the Domino® Directory.

methods.name: Remove
methods.description: This method fails if the database is open by other users. It does not set the database to be deleted once the current users are finished, nor does it prevent new users from accessing the database. See the MarkForDelete method if you want to mark a database in a cluster for deletion once all current users have finished with it.

methods.name: RemoveFTIndex
methods.description: No error occurs if the database does not have a full-text index. This method works only for local databases.

methods.name: Replicate

params.name: serverName$
params.description: The name of the server with which you want to replicate. Any replicas of the current database that exist on serverName$ will replicate.

methods.description: Successful replication does not necessarily mean that documents replicate. The replication settings are honored. For example, if replication is temporarily disabled on one of the databases, the replication task runs without error but no documents actually replicate. If the server does not contain a replica of the database, an error is raised.

methods.name: RevokeAccess

params.name: name$
params.description: The name of the person, group, or server for which you want to revoke access.

methods.description: This method changes any existing NotesACL or NotesACLEntry object associated with the database. Revoking access is different than assigning No Access (which you can do with the GrantAccess method). When you revoke access, you remove the name from the ACL, but the person, group, or server can still access the database at the level specified for the Default. When you use the GrantAccess method to assign No Access, the name remains in the ACL, and the person, group, or server cannot access the database, regardless of the Default setting.

methods.name: Search

params.name: formula$
params.description: A Notes® @function formula that defines the selection criteria.

params.name: notesDateTime
params.description: The method searches only documents created or modified since the start date. Specify Nothing to indicate no start date.

params.name: maxDocs%
params.description: The maximum number of documents you want returned. Specify 0 to receive all matching documents.

returns.name: notesDocumentCollection
returns.description: NotesDocumentCollection. An unsorted collection of documents that match the selection criteria.

methods.description: Given selection criteria for a document, returns all documents in a database that meet the criteria.
methods.syntax: Set notesDocumentCollection = notesDatabase .Search( formula$ , notesDateTime , maxDocs% )

methods.name: SetOption

params.name: optionName%
params.description: Constant. One of the following:

params.name: flag
params.description: Boolean.

methods.description: Compact the database to ensure that the option takes effect. See Compact. See GetOption for getting a database option. Setting DBOPT_REPLICATEUNREADMARKSTOANY True also sets DBOPT_REPLICATEUNREADMARKSTOCLUSTER True. Setting DBOPT_REPLICATEUNREADMARKSTOCLUSTER True sets DBOPT_REPLICATEUNREADMARKSTOANY False. Setting both options False means unread marks are never replicated.

methods.name: SetUserIDForDecrypt

params.name: uid
params.description: After setting the User id, documents in this database will be decrypted with encryption keys of this user id

params.name: idfile
params.description: Provides the file path of id file. After setting it, all documents in this database will be decrypted with encryption keys of this id file.

params.name: password
params.description: After setting the User id, documents in this database will be decrypted with encryption keys of this user id

methods.description: After calling this method on a database, any document that is opened within the database is decrypted using the encryption keys within the userID object, as specified in the document SecretEncryptionKeys field.

methods.name: Sign

params.name: documentType%
params.description: Optional. One of the following constants.

params.name: existingSigsOnly
params.description: Optional.

params.name: nameStr
params.description: Optional. Programmatic name or note ID of a single design element.If this parameter is not specified, all design elements of type parameter 1 are signed.

params.name: nameStrIsNoteID
params.description: Optional.

methods.description: This method signs all design elements of the specified type if you specify parameter 1 and do not specify parameter 3. If you specify parameter 3, this method signs one design element. You can also specify parameter 1 to positively define the design element. If more than one design element has the same name and parameter 1 is not specified, this method signs the first design element with the specified name. To sign all elements of a data document, including active content (hotspots), the method must be performed twice, once specifying parameter 1 of DBSIGN_DOC_ALL and once specifying parameter 1 of DBSIGN_DOC_DATA.
methods.syntax: Call notesDatabase .Sign( [ documentType% ] [ , existingSigsOnly ] [ , nameStr$] [ , nameStrIsNoteID ] )

methods.name: TransactionBegin
methods.description: Begins recording changes to a database on a Domino server.

methods.name: TransactionCommit
methods.description: TransactionCommit can be called only after TransactionBegin. If the database being changed is allowed to go out of scope before TransactionCommit is called, all recorded changes are discarded and do not take effect.

methods.name: TransactionRollback
methods.description: TransactionRollback can be called only after TransactionBegin.

methods.name: UnprocessedFTSearch

params.name: query$
params.description: The full-text query.

params.name: maxdocs%
params.description: The maximum number of documents you want returned from the query. Set this parameter to 0 to retrieve all documents (up to 5,000. See Full-text indexes section as follows.) that match the search criteria.

params.name: sortoptions%
params.description: Optional. Sorting options. Choose one.

params.name: otheroptions%
params.description: Optional. Search options. Combine options by adding.

returns.name: The following table describes the documents that are returned by the UnprocessedFTSearch method. Document selection occurs once, before the agent runs.
returns.description: Agent runs on

methods.description: This method is the same as UnprocessedFTSearchRange less the start parameter. This method is valid only for agent scripts and view actions and may be invoked only on NotesDatabase objects obtained from the CurrentDatabase property in NotesSession. In scripts that are not a part of an agent or view action, this method returns a NotesDocumentCollection with zero documents. When invoked on a NotesDatabase object that was not obtained from the CurrentDatabase property, this method raises an error.
methods.syntax: To search for a word or phrase, enter the word or phrase as is, except that search keywords must be enclosed in quotes. Remember to escape quotes if you are inside a literal. Wildcards, operators, and other syntax are permitted. For the complete syntax rules, see "Finding documents in a database" in Notes® Help.

methods.name: UnprocessedFTSearchRange

params.name: query$
params.description: The full-text query.

params.name: maxdocs%
params.description: The maximum number of documents you want returned from the query. Set this parameter to 0 to retrieve all documents (up to 5,000. See Full-text indexes section as follows.) that match the search criteria.

params.name: sortoptions%
params.description: Optional. Sorting options. Combine options by adding.

params.name: otheroptions%
params.description: Optional. Search options. Combine options by adding.

params.name: start&
params.description: Optional. The starting document to return.

returns.name: The following table describes the documents that are returned by the UnprocessedFTSearch method. Document selection occurs once, before the agent runs.
returns.description: Agent runs on

methods.description: This method is the same as UnprocessedFTSearch plus the start parameter. This method is valid only for agent scripts and view actions and may be invoked only on NotesDatabase objects obtained from the CurrentDatabase property in NotesSession. In scripts that are not a part of an agent or view action, this method returns a NotesDocumentCollection with zero documents. When invoked on a NotesDatabase object that was not obtained from the CurrentDatabase property, this method raises an error.
methods.syntax: To search for a word or phrase, enter the word or phrase as is, except that search keywords must be enclosed in quotes. Remember to escape quotes if you are inside a literal. Wildcards, operators, and other syntax are permitted. For the complete syntax rules, see "Finding documents in a database" in Notes® Help.

methods.name: UnprocessedSearch

params.name: formula$
params.description: A Notes® @function formula that defines the selection criteria.

params.name: notesDateTime
params.description: The method searches only documents created or modified since the cutoff date.

params.name: maxDocs%
params.description: The maximum number of documents you want returned. Set this parameter to 0 to receive all matching documents.

returns.name: The following table describes the documents that are returned by the UnprocessedSearch method. Document selection occurs once, before the agent runs.
returns.description: Agent runs on

methods.description: This method is valid only for agent or view action scripts, and may be invoked only on NotesDatabase objects obtained from the CurrentDatabase property in NotesSession. In scripts that are not a part of an agent or view action, this method returns a NotesDocumentCollection with zero documents. When invoked on a NotesDatabase object that was not obtained from the CurrentDatabase property, this method raises an error.
methods.syntax: Set notesDocumentCollection = notesDatabase .UnprocessedSearch( formula$ , notesDateTime , maxDocs% )

methods.name: UpdateFTIndex

params.name: createFlag
params.description: Specify True if you want to create an index if none exists (valid only for local databases and only if the database contains at least one document). Otherwise, specify False.

methods.description: Notes® returns an error if you attempt to create a full-text index on a database that is not local. A database must contain at least one document in order for an index to be created, even if createFlag is set to True.

methods.name: Decrypt
methods.description: Decrypts a database

methods.name: Encrypt
methods.description: Encrypts a database.

nodes.name: NotesDateRange
nodes.description: Represents a range of dates and times.

props.name: EndDateTime
props.description: Read-write. The ending date-time of a range.
props.syntax: To get: notesDateTime = notesDateRange .EndDateTime To set: Set notesDateRange .EndDateTime = notesDateTime

props.name: Parent
props.description: Read-only. The session that contains a DateRange object.

props.name: StartDateTime
props.description: Read-write. The starting date-time of a range.
props.syntax: To get: notesDateTime = notesDateRange .StartDateTime To set: Set notesDateRange .StartDateTime = notesDateTime

props.name: Text
props.description: Read-write. The text associated with a range.
props.syntax: To get: text = notesDateRange .Text To set: notesDateRange .Text = text

nodes.name: NotesDateTime
nodes.description: Represents a date and time. Provides a means of translating between the LotusScript® date-time format and the Domino® format.

props.name: DateOnly
props.description: Read-only. A string representing the date part of a date-time, in the local time zone.

props.name: GMTTime
props.description: Read-only. A string representing a date-time, converted to Greenwich Mean Time (timezone 0).

props.name: IsDST
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether the time reflects daylight-saving time.

props.name: IsValidDate
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether the string that is used to create the NotesDateTime object is valid.

props.name: LocalTime
props.description: Read-write. A string representing a date-time, in the local time zone.
props.syntax: To get: time$ = notesDateTime .LocalTime To set: notesDateTime .LocalTime = time$

props.name: LSGMTTime
props.description: Read-only. A LotusScript® variant representing a date-time, converted to Greenwich Mean Time (timezone 0).

props.name: LSLocalTime
props.description: Read-write. A LotusScript® variant representing a date-time, in the local time zone.
props.syntax: To get: timeV = notesDateTime .LSLocalTime To set: notesDateTime .LSLocalTime = timeV

props.name: Parent
props.description: Read-only. The session that contains a DateTime object.

props.name: TimeOnly
props.description: Read-only. A string representing the time part of a date-time, in the local time zone.

props.name: TimeZone
props.description: Read-only. An integer representing the time zone of a date-time. In many cases, but not all, this integer indicates the number of hours which must be added to the time to get Greenwich Mean Time. May be positive or negative.

props.name: ZoneTime
props.description: Read-only. A string representation of the time adjusted for the TimeZone and IsDST properties.

methods.name: New

params.name: dateTime$
params.description: The date and time you want the object to represent. If you use an empty string (""), the date is set to a wildcard date. The Notes® date-time expressions "Today," "Tomorrow," and "Yesterday" are supported.

methods.description: Given a string that represents the date and time you want, New creates an object that represents that date and time.
methods.syntax: Dim variableName As New NotesDateTime( dateTime$ ) Set notesDateTime = New NotesDateTime( dateTime$ )

methods.name: AdjustDay

params.name: n%
params.description: The number of days by which to increment the date-time. Can be positive or negative.

params.name: preserveLocalTime
params.description: Optional. Affects adjustments that cross daylight-saving boundaries. Specify True to increment or decrement the GMT time by one hour such that a 24-hour adjustment yields the same local time in the new day. If this parameter is False or omitted, GMT time remains as adjusted and local time gains or loses an hour.

methods.description: Increments a date-time by the number of days you specify.

methods.name: AdjustHour

params.name: n%
params.description: The number of hours by which to increment the date-time. Can be positive or negative.

params.name: preserveLocalTime
params.description: Optional. Affects adjustments that cross daylight-saving boundaries. Specify True to increment or decrement the GMT time by one hour such that a 24-hour adjustment yields the same local time in the new day. If this parameter is False or omitted, GMT time remains as adjusted and local time gains or loses an hour.

methods.description: If the NotesDateTime object consists of only a date component, this method has no effect as long as the adjustment is less than the number of hours in a day. The number of hours by which you increment a NotesDateTime object can cause the date component to be adjusted, too. For example, if n% is 24, the date component changes to the next calendar day.

methods.name: AdjustMinute

params.name: n%
params.description: The number of minutes by which to increment the date-time. Can be positive or negative.

params.name: preserveLocalTime
params.description: Optional. Affects adjustments that cross daylight-saving boundaries. Specify True to increment or decrement the GMT time by one hour such that a 24-hour adjustment yields the same local time in the new day. If this parameter is False or omitted, GMT time remains as adjusted and local time gains or loses an hour.

methods.description: If the NotesDateTime object consists of only a date component, this method has no effect, as long as the adjustment is less than the number of minutes in a day. The number of minutes by which you increment a NotesDateTime object can cause the date component to be adjusted, too. For example, if n% is 1440, the date component changes to the next calendar day.

methods.name: AdjustMonth

params.name: n%
params.description: The number of months by which to increment the date-time. Can be positive or negative.

params.name: preserveLocalTime
params.description: Optional. Affects adjustments that cross daylight-saving boundaries. Specify True to increment or decrement the GMT time by one hour such that a 24-hour adjustment yields the same local time in the new day. If this parameter is False or omitted, GMT time remains as adjusted and local time gains or loses an hour.

methods.description: The number of months by which you increment a NotesDateTime object can cause the year to be incremented. For example, if n% is 12, the date component changes to the next calendar year.

methods.name: AdjustSecond

params.name: n%
params.description: The number of seconds by which to increment the date-time. Can be positive or negative.

params.name: preserveLocalTime
params.description: Optional. Affects adjustments that cross daylight-saving boundaries. Specify True to increment or decrement the GMT time by one hour such that a 24-hour adjustment yields the same local time in the new day. If this parameter is False or omitted, GMT time remains as adjusted and local time gains or loses an hour.

methods.description: If the NotesDateTime object consists of only a date component, this method has no effect.

methods.name: AdjustYear

params.name: n%
params.description: The number of years by which to increment the date-time. Can be positive or negative.

params.name: preserveLocalTime
params.description: Optional. Affects adjustments that cross daylight-saving boundaries. Specify True to increment or decrement the GMT time by one hour such that a 24-hour adjustment yields the same local time in the new day. If this parameter is False or omitted, GMT time remains as adjusted and local time gains or loses an hour.

methods.description: The number of years by which you increment a NotesDateTime object can cause the century to change. If this happens, the year is represented with four digits instead of two, such as 08/18/2001.

methods.name: ConvertToZone

params.name: newzone
params.description: A time zone. Could be negative.

params.name: dst
params.description: Indicates whether daylight-saving time is in effect (True) or not (False) in the new time zone.

methods.description: These changes also affect the ZoneTime property. These changes do not affect the GMTTime and the LocalTime properties. This method has no effect on an invalid TimeZone (because the DateTime object lacks the date or the time). The invalid TimeZone continues to return 0.

methods.name: SetAnyDate
methods.description: This method is useful when passing the object as an argument to other methods. A NotesDateTime object for which you have invoked this method may not be convertible to string format.

methods.name: SetAnyTime
methods.description: This method is useful when passing the object as an argument to other methods. A date-time object for which you have invoked this method may not be convertible to string format.

methods.name: SetNow
methods.description: This method works by taking a snapshot of the current date-time at the moment that the method is executed. After the method executes, the value of the NotesDateTime object does not update itself to always reflect the current date-time. Its value only changes if you change it explicitly.

methods.name: TimeDifference

params.name: notesDateTime
params.description: Any date-time value.

returns.name: Long
returns.description: Long. The date-time object invoking the TimeDifference method minus the notesDateTime object specified by the parameter, in seconds.

methods.description: Finds the difference in seconds between one date-time and another.

methods.name: TimeDifferenceDouble

params.name: notesDateTime
params.description: Any date-time value.

returns.name: Double
returns.description: Double. The date-time object invoking the TimeDifferenceDouble method minus the notesDateTime object specified by the parameter, in seconds.

methods.description: Finds the difference in seconds between one date-time and another.

nodes.name: NotesDbDirectory
nodes.description: Represents the Notes® databases on a specific server or local computer.

props.name: Name
props.description: Read-only. The name of the server that has the database directory you are searching. This property is set when you create a database directory.

props.name: Parent
props.description: Read-only. The session that contains a DbDirectory object.

methods.name: New

params.name: serverName$
params.description: The name of the server that has the database files you want to navigate. Use an empty string ("") to indicate the current computer.

methods.description: You create a new NotesDbDirectory object using the name of the server you want to access. You can use the following:
methods.syntax: Dim variableName as New NotesDbDirectory( serverName$ ) Set notesDbDirectory = New NotesDbDirectory( serverName$ )

methods.name: CreateDatabase

params.name: dbfile$
params.description: The file name of the new database.

params.name: open
params.description: True (default) to open the database.

returns.name: notesDatabase
returns.description: The newly created database.

methods.description: If you do not open the database, only a subset of its properties is available. See IsOpen in NotesDatabase. This method raises ERR_NOTES_ERROR2 (error 0FA5) if the database already exists. Because the new database is not based on a template, it's blank and does not contain any forms or views.

methods.name: GetFirstDatabase

params.name: fileType%
params.description: Indicates the kind of database file you want to retrieve from the server.

params.name: fileType%
params.description: Indicates

params.name: TEMPLATE (1248)
params.description: Any Notes® database template (NTF file)

params.name: REPLICA_CANDIDATE (1245)
params.description: Any Notes® database or template not disabled for replication

params.name: TEMPLATE_CANDIDATE (1246)
params.description: Any Notes® database or template

returns.name: notesDatabase
returns.description: The first database, of the specified fileType%, located in the directory. The database is closed. To open it, use the Open or OpenIfModified method in NotesDatabase.

methods.description: Each time you call this method, the database directory is reset and a new search is conducted. If you're searching for template files, for example, a new call to GetFirstDatabase with the parameter DATABASE (LotusScript®) or NOTES_DATABASE (COM) will start searching the directory from the beginning, this time for database files.

methods.name: GetNextDatabase

returns.name: notesDatabase
returns.description: The next database located on the server. Once you exhaust the directory, returns Nothing. The database is closed. To open it, use the Open or OpenIfModified method in NotesDatabase.

methods.description: Returns the next database in a directory, using the file type specified in the GetFirstDatabase method.

methods.name: OpenDatabase

params.name: dbfile$
params.description: The file name of the database to open.

params.name: open
params.description: If True and the database cannot be opened on the current server, an attempt is made to open it on another server in the cluster (if there is a cluster). The object's Server and FilePath properties reflect the server that the database is opened on. Default is False.

returns.name: notesDatabase
returns.description: The opened database.

methods.description: This method usually raises ERR_SYS_FILE_NOT_FOUND (error 0FA3) if the database does not exist. However, other errors may occur or the method may return an object with blank properties.

methods.name: OpenDatabaseByReplicaID

params.name: rid$
params.description: The replica ID of the database that you want to open.

returns.name: notesDatabase
returns.description: The opened database.

methods.description: This method usually raises ERR_SYS_FILE_NOT_FOUND (error 0FA3) if the database does not exist. However, other errors may occur or the method may return an object with blank properties.

methods.name: OpenDatabaseIfModified

params.name: dbfile$
params.description: The file name of the database that you want to open.

params.name: notesDateTime
params.description: If one or more documents in the database were modified since this date, the database is opened; if not, it is not opened.

returns.name: notesDatabase
returns.description: The opened database.

methods.description: This method raises error 023C if the database has not been modified since the specified date. This method usually raises ERR_SYS_FILE_NOT_FOUND (error 0FA3) if the database does not exist. However, other errors may occur or the method may return an object with blank properties.

methods.name: OpenMailDatabase

returns.name: notesDatabase
returns.description: The opened database.

methods.description: The value of the parent NotesDbDirectory does not matter except that it must be set. It can be the local or any server directory.

nodes.name: NotesDirectory
nodes.description: Represents the Notes® directories on a specific server or local computer, each of which is associated with one or more directory navigators to allow directory lookups.

props.name: AvailableItems
props.description: Read-only. Array of summary data items which have been looked up and cached.

props.name: AvailableNames
props.description: Read-only. Array of names which have been looked up and cached.

props.name: AvailableView
props.description: Read-only. The view name which has been looked up and cached.

props.name: GroupAuthorizationOnly
props.description: Read-write. Indicates if only those directories enabled for group authorization will be searched by lookups.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesDirectory .GroupAuthorizationsOnly To set: notesDirectory .GroupAuthorizationsOnly = flag

props.name: LimitMatches
props.description: Read-write. Indicates if the results of lookups are limited to fifty entries.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesDirectory .LimitMatches To set: notesDirectory .LimitMatches = flag

props.name: PartialMatches
props.description: Read-only. Indicates if lookups match on partial names.

props.name: SearchAllDirectories
props.description: Read-write. Indicates if all directories will be searched by lookups.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesDirectory .SearchAllDirectories To set: notesDirectory .SearchAllDirectories = flag

props.name: Server
props.description: Read-only. The name of the server represented by the NotesDirectory.

props.name: TrustedOnly
props.description: Read-write. Indicates if only directories that contain trust information will be searched by lookups.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesDirectory .TrustedOnly To set: notesDirectory .TrustedOnly = flag

props.name: UseContextServer
props.description: Read-write. Indicates if the server of the context database will be used.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesDirectory .UseContextServer To set: notesDirectory .UseContextServer = flag

methods.name: CreateNavigator

returns.name: notesDirectoryNavigator
returns.description: The newly created directory navigator.

methods.description: Calls to Lookup against a Directory return a resident DirectoryNavigator, with successive Lookups resetting the cache and associated navigator. The CreateNavigator method is used to create additional DirectoryNavigators, allowing multiple simultaneous information retrievals.

methods.name: FreeLookupBuffer
methods.description: This method is used for memory management, since the namelookup buffer may be large.

methods.name: GetMailInfo

params.name: username
params.description: The name of a user for whom mail information is requested.

params.name: getver
params.description: Boolean. Flag to request build number and version information of the user's home mail server. Default value is False.

params.name: errorOnMultipleMatches
params.description: Boolean. Flag to indicate how to handle multiple matches for the username. If this argument is True, multiple matches throw an error code. If this argument is False, if multiple matches occur, the first match is used. Default value is True.

returns.name: mailinfo
returns.description: Variant containing a String array. Mail data for the specified person. Elements returned are as follows.

methods.description: For Directory lookups of mail information, the resident Server specified for the Directory class instance will be used, if present. If that fails, bootstrap information for the method will be gleaned from the user's current operating environment. If errorOnMultipleMatches is True, and multiple matches for the same name are found, the error code that is thrown is error code 4751 "Directory contains multiple entries for this user." If no server responds to the lookup requests, the error code that is thrown is error code 4749 "Unable to access server."

methods.name: LookupAllNames

params.name: view$
params.description: The name of the view in which to look up names.

params.name: items
params.description: Summary data items whose values will be returned by the lookup.

returns.name: notesDirectoryNavigator
returns.description: The newly created directory navigator, containing values of the specified lookup items for all names in the specified view.

methods.description: This method will flush the NotesDirectory cache and reset all child navigators. The items specified in this method can be: For best performance, use column names from the specified view or computed items.

methods.name: LookupNames

params.name: view$
params.description: The name of the view in which to look up names.

params.name: names
params.description: Either a single string, or an array of strings. The names to be searched in the view. Entries must match one of these names to be searched for items.

params.name: items
params.description: Either a single string, or an array of strings. Summary data items whose values will be returned by the lookup.

params.name: partialmatches
params.description: Optional. Indicates partial matches of names are allowed.

returns.name: notesDirectoryNavigator
returns.description: The newly created directory navigator, containing the values in items of entries whose name matches (or partially matches, if partialmatches is true) names.

methods.description: This method will flush the NotesDirectory cache and reset all child navigators. The items specified in this method can be: For best performance, use column names from the specified view or computed items.
methods.syntax: Set notesDirectoryNavigator = notesDirectory .LookupNames(view$, names, items[, partialmatches])

nodes.name: NotesDirectoryNavigator
nodes.description: Allows lookups of a specific associated Notes® directory on a specific server or local computer.

props.name: CurrentItem
props.description: Read-only. The name of the current item in the current match to which the navigator is pointing.

props.name: CurrentMatch
props.description: Read-only. The iteration index into the internal match list of the current match to which the navigator is pointing.

props.name: CurrentMatches
props.description: Read-only. The number of matches found for the current name.

props.name: CurrentName
props.description: Read-only. The name of the current match to which the navigator is pointing.

props.name: CurrentView
props.description: Read-only. The name of the cached view.

props.name: MatchLocated
props.description: Read-only. Indicates success of most recent match.

props.name: NameLocated
props.description: Read-only. Indicates success of finding a name.

methods.name: FindFirstMatch

returns.name: flag
returns.description: Boolean. True if a match was found in the navigator. False if no match was found.

methods.description: Positions the directory navigator at the first match, and sets the CurrentName property with the name found in that match. Lookups automatically position the navigator at the first match of the first name.

methods.name: FindFirstName

returns.name: Double
returns.description: Long. The number of matches found in the navigator.

methods.description: Positions the directory navigator at the first match of the first name, and sets the NameLocated property to true if the method succeeds. If the method fails, the NameLocated property is set to false. Does not change the current match. Lookups automatically position the navigator at the first match of the first name.

methods.name: FindNextMatch

returns.name: flag
returns.description: Boolean. True if a match was found in the navigator. False if no match was found.

methods.description: Positions the directory navigator at the next match for the current name.

methods.name: FindNextName

returns.name: Double
returns.description: Long. The number of matches found in the navigator.

methods.description: Positions the directory navigator at the next name in the namelist, and sets the NameLocated property to true if the method succeeds. If the method fails, the NameLocated property is set to false. Does not change the current match.

methods.name: FindNthMatch

params.name: n%
params.description: The number of the match at which to position the navigator, starting with 1.

returns.name: flag
returns.description: Boolean. True if a match was found in the navigator. False if no match was found.

methods.description: Positions the directory navigator at the nth match for the current name.

methods.name: FindNthName

params.name: n%
params.description: The number of the name at which to position the navigator, starting with 1.

returns.name: Double
returns.description: Long. The number of matches found in the navigator.

methods.description: Positions the directory navigator at the nth name in the namelist, and sets the NameLocated property to true if the method succeeds. If the method fails, the NameLocated property is set to false. Does not change the current match.

methods.name: GetFirstItemValue

returns.name: value
returns.description: Variant. The value of the first item of the current match.

methods.description: Lookups automatically position the navigator at the first match of the first name. This method always returns an array. If it is a single valued item, only the first value of the array will be populated.

methods.name: GetNextItemValue

returns.name: value
returns.description: Variant. The value of the next item of the current match.

methods.description: Positions navigator to next item within current match, and updates the CurrentItem property with the item name. Lookups automatically position the navigator at the first match of the first name. This method always returns an array. If it is a single valued item, only the first value of the array will be populated.

methods.name: GetNthItemValue

params.name: n%
params.description: The number of the match at which to position the navigator, starting with 1.

returns.name: value
returns.description: Variant. The value of the current item of the nth match.

methods.description: Positions navigator to the nth match of the current name, and updates the CurrentItem property with the item name. The only way to advance the current item is by use of the GetNextItemValue property. This method always returns an array. If it is a single valued item, only the first value of the array will be populated.

nodes.name: NotesDocument
nodes.description: Represents a document in a database.

props.name: Authors
props.description: Read-only. The names of the people who have saved a document.

props.name: ColumnValues
props.description: Read-only. An array of values, each element of which corresponds to a column value in the document's parent view. The first value in the array is the value that appears in the view's first column for the document, the second value is the one that appears in the second column, and so on. The value of each element of the array is the result of the corresponding column's formula and the items on the current document. Some elements in the array might have no value.

props.name: Created
props.description: Read-only. The date/time a document was created.

props.name: EmbeddedObjects
props.description: Read-only. The OLE/2 and OLE/1 embedded objects in a document.

props.name: EncryptionKeys
props.description: Read-write. The key(s) used to encrypt a document. The Encrypt method uses these keys when it encrypts the document.
props.syntax: To get: stringArray = notesDocument.EncryptionKeys To set: notesDocument.EncryptionKeys = stringArray notesDocument.EncryptionKeys = string$

props.name: EncryptOnSend
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether a document is encrypted when mailed.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesDocument.EncryptOnSend To set: notesDocument.EncryptOnSend = flag

props.name: FolderReferences
props.description: Read-only. The names of the folders containing a document.

props.name: FTSearchScore
props.description: Read-only. The full-text search score of a document, if it was retrieved as part of a full-text search.

props.name: HasEmbedded
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether a document contains one or more embedded objects, object links, or file attachments.

props.name: HttpURL
props.description: Read-only. The Domino® URL of a document when HTTP protocols are in effect.

props.name: IsDeleted
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether a Document object represents an existing document (not a deletion stub) on an ongoing basis.

props.name: IsEncrypted
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether a document is encrypted.

props.name: IsNamedDoc
props.description: Indicates whether a document is a named document.

props.name: IsNewNote
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether a document is new. A document is new if it hasn't been saved.

props.name: IsProfile
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether a NotesDocument object is a Profile document.

props.name: IsResponse
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether a document is a response to another document.

props.name: IsSigned
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether a document contains a signature.

props.name: IsUIDocOpen
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether a NotesDocument object (back-end document) is accessed through a NotesUIDocument object (front-end document).

props.name: IsValid
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether a Document object represents an existing document (not a deletion stub) initially.

props.name: Items
props.description: Read-only. All the items in a document. An item is any piece of data stored in a document.

props.name: Key
props.description: Read-only. If a Profile document, the user name (key) of the profile.

props.name: LastAccessed
props.description: Read-only. The date/time a document was last modified or read.

props.name: LastModified
props.description: Read-only. The date/time a document was last modified.

props.name: LockHolders
props.description: Read-only. The names of the holders of a lock.

props.name: NameOfDoc
props.description: Returns the name of a named document.

props.name: NameOfProfile
props.description: Read-only. If a Profile document, the name of the profile.

props.name: NoteID
props.description: Read-only. The note ID of a document, which is an 8-character combination of letters and numbers that uniquely identifies a document within a particular database.

props.name: NotesURL
props.description: Read-only. The Domino® URL of a document when Notes® protocols are in effect.

props.name: ParentDatabase
props.description: Read-only. The database that contains a document.

props.name: ParentDocumentUNID
props.description: Read-only. The universal ID of a document's parent, if the document is a response. Returns an empty string ("") if a document doesn't have a parent.

props.name: ParentView
props.description: Read-only. The view from which a document was retrieved, if any.

props.name: Responses
props.description: Read-only. The immediate responses to a document.

props.name: SaveMessageOnSend
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether a document is saved to a database when mailed. Only applies to new documents that have not yet been saved.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesDocument.SaveMessageOnSend To set: notesDocument.SaveMessageOnSend = flag

props.name: SentByAgent
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether a document was mailed by a script.

props.name: Signer
props.description: Read-only. The name of the person who created the signature, if a document is signed.

props.name: SignOnSend
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether a document is signed when mailed.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesDocument.SignOnSend To set: notesDocument.SignOnSend = flag

props.name: Size
props.description: Read-only. The size of a document in bytes, which includes the size of any file attachments on the document.

props.name: UniversalID
props.description: Read-write. The universal ID, which uniquely identifies a document across all replicas of a database. In character format, the universal ID is a 32-character combination of hexadecimal digits (0-9, A-F).
props.syntax: To get: unid$ = notesDocument.UniversalID To set: notesDocument.UniversalID = unid$

props.name: UserNameOfDoc
props.description: Returns a user name associated with a named document.

props.name: Verifier
props.description: Read-only. The name of the certificate that verified a signature, if a document is signed.

methods.name: AppendItemValue

params.name: itemName$
params.description: The name of the new item.

params.name: value
params.type: The value of the new item
params.description: The data type of the new item depends upon the data type of the value that you place in it.

params.name: Resulting NotesItem
params.description: String

params.name: Array of String
params.description: Multivalued text item with a value corresponding to each element of the array

params.name: NotesItem
params.description: Item whose data type matches the NotesItem type and whose value(s) match the NotesItem value(s)

returns.name: notesItem
returns.description: A NotesItem. The new item.

methods.description: To keep the new item in the document, you must call the Save method after calling AppendItemValue. If the document already has an item called itemName$, AppendItemValue does not replace it. Instead, it creates another item of the same name, and gives it the value you specify. The IsSummary property of the new item defaults to True, which means that the item value can be displayed in a view or folder.
methods.syntax: You can also create an item in a document using the NotesDocument "extended class" syntax, which eliminates the need for AppendItemValue. For example, you have the following script: You can achieve the same result by doing the following: This syntax lets you treat NotesDocument as an "extended class" by using an item name as if it were a property of NotesDocument. In the preceding example, "Subject" is used as if it is a property of the NotesDocument class. The result is the same as if you used AppendItemValue, but note these differences in behavior: While this syntax may be adequate for one-time scripts, you should prefer appendItemValue because:

methods.name: AttachVCard

params.name: ClientADTObject
params.description: The Contact document or documents to be attached in vCard format. If a collection is specified, a single attachment will be made with the individual vCards lying end to end.

methods.description: To keep the new attachment in the document, you must call the Save method after calling AttachVCard. If an empty collection is supplied, the error "No documents in collection" will be thrown. If documents are not Contact documents, the documents will not be attached, and the error "Attach of VCard failed" will be thrown.

methods.name: CloseMIMEEntities

params.name: savechanges
params.description: Optional. Whether MIME entity content and header changes are saved in the associated in-memory items.

params.name: entityitemname$
params.description: Optional. The name of the item or items associated with the MIME processing. "Body" is the default.

returns.name: flag
returns.description: Boolean.

methods.description: This method allows you terminate the scope of MIME processing so you can safely work on items through the item interface. Do not work on items directly without first closing MIME processing. Saving the MIME entity content and headers with this method is an in-memory process. To save document content permanently, you must call Save in NotesDocument. This method returns False if no MIME processing is associated with items of the specified name.

methods.name: ComputeWithForm

params.name: doDataTypes
params.description: The method ignores this parameter. Specify either True or False.

params.name: raiseError
params.description: If True, an error is raised if the validation fails. If False, no error is raised; instead, the method returns False if validation fails.

methods.description: Notes® determines the form for the document using: In the Notes® user interface, you must use a form to create a document. The document must meet the form's requirements for input validation, and the user interface informs you if the document does not meet these requirements. With the Notes® classes, you can create a document without a form. The ComputeWithForm method provides a means of checking that the data you've placed in a document meets a form's requirements, although (unlike in the user interface) you can still save a document if the ComputeWithForm returns False or raises an error. When you create a new document, however, no form item exists in the back-end. To avoid having ComputeWithForm fall back to the default form, put a Form field on your form when you create it. That way, you start with something in the back-end, and you get the expected results from your new document. Otherwise, you can get results from fields that exist only in the default form, but not the document you create.

methods.name: ConvertToMIME

params.name: conversionType
params.description: Optional. One of the following:

params.name: options
params.description: Optional. Reserved for future use.

methods.description: The document's form is evaluated and the fields are used in the MIME conversion. The conversion of rich text is imperfect.

methods.name: CopyAllItems

params.name: notesDocument
params.description: The destination document.

params.name: replace
params.description: If True, the items in the destination document are replaced. If False (default), the items in the destination document are appended.

methods.description: Given a destination document, copies all of the items in the current document into the destination document. The item names are unchanged.

methods.name: CopyItem

params.name: item
params.description: The item, usually from another document, that you want to copy. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

params.name: newName$
params.description: The name to assign to the copied item. Specify an empty string ("") to retain the item's existing name.

returns.name: notesItem
returns.description: A NotesItem. A copy of the specified item, identical except for its newName$.

methods.description: Given an item, copies it into the current document and optionally assigns the copied item a new name.
methods.syntax: Set notesItem = notesDocument.CopyItem( item , newName$ ) Call notesDocument.CopyItem( item , newName$ ) Use the Call statement when the program does not require the return value. Use the Set statement if the program requires the return value. Both statements yield the same results for this method.

methods.name: CopyToDatabase

params.name: notesDatabase
params.description: The database in which you want a copy of the document. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: newNotesDocument
returns.description: The new document in the specified database.

methods.description: Copies a document into the specified database.

methods.name: CreateMIMEEntity

params.name: itemName$
params.description: The name of the item containing the MIME entity. "Body" is the default.

returns.name: notesMIMEEntity
returns.description: NotesMIMEEntity. The new MIME entity.

methods.description: An item created with this method is of type MIME_PART. To initialize the MIME entity, provide some content and save the containing document. A MIME entity named "Body" gets special support as a Domino® mail message. In particular, non-Content headers become items in the document. If you specify an item name other than Body, the containing document cannot be mailed.

methods.name: CreateReplyMessage

params.name: all
params.description: If True, the new document recipient list contains all the recipients of the original. If False, the new document recipient list contains only the sender of the original.

returns.name: replyNotesDocument
returns.description: A reply to the current document.

methods.description: The new document does not contain a Subject item. If you want one, the script must explicitly add it to the document. The new document does not get automatically mailed. If you want to mail it, the script must explicitly call the Send method.

methods.name: CreateRichTextItem

params.name: name$
params.description: The name of the new rich text item.

returns.name: notesRichTextItem
returns.description: NotesRichTextItem. The newly created item.

methods.description: For more information, see the NotesRichTextItem class.

methods.name: Encrypt

params.name: userid
params.description: After setting the User id, documents in this database will be decrypted with encryption keys of this user id

params.name: idfile
params.description: Provides the file path of id file. After setting it, all documents in this database will be decrypted with encryption keys of this id file.

params.name: password
params.description: After setting the User id, documents in this database will be decrypted with encryption keys of this user id

methods.description: General usage The encrypted document is not written to disk until you call the Save method. Only the items for which the IsEncrypted property is True are encrypted. Items for which the IsEncrypted property is False remain visible to any user, even if the user does not have the proper encryption key. If the EncryptionKeys property is set with one or more named keys, those keys are used to encrypt the document. Any user who has one of the encryption keys can decrypt the document. If there are no encryption keys specified, the document is encrypted with the user's public key, in which case only the user who encrypted the document can decrypt it.
methods.syntax: Call notesDocument.Encrypt Call notesDocument.Encrypt(userid) Call notesDocument.Encrypt(idfile, password)

methods.name: GetAttachment

params.name: fileName$
params.description: The name of the file attachment you want.

returns.name: notesEmbeddedObject
returns.description: A NotesEmbeddedObject that represents the file attachment. Returns Nothing if an attachment by the specified name is not found.

methods.description: The Parent property for the returned NotesEmbeddedObject returns Nothing, since it was not accessed through a NotesRichTextItem.

methods.name: GetFirstItem

params.name: name$
params.description: The name of the item you want to find.

returns.name: notesItem
returns.description: NotesItem or NotesRichTextItem. The first item with the specified name. Returns Nothing if the document does not contain an item with this name.

methods.description: If multiple items in a document have the same name, programmatic access is limited to the first item. The remaining items yield invalid data. A work-around is to get the first item, process it, remove it, again get the first item (which was the second item), and so on until you process all the items with the same name. If you do not save the document, the items are not actually removed. However, the recommendation is that you avoid creating multiple items with the same name. If the value of a field is computed for display, the value is not stored as an item. The value is inaccessible from a NotesDocument object, unless the object is accessed as the Document property of NotesUIDocument, and GetFirstItem returns Nothing. In some cases, you can use LotusScript® to access the field value another way. For example, if a document has a DateComposed field that's computed for display with a formula @Created, use the Created property in NotesDocument.

methods.name: GetItemValue

params.name: itemName$
params.description: The name of an item.

returns.name: value
returns.description: The value or values contained in the specified name. The data type of the value depends on the data type of the item.

methods.description: If multiple items have the same name, this method returns the value of the first item. For text, number, and time-date items, GetItemValue always returns an array, even when there is only a single value in the item. If you know the item contains only a single value, access the first element in the array, which is at index 0. If you know the item contains multiple values, but you don't know how many, iterate over the array elements using the Forall statement. To get a date-time value as an array of NotesDateTime and NotesDateRange objects, see GetItemValueDateTimeArray.
methods.syntax: You can also access the contents of an item directly, without using GetItemValue. The following two statements are equivalent: This syntax lets you access and modify items the same way you access and modify other NotesDocument properties. The return value is the same, that is, an array of values for text, number, or time-date items, and a string for rich text items. You can set the value of an item with this syntax, too: While this syntax may be adequate for one-time scripts, you should prefer GetItemValue because: For more information about setting an item value with this syntax, see the AppendItemValue and ReplaceItemValue methods.

methods.name: GetItemValueCustomDataBytes

params.name: itemName$
params.description: The name of the item.

params.name: dataTypeName$
params.description: The name of the data type. This name must match the data type name specified when the item was written.

returns.name: byteArray
returns.description: Array of type Byte. The value of the item.

methods.description: An item that contains custom data is of type Item.USERDATA (14). For another method that gets custom data, see: To write custom data to an item, see:

methods.name: GetItemValueDateTimeArray

params.name: itemName$
params.description: The name of a date-time item.

returns.name: notesDateTimeArray
returns.description: Array of type NotesDateTime and NotesDateRange. Each element in the array corresponds to a value in the item. If the item contains a single value, the array has one element.

methods.description: If multiple items have the same name, this method returns the value of the first item. To get the value as an array of variants of type Date, see GetItemValue.

methods.name: GetMIMEEntity

params.name: itemName$
params.description: The name of the item containing the MIME entity. "Body" is the default.

returns.name: notesMIMEEntity
returns.description: NotesMIMEEntity. The MIME entity.

methods.description: An item containing a MIME entity is of type MIME_PART. This method returns Nothing if the requested item does not exist or is not of type MIME_PART. This method is equivalent to NotesDocument.GetFirstItem followed by NotesItem.GetMIMEEntity.

methods.name: GetRead method

params.name: username

returns.name: flag
returns.description: Boolean. True if the current document has been read by username. False if it has not been read by username.

methods.description: Returns true if the current document has been read, otherwise false.

methods.name: GetReceivedItemText

returns.name: stringArray
returns.description: Array of type String. The text values of the Received items, one item per array element.

methods.description: This method applies to Received items generated from an Internet mail message. The items can be in MIME or Notes® format. If the document has no Received items, this method returns an array of one element whose value is an empty string. A Received item with an incorrect format (not an Internet mail message) raises error lsERR_NOTES_INVALID_RECEIVEDITEM (4639) "Received Item is not standard format - access with GetItemValue."

methods.name: HasItem

params.name: itemName$
