params.name: document
params.description: The document to which notesDocument becomes a response. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

methods.description: You must call Save after this method if you want to save the change you have made.

methods.name: MarkRead

params.name: username
params.description: Optional. If present, the method marks the note read on behalf of the given name. If omitted, the method marks the note read on behalf of the current user ID.

methods.description: If the database does not track unread marks, all documents are considered read, and this method has no effect.

methods.name: MarkUnread

params.name: username
params.description: Optional. If present, the method marks the note unread on behalf of the given name. If omitted, the method marks the note unread on behalf of the current user ID.

methods.description: If the database does not track unread marks, all documents are considered read, and this method has no effect.

methods.name: New

params.name: notesDatabase
params.description: The database in which to create a new document.

methods.description: Given a database, New creates a document in the database and returns a NotesDocument object that represents the document. You must call Save if you want the new document to be saved to disk.
methods.syntax: Dim variableName As New NotesDocument( notesDatabase ) Set notesDocument = New NotesDocument( notesDatabase )

methods.name: PutInFolder

params.name: folderName$
params.description: The name of the folder in which to place the document. The folder may be personal if the script is running on the workstation. If the folder is within another folder, specify a path to it, separating folder names with backward slashes, for example, "Vehicles\Bikes".

params.name: createonfail
params.description: Optional. Specify True so that a folder will be created if it does not exist.

methods.description: If the document is already inside the folder you specify, PutInFolder does nothing. If you specify a path to a folder, and none of the folders exists, the method creates all of them for you. For example: If neither Vehicles nor Bikes exists, PutInFolder creates both, placing the Bikes folder inside the Vehicles folder. This method cannot add the first document to a folder that is "Shared, Personal on first use."

methods.name: Remove

params.name: force
params.description: If True, the document is deleted even if another user modifies the document after the script opens it. If False, the document is not deleted if another user modifies it.

methods.description: This method does a soft deletion if "Allow soft deletions" is enabled. See RemovePermanently to do a hard deletion. If you access a NotesDocument object through the Document property of NotesUIDocument, you can't delete the back-end document. If you could, the UI rendition would have no basis. You must access the document strictly through the back-end. A deleted document cannot be used as a basis for navigation in a view or a document collection.

methods.name: RemoveFromFolder

params.name: folderName$
params.description: The name of the folder from which to remove the document. The folder may be personal if the script is running on a workstation. If the folder is within another folder, specify a path to it, separating folder names with backward slashes, for example, "Vehicles\Bikes".

methods.description: The method does nothing if the document is not in the folder you specify, or if the folder you specify does not exist.

methods.name: RemoveItem

params.name: itemName$
params.description: The name of the item to delete from the document. If more than one item has the specified name, all items with this name are deleted. If there is no item with the specified name,the method does nothing.

methods.description: You can achieve the same result with the Remove method in the NotesItem class. Remember to call Save to save your changes to a document.

methods.name: RemovePermanently

params.name: force
params.description: If True, the document is deleted even if another user modifies the document after the script opens it. If False, the document is not deleted if another user modifies it.

methods.description: This method does a hard deletion even if "Allow soft deletions" is enabled. See Remove to do a soft deletion. If you access a NotesDocument object through the Document property of NotesUIDocument, you can't delete the back-end document. If you could, the UI rendition would have no basis. You must access the document strictly through the back-end. A deleted document cannot be used as a basis for navigation in a view or a document collection.

methods.name: RenderToRTItem

params.name: notesRichTextItem
params.description: The destination for the picture. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: flag
returns.description: Boolean. If True, the method was successful. If False, the method was not successful and the rich text item remains unchanged. This can happen if an input validation formula fails on the document's form.

methods.description: The picture is created using both the document and its form. Therefore, the input translation and validation formulas of the form are executed. If the target rich text item is in a new document, you must save the document before calling RenderToRTItem.

methods.name: ReplaceItemValue

params.name: itemName$
params.description: The name of the item(s) you want to replace.

params.name: value
params.type: The value of the new item
params.description: The data type of the item depends upon the data type of the value, and does not need to match the data type of the old item.

params.name: Resulting NotesItem
params.description: String

params.name: Array of String
params.description: Text item containing each element of the value

params.name: NotesItem
params.description: Item whose data type matches the NotesItem type and whose value(s) match the NotesItem value(s)

returns.name: notesItem
returns.description: NotesItem. The new item, which replaces all previous items that had the specified name. The new item has a new value, which may be of a different data type than the old item.

methods.description: To keep the new item in the document, you must call the Save method after calling ReplaceItemValue. The IsSummary property of the new item defaults to True, which means that the item value can be displayed in a view or folder. Do not use this method to replace the value of a rich text item (or MIME entity) unless you want to change it to a text item. To replace the contents of a rich text item, use NotesDocument.RemoveItem or NotesRichTextItem.Remove to delete the old item, NotesDocument.CreateRichTextItem to create a new one with the same name, and NotesRichTextItem.AppendText and other methods to provide the new content.
methods.syntax: You can also change an item's value using the NotesDocument "extended class" syntax, which eliminates the need for ReplaceItemValue. For example, you have the following script: You can achieve the same result by doing the following: This syntax lets you treat NotesDocument as an "extended class" by using an item name as if it were a property of NotesDocument. In the preceding example, "Subject" is used as if it is a property of the NotesDocument class. The result is the same as if you used ReplaceItemValue, except that ReplaceItemValue returns a NotesItem object representing the item you just created, and the IsSummary property defaults to True. While this syntax may be adequate for one-time scripts, you should prefer ReplaceItemValue because:

methods.name: ReplaceItemValueCustomDataBytes

params.name: itemName$
params.description: The name of the item.

params.name: dataTypeName$
params.description: A name for the data type. When getting custom data, use this name for verification.

params.name: byteArray
params.description: The custom data.

methods.description: The new item replaces all previous items with the same name. To keep the changes, you must call Save after calling ReplaceItemValueCustomData. The custom data cannot exceed 64K.

methods.name: Save

params.name: force
params.description: If True, the document is saved even if someone else edits and saves the document while the script is running. The last version of the document that was saved wins; the earlier version is discarded.

params.name: createResponse
params.description: If True, the current document becomes a response to the original document (this is what the replicator does when there's a replication conflict). If False, the save is canceled. If the force parameter is True, the createResponse parameter has no effect.

params.name: markRead
params.description: If True, the document is marked as read on behalf of the current user ID. If False (default), the document is not marked as read.

methods.description: Avoid using this method in a document returned by NotesUIDocument.Document. Use NotesUIDocument.Save instead. Otherwise, a user who saves in the front-end creates a save conflict. When you use a NotesDocument, you access the "items" of the note directly, without using any form. Any computed field formulas, input translations, or validations, will not occur when you call the Save method. If you need a field to have a particular value, your code must assign that field. Or, use ComputeWithForm method to recalculate formulas. The Send method does not automatically convert your document into a regular memo. It is possible to Send documents that contain none of the usual fields that you would expect to find in an email message, such as Subject and Body. You can design custom forms with many fields on them, and mail documents created using those forms to another mail user or to a mail-in database, where the field information remains in its original fields. However, to make use of these documents as you intended, the receiving user or database must have a form with the same name (or alias) and the same fields.

methods.name: Send

params.name: attachForm
params.description: If True, the form is stored and sent along with the document. If False, it isn't. Do not attach a form that uses computed subforms.

params.name: recipients
params.description: Optional. The recipients of the document. See the following information.

methods.description: The following rules apply to specification of the recipient or recipients: If you have only Reader access to a database, you can run an agent that creates and sends a document, but the agent will not work if you attach a file to that document. Two kinds of items affect the mailing of the document when you use Send:

methods.name: Sign
methods.description: If you want the signature to be saved, you must call the Save method after signing the document. If the script is running on a server, the signer must be listed in "Run unrestricted methods and operations" under the Security tab in the Server record of the Domino® Directory. Otherwise the following error occurs: lsERR_NOTES_SIGN_NOPERM (4165) "You must have permission to sign documents for server based agents."

methods.name: UnLock
methods.description: IsDocumentLockingEnabled in NotesDatabase must be True or this method raises an error. This method raises an error if the current user is not one of the lock holders and does not have lock breaking authority.

nodes.name: NotesDocumentCollection
nodes.description: Represents a collection of documents from a database, selected according to specific criteria.

props.name: Count
props.description: Read-only. The number of documents in a collection.

props.name: IsSorted
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether the documents in a collection are sorted. A collection is sorted only when it results from a full-text search of a database.

props.name: Parent
props.description: Read-only. The database that contains a document collection.
props.syntax: To get: S et notesDatabase = notesDocumentCollection.Parent

props.name: Query
props.description: Read-only. The text of the query that produced a document collection, if the collection results from a full-text or other search.

props.name: UntilTime
props.description: This time should be specified as the "since" time in a subsequent call to GetModifiedDocuments where you want to get all modified documents since the most recent call. The database time may differ from the system time. Do not use the system time in GetModifiedDocuments where you want to get all modified documents since the most recent call. For collections not produced through GetModifiedDocuments, this property returns Nothing, an uninitialized object.

methods.name: AddDocument

params.name: document
params.description: If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

params.name: checkDups
params.description: Optional. Checks for duplicate documents. If True, checks for duplicate documents. Defaults to False.

methods.description: For sorted collections, the document will be appended. For unsorted collections, the document will be inserted using an internal algorithm. Sorted collections include all ViewEntryCollections and DocumentCollections created from FTSearch (including DocumentCollections modified by calling the FTSearch method on the collection). All other DocumentCollections are unsorted.

methods.name: Clone
methods.description: Returns a NotesDocumentCollection object which is a copy of the original collection.

methods.name: Contains

params.name: inputNotes
params.type: String, NotesDocument, NotesDocumentCollection, NotesViewEntry or NotesViewEntryCollection
params.description: The NotesDocument, NotesDocuments, NotesViewEntry, or NotesViewEntries that the method determines are or are not contained in the NotesViewEntryCollection. A single NotesDocument can be specified by note ID (a String value) or NotesDocument object. Multiple NotesDocuments must be specified as a NotesDocumentCollection. A single NotesViewEntry object can be specified, as long as the NotesViewEntry object has a NotesDocument associated with it. Multiple NotesViewEntry objects that have NotesDocuments associated with them, can be specified as a NotesViewEntryCollection.

methods.description: The document or documents whose containment this method determines (or the view entries associated with those documents) must be in the same database as the original collection. Otherwise, the method will return the error "the specified note or notes do not exist in the database" or, if a noteID was passed to the method that matches a noteID in the original collection's database, the method will use the unintended document. If inputNotes is an empty document collection, this method will return True. Example

methods.name: DeleteDocument

params.name: document
params.description: The document to be deleted. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

methods.description: The specified document must have originated in this collection. If the document does not exist in the collection, or if it was removed from the database by a RemoveAll operation, an error will be raised.

methods.name: FTSearch

params.name: query$
params.description: The full-text query. See the following for the syntax.

params.name: maxDocs%
params.description: The maximum number of documents you want returned from the query. Set this parameter to 0 to receive all matching documents.

methods.description: Conducts a full-text search of all the documents in a Notes® document collection, and reduces the collection to those documents that match.

methods.name: GetDocument

params.name: document
params.description: Any document in the collection. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesDocument
returns.description: NotesDocument. The document that matches the notesDocument parameter. If the specified document doesn't exist, returns Nothing.

methods.description: Returns the entry in the collection that corresponds to the specified document.

methods.name: GetFirstDocument

returns.name: notesDocument
returns.description: NotesDocument. The first document in the collection. If there are no documents in the collection, returns Nothing.

methods.description: Gets the first document in a collection.

methods.name: GetLastDocument

returns.name: notesDocument
returns.description: NotesDocument. The last document in the collection. If there are no documents in the collection, returns Nothing.

methods.description: Gets the last document in a collection.

methods.name: GetNextDocument

params.name: document
params.description: Any document in the collection. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesDocument
returns.description: NotesDocument. The document following the notesDocument parameter in the collection. If there is no next document, returns Nothing.

methods.description: When processing documents in a loop, do not delete the document, or modify it in a way that causes it to disappear from the view or for its position to change. In that situation, GetNextDocument method may not return a useful result because the document that was the next document, is now at a different position in the view. The most common symptom of this problem is that documents are skipped when you loop through them. To address this issue, change your logic so that you call GetNextDocument before making any change to the document. Or, use NotesView.AutoUpdate (= False) to prevent re-indexing of the view while you're using it.

methods.name: GetNthDocument

params.name: n&
params.description: A number indicating the document to return. Use 1 to indicate the first document in the collection, 2 to indicate the second document, and so on.

returns.name: notesDocument
returns.description: NotesDocument. The document in the specified position in the collection. If there is no document at the specified position, returns Nothing.

methods.description: Using GetNthDocument to iterate through a loop is strongly discouraged for performance reasons. See GetDocument, GetFirstDocument, GetLastDocument, GetNextDocument, and GetPrevDocument for the preferred loop structure.

methods.name: GetPrevDocument

params.name: document
params.description: Any document in the collection. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesDocument
returns.description: NotesDocument. The document preceding the specified document in the collection. If there is no previous document, returns Nothing.

methods.description: Given a document, finds the document immediately preceding it in a collection.

methods.name: Intersect

params.name: inputNotes
params.type: String, NotesDocument, NotesDocumentCollection, NotesViewEntry or NotesViewEntryCollection
params.description: The NotesDocument, NotesDocuments, NotesViewEntry, or NotesViewEntries that the method will intersect with the NotesViewEntryCollection. A single NotesDocument can be specified by note ID (a String value) or NotesDocument object. Multiple NotesDocuments must be specified as a NotesDocumentCollection. A single NotesViewEntry object can be specified, as long as the NotesViewEntry object has a NotesDocument associated with it. Multiple NotesViewEntry objects that have NotesDocuments associated with them, can be specified as a NotesViewEntryCollection.

methods.description: The document or documents (or the view entries and their associated documents) being intersected must be in the same database as the original document collection. Otherwise, the method will return the error "the specified note or notes do not exist in the database" or, if a noteID was passed to the method that matches a noteID in the original collection's database, the method will use the unintended document. Example

methods.name: MarkAllRead

params.name: username
params.description: Optional. If present, the method marks all documents in the collection as read on behalf of the given name. If omitted, the method marks all documents in the collection as read on behalf of the current user.

methods.description: If the database does not track unread marks, all documents are considered read, and this method has no effect.

methods.name: MarkAllUnread

params.name: username
params.description: Optional. If present, the method marks all documents in the collection as unread on behalf of the given name. If omitted, the method marks all documents in the collection as unread on behalf of the current user.

methods.description: If the database does not track unread marks, all documents are considered read, and this method has no effect.

methods.name: Merge

params.name: inputNotes
params.type: String, NotesDocument, NotesDocumentCollection, NotesViewEntry or NotesViewEntryCollection
params.description: The NotesDocument, NotesDocuments, NotesViewEntry or NotesViewEntries that the method will merge with the NotesViewEntryCollection. A single NotesDocument can be specified by note ID (a String value) or NotesDocument object. Multiple NotesDocuments must be specified as a NotesDocumentCollection. A single NotesViewEntry object can be specified, as long as the NotesViewEntry object has a NotesDocument associated with it. Multiple NotesViewEntry objects that have NotesDocuments associated with them, can be specified as a NotesViewEntryCollection.

methods.description: The document or documents (or the view entries and their associated documents) being merged by this method must be in the same database as the original collection. Otherwise, the method will return the error "the specified note or notes do not exist in the database" or, if a noteID was passed to the method that matches a noteID in the original collection's database, the method will use the unintended document. This method performs a union of the two document collections. Example

methods.name: PutAllInFolder

params.name: folderName$
params.description: The name of the folder in which to place the documents. The folder may be personal if the script is running on the workstation. If the folder is within another folder, specify a path to it, separating folder names with backward slashes, for example, "Vehicles\Bikes".

params.name: createonfail
params.description: Optional. If True (default), creates the folder if one does not already exist.

methods.description: If a document is already inside the folder you specify, PutAllInFolder does nothing for that document. If you specify a path to a folder, and none of the folders exist, the method creates all of them for you. For example: If neither Vehicles nor Bikes exists, PutAllInFolder creates both, placing the Bikes folder inside the Vehicles folder.

methods.name: RemoveAll

params.name: force
params.description: If True, a document is removed even if another user modifies the document after it is retrieved. If False, a document is not removed if another document modifies it first.

methods.description: Deletes all documents in a collection from a database.

methods.name: RemoveAllFromFolder

params.name: folderName$
params.description: The name of the folder from which to remove the document. The folder may be personal if the script is running on a workstation. If the folder is within another folder, specify a path to it, separating folder names with backward slashes, for example, "Vehicles\Bikes".

methods.description: The method does nothing for documents not in the folder you specify. This method does nothing if the folder you specify does not exist.

methods.name: StampAll

params.name: itemname
params.description: The name of the item.

params.name: value
params.description: A value appropriate for the item type.

methods.description: If the item does not exist, it is created. The item values are immediately written to the back-end documents. You do not have to use the Save method of NotesDocument after StampAll. However, any documents modified by your script must be saved before calling StampAll. This method does not modify existing NotesDocument objects. Documents must be retrieved again to see the changes.

methods.name: StampAllMulti method

params.name: document
params.description: The document contains multiple items, each with values appropriate for the item type.

methods.description: If an item does not exist, it is created. If the item is of a different data type, the existing item will be deleted and a new item of the new data type created. The item values are immediately written to the documents on the server. You do not have to use the Save method of NotesDocument after StampAllMulti. However, any documents modified by your script must be saved before calling StampAllMulti. This method does not modify existing NotesDocument objects. Documents must be retrieved again to see the changes.

methods.name: Subtract

params.name: inputNotes
params.type: String, NotesDocument, NotesDocumentCollection, NotesViewEntry or NotesViewEntryCollection
params.description: The NotesDocument, NotesDocuments, NotesViewEntry, or NotesViewEntries that the method will subtract from the NotesViewEntryCollection. A single NotesDocument can be specified by note ID (a String value) or NotesDocument object. Multiple NotesDocuments must be specified as a NotesDocumentCollection. A single NotesViewEntry object can be specified, as long as the NotesViewEntry object has a NotesDocument associated with it. Multiple NotesViewEntry objects that have NotesDocuments associated with them, can be specified as a NotesViewEntryCollection.

methods.description: The document or documents (or the view entries and their associated documents) being subtracted must be in the same database as the original document collection. Otherwise, the method will return the error "the specified note or notes do not exist in the database" or, if a noteID was passed to the method that matches a noteID in the original collection's database, the method will use the unintended document. Example

params.description: Read-only. The offset at which to split, starting from 0. When the offset is equal to the length of this node, the new node has no data.

returns.name: notesDOMTextNode
returns.description: The text node containing all the content at and after the offset point.

methods.description: After being split, this node contains all the content up to the offset point.

methods.name: SubstringData

params.name: offset
params.description: The offset from which to start extracting.

params.name: count
params.description: The number of characters to extract.

returns.name: String
returns.description: String. The specified substring.

methods.description: If the sum of offset and count exceeds the length, then all characters to the end of the data are returned.

params.name: oldChild
params.description: The node being replaced in the list. This object and the calling object must be derived from the same NotesDOMParser object.

returns.name: notesDOMNode
returns.description: The replaced node.

methods.description: The method fails if the nodes are not of the same type. For example, you cannot replace TEXT node with an ELEMENT node.

params.name: attributeName
params.description: The name of the attribute.

returns.description: The new NotesDOMAttributeNode object.

methods.description: Sets NodeName to attributeName. Sets LocalName, Prefix, NamespaceURI, and NodeValue to an empty string.

methods.name: CreateAttributeNodeNS

params.name: namespaceURI
params.description: The namespace URI of a qualified name.

params.name: qualifiedName
params.description: A qualified name, or the local part of a qualified name.

returns.name: notesDOMAttributeNode
returns.description: The new NotesDOMAttributeNode object.

methods.description: Sets NodeName to qualifiedName. Sets LocalName to the local part of qualifiedName. Sets NamespaceURI to namespaceURI. Sets Prefix to the prefix part of qualifiedName, or an empty string if only the local part is specified. In the latter case, you must explicitly set Prefix. See NamespaceURI for a discussion of namespaces and qualified names. Refer to http://www.w3.org/TR/1999/REC-xml-names-19990114/#ns-qualnames for the W3C specification on qualified names. Refer to http://www.w3.org/TR/2001/WD-DOM-Level-3-Core-20010605/glossary.html#dt-qualifiedname for a definition.

params.description: String.

returns.name: notesDOMXMLDeclNode
returns.description: The new NotesDOMXMLDeclNode object.

methods.description: Sets NodeName to "#xmldecl" literally. Sets Encoding to encoding. For more information about declaration encoding, see http://www.w3.org/TR/REC-xml#NT-EncodingDecl. Sets Standalone to standalone. For more information see "Standalone Document Declaration" at http://www.w3.org/TR/REC-xml#NT-SDDecl.
methods.syntax: Set notesDOMXMLDeclNode = notesDOMDocumentNode .CreateXMLDeclNode( version$ , encoding$ , standalone$ )

methods.name: GetElementsByTagName

params.name: elementName
params.description: The name of the element to match on.

returns.name: notesDOMNodeList
returns.description: A new NotesDOMNodeList object containing all the matched NotesDOMElementNode objects.

methods.description: Set elementName to "*" to match all elements.

methods.name: GetElementsByTagNameNS

params.name: namespaceURI
params.description: The namespace URI of a qualified name.

params.name: localName
params.description: The local part of a qualified name.

returns.name: notesDOMNodeList
returns.description: A new NotesDOMNodeList object containing all the matched NotesDOMElementNode objects.

methods.description: Set localName to "*" to match all elements in namespaceURI. Set namespaceURI to "*" to match all elements with localName. Set both namespaceURI and localName to "*" to match all elements. See NamespaceURI for a discussion of namespaces and qualified names. Refer to http://www.w3.org/TR/1999/REC-xml-names-19990114/#ns-qualnames for the W3C specification on qualified names. Refer to http://www.w3.org/TR/2001/WD-DOM-Level-3-Core-20010605/glossary.html#dt-qualifiedname for a definition.

nodes.name: NotesDominoQuery
nodes.description: LotusScript® class to compile, tune, and run Domino® Query Language queries.

props.name: DesignDocumentsOnly
props.description: Specifies if the execute() and explain() methods should only search for design documents. If set to true, then only design documents will be searched for; if set to false, then only data documents are searched like normal. Default value is False.
props.syntax: Where dquery is a type NotesDominoQuery variable

props.name: MaxScanDocs
props.description: Specifies the maximum allowable number of documents scanned across all query terms. DQL execution returns an error when exceeded. Default is 500,000.

props.name: MaxScanEntries
props.description: Specifies the maximum allowable number of view entries scanned across all query terms. DQL execution return an error when exceeded. Default is 200,000.

props.name: NoViews
props.description: Specifies not to perform any view processing in satisfying a query. Default is False (off).

props.name: RebuildDesignCatalog
props.description: Specifies to DQL processing to completely rebuild the Design Catalog before processing a query using Explain or Execute.

props.name: RefreshDesignCatalog
props.description: Specifies to DQL processing to refresh the Design Catalog before processing a query using Explain or Execute.

props.name: RefreshFullText
props.description: Specifies to DQL processing to refresh the full text index for a database prior to query processing using Explain or Execute.

props.name: RefreshViews
props.description: Specifies to DQL processing to refresh every view it opens to perform any view processing to satisfy a query. Default is False.

props.name: TimeoutSec
props.description: Specifies the maximum allowable seconds a DQL query is allowed to run. DQL execution returns an error when exceeded. Default is 300 (5 minutes).

methods.name: CreateIndex

params.name: Field
params.description: The name of the field to be indexed.

params.name: IsVisible
params.description: Makes the view visible. If not specified, a hidden view is created using parentheses. For example, "myindex" becomes "(myindex)". If set to true, all users see the view created by the call when they open the database.

params.name: Nobuild
params.description: Doesn't build the view, allowing for normal view processing to do so. All views created by CreateIndex are created with refresh options set to "Automatic". If not specified, the view created is built as part of the CreateIndex operation.

methods.description: Creates an index (Domino view) that is optimized for DQL query terms.

methods.name: Execute

params.name: Query$
params.description: A query in DQL syntax.

params.name: ResultName
params.description: The name to use to save a DocumentCollection in the database for future DQL reference. The name is case-insensitive.

params.name: Replace
params.description: If a saved DocumentCollection with ResultName exists, overwrite it. If not set, an error is thrown indicating a conflict.

methods.description: Executes a query string passed in according to set parameters and returns named results in a database where they can be accessed again more quickly. For reuse of complex queries, this saves significant processing time.
methods.syntax: Set doccol = NotesDominoQuery.Execute(Query$, Optional String ResultName, Optional Boolean Replace, Optional Long ExpireHours)

methods.name: Explain

params.name: Query$
params.description: A query in DQL syntax.

returns.name: String
returns.description: To understand and work with explain output, see explain.

methods.description: Executes a query string passed in according to set parameters and returns an explain string to indicate how the query was executed.

methods.name: ListIndexes
methods.description: Lists the indexes that are optimized for Domino DQL query terms.

methods.name: Parse

params.name: Query$
params.description: A query in DQL syntax.

returns.name: String
returns.description: The error messages produced by a malformed query are self-explanatory.

methods.description: Parses a DQL query string for correct syntax.

methods.name: RemoveIndex

params.name: IndexName
params.description: The name of the index to remove.

methods.description: Removes an index (Domino view) that is optimized for DQL query terms. RemoveIndex is very powerful and can remove production views. Take care in choosing what indexes (views) to remove.

methods.name: ResetNamedVariables
methods.description: Removes all previously set named variables and values from a DominoQuery instance. If SetNamedVariable returns an error indicating that there is already a bound value for a specified name, call ResetNamedVariables to clear the variable state. Overwriting existing bound variable values is not supported; you must call ResetNamedVariables to clear the variable state prior to setting variables by the same name.

methods.name: SetNamedVariable

params.name: VariableName$
params.description: The name of a substitution variable specified in a DQL query, after the question mark. So "date_of_birth? is seen as ?date_of_birth in DQL syntax.

params.name: Value
params.description: Can be String, number or DateTime object.

methods.description: Assigns a value to a named substitution variable in a DQL query.

props.name: ExpandEntityReferences
props.description: Read-write. Indicates that every reference within a text value to an entity should be replaced by the entity's corresponding text value.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesDOMParser .ExpandEntityReferences To set: notesDOMParser .ExpandEntityReferences = flag

props.name: InputValidationOption
props.description: Read-write. Indicates that if a DTD is specified in the XML declaration statement, it should be used to validate the input XML.
props.syntax: To get: option% = notesDOMParser .InputValidationOption To set: notesDOMParser .InputValidationOption = option%

methods.name: Output

params.name: outputText
params.description: The content of the output.

methods.description: See SetOutput for a discussion of the output object. The NotesDOMParser class does not automatically generate output. You must explicitly call this method. For pipelined output, you must use this method in the PostDOMParse event.

methods.name: Parse

params.name: input
params.description: The input XML. A string or an object of the following type:

params.name: output
params.description: Output. An object of the following type:

methods.description: This method is an alternative to Process if pipelining is not used. You cannot explicitly read or write a NotesStream object associated with a file prior to using it for XML input or output. For example, if you write to a file then use it for XML input, you must close and reopen the NotesStream object.

methods.name: Serialize
methods.description: Walks the DOM document tree and streams the resulting XML to the DOM parser's output object, provided one was specified.

events.name: PostDOMParse
events.description: The reason for this event is to allow pipelined output. ProcessThe and Parse methods for the NotesDOMParser class do not automatically generate output. You must explicitly call Output. For pipelined output, you must call Output from this event. Output generated in this event becomes available to the next stage of the pipeline. Any processing that leads to the output must also, necessarily be in this event.

props.description: Read-write. Specifies whether to omit OLE objects that occur within documents.

nodes.name: NotesEmbeddedObject
nodes.description: Represents any one of the following:

props.name: Class
props.description: Read-only. For embedded objects and links, the name of the application that created the object.

props.description: Read-only. For embedded objects, provides a string representing the date and time the file was created.

props.name: FileEncoding
props.description: Read-only. For file attachments, returns the type of file encoding.

props.name: FileModified
props.description: Read-only. For embedded objects, provides a string representing the date and time the file was last modified.

props.name: FileSize
props.description: Read-only. For file attachments, the size of the file in bytes.

props.name: FitBelowFields
props.description: Read-write. Sizes the embedded object to fit below the first layout in the document. The embedded object takes over the entire document window below the layout, when activated.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesEmbeddedObject.FitBelowFields To set: notesEmbeddedObject.FitBelowFields = flag

props.name: FitToWindow
props.description: Read-write. Sizes the embedded object to fit the document window. The embedded object takes over the entire document window when activated.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesEmbeddedObject.FitToWindow To set: notesEmbeddedObject.FitToWindow = flag

props.name: Name
props.description: Read-only. The name used to reference an embedded object, object link, or attachment.

props.name: Object
props.description: Read-only. If an embedded object has been loaded into memory, returns the OLE handle (IDispatch handle). If the OLE object supports OLE Automation, you can invoke the methods and properties of the object using the handle.

props.name: Parent
props.description: Read-only. The rich-text item that holds an object.

props.name: RunReadOnly
props.description: Read-write. Runs the embedded object when reading the document.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesEmbeddedObject.RunReadOnly To set: notesEmbeddedObject.RunReadOnly = flag

props.name: Source
props.description: Read-only.

props.name: Type
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether a NotesEmbeddedObject is an embedded object, an object link, or a file attachment.

props.name: Verbs
props.description: Read-only. The verbs that an object supports, if the object is an OLE/2 embedded object.

methods.name: Activate

params.name: show
params.description: If True, the server application displays its user interface. If False, the server application hides its user interface.

returns.name: handleV
returns.description: Variant. The OLE handle to the object. Returns Nothing if the embedded object or object link does not support OLE automation.

methods.description: Scripts running on a server must set the show parameter to False. This method raises an error if you invoke it on a NotesEmbeddedObject that is a file attachment. This method may or may not return a valid OLE handle for object links, depending upon the application used to create the object link.

props.description: Tells the platform to return only a high-accuracy location. Not for use when indoors.

props.name: TimeoutSec property
props.description: Timeout in seconds for a NotesGPS request.

nodes.name: NotesGPSCoordinates
nodes.description: Contains the current coordinates of the position of a device. Currently supported for HCL Nomad beginning with Release 1.0.4.

props.name: Accuracy
props.description: Contains the relative accuracy of the current position if available.

props.name: Altitude
props.description: Contains the altitude of the current position if available.

props.name: AltitudeAccuracy
props.description: Contains the altitude accuracy of the current position if available.

props.name: Heading
props.description: Contains the heading of the current position if available.

props.name: Latitude
props.description: Contains the latitude of the current position if available.

props.name: Longitude
props.description: Contains the longitude of the current position if available.

props.name: Speed
props.description: Contains the speed of the current position if available.

nodes.name: NotesGPSPosition
nodes.description: Represents a position provided by the global positioning device on a platform. Currently supported for HCL Nomad beginning with Release 1.0.4.

props.name: TimeStamp
props.description: A NotesDateTime object representing the time at which a NotesGPSPosition was created by a call to NotesGPS.GetCurrentPosition() or updated by a call to NotesGPSPosition.Update().

methods.name: Update
methods.description: Requests the platform to update the current NotesGPSPosition.

nodes.name: NotesHTTPRequest
nodes.description: LotusScript class used to make HTTP requests to web servers.

props.name: Maxredirects
props.description: Number of redirects allowed in the HTTP request.

props.name: PreferJSONNavigator
props.description: It is a Boolean value that can be set to either True or False. By default, it is set to False.

props.name: Preferstrings
props.description: Controls the format used for the output of international characters.

props.name: PreferUTF8
props.description: This property is automatically set based on the values of two other properties: Preferstrings and Preferjsonnavigator. If either of these two properties is set to True, the HTTP request will prefer UTF8 and return the output characters as a UTF-8 byte array.

props.name: Responsecode
props.description: Returns the HTTP response code for a request to help identify any problem loading the request. This property is read-only.

props.name: Timeoutsec
props.description: Timeout in seconds for an HTTP request.

params.name: URL$
params.description: The URL that is needed to send the request.

returns.name: Returns Variant content as JSON UTF8 byte array. Use the preferstrings property to return Unicode rather than UTF8 byte array.

params.name: URL$

nodes.name: NotesIDVault
nodes.description: The NotesIDVault class is a representation of the secure storage facility for UserIDs that may be configured for Domino by policy. IDs are stored there to allow for easier management by administrators and users. IDs from more than one server may be stored within an IDVault. The NotesIDVault class provides methods to access UserID files, UserID objects, query the presence of an ID in the vault, sycnronise copies of IDs, and reset passwords. The NotesIDVault class can be used to access UserID objects or files. These can be used to accomplish the encryption or decryption of Documents while running on the server .

nodes.name: NotesInternational
nodes.description: Represents the international settings in the operating environment, for example, the Windows™ Control Panel international settings. When any of these settings is changed in the operating environment, Domino® recognizes the new settings immediately.

props.description: Read-only. The character used to separate months, days, and years, for example, the slash.

props.description: Read-only. The decimal separator in number format, for example, the decimal point.

props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether the order of the date format is month-day-year.

props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether the order of the date format is year-month-day.

props.name: Yesterday
props.description: Read-only. The string that means yesterday in a time-date specification, for example, "Yesterday" in English.

nodes.name: NotesLLMRequest
nodes.description: LotusScript class used to send request to Language Model (LLM) via Domino IQ server.

methods.name: CancelStream
methods.description: When you use CompletionStream method to receive response in stream mode, you can use CancelStream method to stop the stream of response if you want.

methods.name: Completion

params.name: SERVER$
params.description: The Domino Server IQ name that is needed to send the request. If empty, the request will be routed to the Domino IQ server configured to service the command.

params.name: COMMAND$
params.description: The name of a command document defined in the Domino IQ configuration database. The command document specifies a system prompt that guides the LLM behavior and responses.

params.name: userPROMPT$
params.description: The user prompt is the specific input given by the caller of this method. This prompt is merged with the system prompt defined in the command document and then presented to the language model through the Domino IQ server.

returns.name: NotesLLMResponse
returns.description: Example

methods.description: Sends a chat completion request to a large language model (LLM) hosted by the Domino IQ server.

methods.name: CompletionStream

params.name: SERVER$
params.description: The Domino Server IQ name that is needed to send the request. If empty, the request will be routed to the Domino IQ server configured to service the command.

params.name: COMMAND$
params.description: The name of a command document defined in the Domino IQ configuration database. The command document specifies a system prompt that guides the LLM behavior and responses.

params.name: userPROMPT$
params.description: The user prompt is the specific input given by the caller of this method. This prompt is merged with the system prompt defined in the command document and then presented to the language model through the Domino IQ server.

methods.description: Send a chat completion method request to a large language model (LLM) hosted by a DominoIQ and receive response in stream mode. Base on the notify event fired by DominoIQ server, user can start process response immediately.

methods.name: GetAvailableCommands

params.name: SERVER$
params.description: The Domino Server IQ name that is needed to send the request. If empty, the request will be routed to the Domino IQ server configured to service the command.

params.name: [,suppressErrors ]
params.description: Optional. If True, suppress any internal Notes error handling, will not raise exception only return Empty array of strings.

methods.description: If not pass in suppressErrors = True, raises an Error if availability of a request can not be determined due to lower-level failures. If your agent uses this method on Notes Client, keep in mind that Notes Client will catch the command list until it is restarted. To ensure the method return up-to-date command list, you need to restart Notes Client after making any updates.

methods.name: IsCommandAvailable

params.name: SERVER$
params.description: The Domino Server IQ name that is needed to send the request. If empty, the request will be routed to the Domino IQ server configured to service the command.

params.name: COMMAND$
params.description: The name of a command document defined in the Domino IQ configuration database. The command document specifies a system prompt that guides the LLM behavior and responses.

params.name: suppressErrors
params.description: Optional. If True, suppress any internal Notes error handling, will not raise exception only return False in case of error.

returns.name: Returns True if the command is available for Domino IQ.
returns.description: Returns False if the command is not available for Domino IQ.

methods.description: If not pass in suppressErrors = True, raises an Error if the availability of a request can not be determined due to lower-level failures. If your agent uses this method on Notes Client, keep in mind that Notes Client will catch the command list until it is restarted. To ensure the method checks the updated command list, you need to restart Notes Client after making any updates.

nodes.name: NotesLLMResponse
nodes.description: LotusScript class that represents the response from the NotesLLMRequest.completion method.

props.name: Content
props.description: The response message generated by Language Model.

props.name: FinishReason
props.description: The reason the language model stopped generating a response.

props.name: Role
props.description: Role who send request (either system, user or assistant) Role associated with the message. Usually this is "assistant" when produced by the NotesLLMRequest.completion method. Values can vary based on the language model in use.

nodes.name: NotesLog
nodes.description: Enables you to record actions and errors that take place during a script's execution. You can record actions and errors in:

props.name: LogActions
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether action logging is enabled or not.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesLog .LogActions To set: notesLog .LogActions = flag

props.name: LogErrors
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether error logging is enabled or not.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesLog .LogErrors To set: notesLog .LogErrors = flag

props.name: NumActions
props.description: Read-only. The number of actions logged so far.

props.name: NumErrors
props.description: Read-only. The number of errors logged so far.

props.name: OverwriteFile
props.description: Read-write. For a log that records to a file, indicates whether the log should write over the existing file or append to it. This property has no effect on logs that record to a mail message or database.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesLog .OverwriteFile To set: notesLog .OverwriteFile = flag

props.name: Parent
props.description: Read-only. The session that contains a NotesLog object.

props.name: ProgramName
props.description: Read-write. The name that identifies the script whose actions and errors you're logging. The name is the same as the name specified with New or CreateLog.
props.syntax: To get: programName$ = notesLog .ProgramName To set: notesLog .ProgramName = programName$

methods.name: New

params.name: programName$
params.description: A name that identifies the log.

methods.description: New creates a log with the name you specify.
methods.syntax: Dim variableName As New NotesLog( programName$ ) Set notesLog = New NotesLog( programName$ )

methods.name: Close
methods.description: If you are logging to a mail message, Close sends the mail message to its recipient(s).

methods.name: LogAction

params.name: description$
params.description: A description of the action that the script performed, as you want it to appear in the log.

methods.description: The behavior of this method depends upon the type of log you've opened.

methods.name: LogError

params.name: code%
params.description: A number indicating which error occurred.

params.name: description$
params.description: A description of the error that occurred, as you want it to appear in the log.

methods.description: The behavior of this method depends upon the type of log you've opened.

methods.name: LogEvent

params.name: message$
params.description: The message to send to the network.

params.name: queuename$
params.description: The name of the queue. Domino® picks a queue if you send an empty string ("").

params.name: type%
params.description: Indicates the kind of event being logged. May be any of the following:

params.name: EV_ALARM (8)
params.description: EV_COMM (1)

params.name: EV_MAIL (3)
params.description: EV_MISC (6)

params.name: EV_REPLICA (4)
params.description: EV_RESOURCE (5)

params.name: EV_SECURITY (2)
params.description: EV_SERVER (7)

params.name: EV_UNKNOWN (0)
params.description: EV_UPDATE (9)

params.name: severity%
params.description: Indicates the severity of the event being logged. May be any of the following:

params.name: SEV_FAILURE (2)
params.description: SEV_FATAL (1)

params.name: SEV_NORMAL (5)
params.description: SEV_WARNING1 (3)

methods.description: Only scripts running on a server can use this method. This method is a way to post to API event queues and does not affect database, mail, file and agent logs. Using the LogAction or LogError methods has no effect on event logging.
methods.syntax: Call notesLog .LogEvent( message$ , queuename$ , type% , severity% )

methods.name: OpenAgentLog
methods.description: This method stores output in the agent log and fails if there is no current agent running. To display an agent log, select the agent and choose Agent - Log.

methods.name: OpenFileLog

params.name: path$
params.description: The path and file name of the log file. If the file does not exist, the method creates it for you. If a directory in the path does not exist, the method raises an error.

methods.description: If an agent is signed by a user that has unrestricted access then this method can be called on the server. To write over an existing log file, you must set the OverwriteFile property to True before calling the OpenFileLog method.

methods.name: OpenMailLog

params.name: recipientsV
params.description: The recipients of the mail memo. Can be a string containing the name of a single user or an array of strings containing the names of several users.

params.name: subject$
params.description: The subject of the mail memo.

methods.description: When you call this method, it opens the current user's mail database, as specified in the Address Book, and uses this database to create and send the mail memo. The memo is not saved to the database.

methods.name: OpenNotesLog

params.name: server$
params.description: The server on which the database log resides. Use an empty string ("") to indicate the current computer. If the script runs on the workstation, the empty string indicates a local database. If the script runs on a server, it indicates a database on that server.

params.name: dbfile$
params.description: The path and file name of the database. If you specify "" for a server name, you must specify the full path to the log database, including the drive letter.

methods.description: One document is created in the database for each error or action that you log. Each document has a Form item set to "Log Entry" and contains the following items: Item Contents

nodes.name: NotesMIMEEntity
nodes.description: Represents the MIME (Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions) content of a document.

props.name: BoundaryEnd
props.description: Read-only. The boundary that follows the last child entity in a multipart entity.

props.name: BoundaryStart
props.description: Read-only. The boundary that precedes a child entity in a multipart entity.

props.name: Charset
props.description: Read-only. Content character set of a MIME entity.

props.name: ContentAsText
props.description: Read-only. The content of a MIME entity in text format.

props.name: ContentSubType
props.description: Read-only. The second part of the Content-Type of a MIME entity.

props.name: ContentType
props.description: Read-only. The first part of the Content-Type of a MIME entity.

props.name: Encoding
props.description: Read-only. Current® encoding for the non-header content of a MIME entity.

props.name: HeaderObjects
props.description: Read-only. All headers for a MIME entity.

props.name: Headers
props.description: Read-only. All headers for a MIME entity as one string.

props.name: Preamble
props.description: Read-write. Preamble of a MIME multipart entity.
props.syntax: To get: preamble$ = notesMIMEEntity .Preamble To set: notesMIMEEntity .Preamble = preamble$

methods.name: CreateChildEntity

params.name: nextSibling
params.description: Optional. An existing child of the calling MIME entity. If this parameter is specified, the new MIME entity is positioned ahead of it.

returns.name: childNotesMIMEEntity
returns.description: NotesMIMEEntity. The child MIME entity.

methods.description: A multipart MIME entity consists of parent and child entities. A parent can be a child. The child entities of a parent form a sequential list. This method establishes the calling entity as a parent and establishes the new entity as its child. If the parent has no existing child entity, the new entity becomes the first child. If nextSibling is specified, this entity is placed ahead of it. Otherwise, this entity follows the last existing child entity.

methods.name: CreateHeader

params.name: headerName$
params.description: The name of the header.

returns.name: notesMIMEHeader
returns.description: NotesMIMEHeader. The new MIME header.

methods.description: The MIME header applies to the calling MIME entity. The new MIME header has only a name. It has no value or parameters. This method returns Nothing if creation of the header exceeds a known limit for headers of the same name.

methods.name: CreateParentEntity

returns.name: notesMIMEEntity
returns.description: NotesMIMEEntity. The new MIME entity.

methods.description: This method generates for the new parent a Content-Type header with "multipart/mixed" as the value, and a boundary parameter. If the calling entity is named "Body" (is a top-level mail entity), associated envelope headers are promoted to the parent. The calling entity does retain its "Content-" headers.

methods.name: DecodeContent
methods.description: This method decodes the non-header content of a MIME entity if the entity is encoded, and removes the Content-Transfer-Encoding header if one exists. Encoding contains the current encoding. EncodeContent also affects an entity's encoding.

methods.name: EncodeContent

params.name: encoding
params.description: The new encoding.

methods.description: This method affects the non-header content of a MIME entity and the Content-Transfer-Encoding header as defined in RFC-2045. Actual encoding of the content occurs only for ENC_BASE64 and ENC_QUOTED_PRINTABLE. Other options change the header but not the content. ENC_QUOTED_PRINTABLE affects only "text" content. If the content is already encoded, no action occurs. You must decode the content first.

methods.name: GetContentAsBytes

params.name: stream
params.description: Output medium for the byte stream.

params.name: decoded
params.description: Optional.

methods.description: This method sets the stream Position at end of stream.

methods.name: GetContentAsText

params.name: stream
params.description: Output medium for the text.

params.name: decoded
params.description: Optional.

methods.description: To get the MIME content as a String, see the ContentAsText property. This method sets the stream Position at end of stream.

methods.name: GetEntityAsText

params.name: stream
params.description: Output medium for the text.

params.name: headerFilters
params.description: Optional. Names of headers that may be present in the MIME entity. See the next parameter.

params.name: inclusive
params.description: Optional.

methods.description: Any encoding is left in place. This method excludes any leading boundary string defined by a multipart parent. This method sets the stream Position at end of stream.

methods.name: GetFirstChildEntity

returns.name: notesMIMEEntity
returns.description: NotesMIMEEntity. The first child MIME entity or Nothing.

methods.description: Returns the first child MIME entity that is contained within a multipart MIME entity.

methods.name: GetNextEntity

params.name: search%
params.description: Optional.

returns.name: notesMIMEEntity
returns.description: NotesMIMEEntity. The next MIME entity or Nothing.

methods.description: Returns the MIME entity immediately following the current MIME entity within a multipart entity.

methods.name: GetNextSibling

returns.name: notesMIMEEntity
returns.description: NotesMIMEEntity. The next sibling MIME entity or Nothing.

methods.description: Returns the MIME entity immediately following the current MIME entity at the same level within a multipart MIME entity.

methods.name: GetNthHeader

params.name: headerName$
params.description: The name of the header.

params.name: instance%
params.description: Optional. The header instance where 1 is the first header. Defaults to 1.

returns.name: notesMIMEHeader
returns.description: NotesMIMEHeader. The MIME header or Nothing.

methods.description: Multiple headers of the same name exist in the order they were last written.

methods.name: GetParentEntity

returns.name: notesMIMEEntityparent
returns.description: NotesMIMEEntity. The parent MIME entity. If there is no parent, GetParentEntity returns null.

methods.description: Returns the parent MIME entity of the current MIME entity within a multipart entity.

methods.name: GetPrevEntity

params.name: search%
params.description: Optional.

returns.name: notesMIMEEntity
returns.description: NotesMIMEEntity. The previous MIME entity or Nothing.

methods.description: Returns the MIME entity immediately preceding the current MIME entity within a multipart entity.

methods.name: GetPrevSibling

returns.name: notesMIMEEntity
returns.description: NotesMIMEEntity. The previous sibling MIME entity or Nothing.

methods.description: Returns the MIME entity immediately preceding the current MIME entity at the same level within a multipart MIME entity.

methods.name: GetSomeHeaders

params.name: headerFilters
params.description: Optional. Names of headers that may be present in the MIME entity. See the next parameter.

params.name: inclusive
params.description: Optional.

returns.name: String
returns.description: String. The headers.

methods.description: If you specify no parameters, this method is the same as Headers. Representation is as one string with new lines separating the headers.

methods.name: Remove
methods.description: Removes the current MIME entity and its children.

methods.name: SetContentFromBytes

params.name: stream
params.description: The byte input. This input replaces any existing content. If the stream is empty, any current content is removed.

params.name: contentType
params.description: Content type/subtype of input. This parameter generates a Content-Type header.

params.name: encoding
params.description: The MIME transfer encoding, which should reflect the encoding of the input stream. This parameter generates a Content-Transfer-Encoding header. See also EncodeContent.

methods.description: This method sets the stream Position at end of stream.

methods.name: SetContentFromText

params.name: stream
params.description: The text input. This input replaces any existing content. If the stream is empty, any current content is removed.

params.name: contentType
params.description: Content type/subtype of input. This parameter generates a Content-Type header.

params.name: encoding
params.description: The MIME content transfer encoding. This parameter generates a Content-Transfer-Encoding header. See also EncodeContent.

methods.description: If the NotesStream input is the result of NotesStream.WriteText, translation of the internal Unicode defaults to US-ASCII. To translate characters other than US-ASCII, append a charset parameter such as "charset=UTF-8" or "charset=Unicode-1-1" to the type/subtype. If Content-Type specifies "text" and the "charset" parameter specifies a known Internet encoding, and encoding is ENC_IDENTITY_8BIT or ENC_IDENTITY_BINARY, content storage is with the specified character set. Otherwise, content storage is attempted with US-ASCII. This method sets the stream Position at end of stream.

nodes.name: NotesMIMEHeader
nodes.description: Represents a header in a MIME (Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions) document.

props.name: HeaderName
props.description: Read-only. Name of a MIME header.

methods.name: AddValText

params.name: valueText
params.description: The text to be appended. If the header has no value, this text becomes the complete value.

params.name: charSet
params.description: Optional. A character set acceptable to RFC-2047.

returns.name: flag
returns.description: Boolean.

methods.description: If valueText contains only US-ASCII characters, no encoding occurs. If valueText contains non-US-ASCII characters and a character set is specified, encoding occurs according to RFC-2047. You must call NotesDocument.Save to commit this change.

methods.name: GetHeaderVal

params.name: folded
params.description: Optional. True formats for multi-line representation per the RFC-822 concept of folding. False (default) formats for single-line representation per the RFC-822 concept of unfolding.

params.name: decoded
params.description: Optional. True decodes any RFC-2047 encodings. False (default) retains any encodings. False is enforced if folded is True.

returns.name: String
returns.description: String. The header value.

methods.description: Folding terminates the header with a CRLF. Depending on length (1024 for X-Notes-Item headers and 78 for other headers), folding inserts CRLFs to break the header into multiple lines. Folding also inserts CRLFs after semicolons and commas. Unfolding removes all CRLFs.

methods.name: GetHeaderValAndParams

params.name: folded
params.description: Optional. True formats for multi-line representation per the RFC-822 concept of folding. False (default) formats for single-line representation per the RFC-822 concept of unfolding.

params.name: decoded
params.description: Optional. True decodes any RFC-2047 encodings. False (default) retains any encodings. False is enforced if folded is True.

returns.name: String
returns.description: String. The header value and any parameters.

methods.description: Folding terminates the header with a CRLF. Depending on length (1024 for X-Notes-Item headers and 78 for other headers), folding inserts CRLFs to break the header into multiple lines. Folding also inserts CRLFs after semicolons and commas. Unfolding removes all CRLFs.

methods.name: GetParamVal

params.name: paramName$
params.description: The parameter name.

params.name: folded
params.description: Optional. True formats for multi-line representation per the RFC-822 concept of folding. False (default) formats for single-line representation per the RFC-822 concept of unfolding.

returns.name: String
returns.description: String. The parameter value.

methods.description: The parameter value includes the delimiting quotation marks. Folding terminates the header with a CRLF. Depending on length (1024 for X-Notes-Item headers and 78 for other headers), folding inserts CRLFs to break the header into multiple lines. Folding also inserts CRLFs after semicolons and commas. Unfolding removes all CRLFs.

methods.name: Remove
methods.description: You must call NotesDocument.Save to commit this change.

methods.name: SetHeaderVal

params.name: headerValue$
params.description: The header value.

returns.name: flag
returns.description: Boolean.

methods.description: This method replaces any existing value for the header leaving the parameters intact. You must call NotesDocument.Save to commit this change.

methods.name: SetHeaderValAndParams

params.name: headerParamValue$
params.description: The header value and any parameters.

returns.name: flag
returns.description: Boolean.

methods.description: Any existing value for this header is replaced. Any existing parameters are replaced. You must call NotesDocument.Save to commit this change.

methods.name: SetParamVal

params.name: parameterName$
params.description: The name of the parameter.

params.name: parameterValue$
params.description: The parameter value.

returns.name: flag
returns.description: Boolean.

methods.description: The parameter value includes any delimiting quotation marks. You must call NotesDocument.Save to commit this change.

methods.name: FormatDocument

params.name: notesDatabase
params.type: The database in which to create the newsletter document
params.description: If you do not specify a notesDatabase, the document is created in the user's default mail database.

params.name: n%
params.description: A number indicating the document to render. Use 1 to indicate the first document in the collection, 2 to indicate the second document, and so on.

returns.name: notesDocument
returns.description: A document in notesDatabase, containing a rendering of the nth document in the collection. The rendering is placed into the Body item.

methods.description: If you want to save the rendered document, you must explicitly call the Save method on the NotesDocument. The document is saved to the notesDatabase you specify. If you want to mail the rendered document, you must explicitly call the Send method on the NotesDocument. The target database must have a default view or the following occurs: "Notes® error: Special database object cannot be located."

methods.name: FormatMsgWithDoclinks

params.name: notesDatabase
params.type: The database in which to create the newsletter document
params.description: If you do not specify a notesDatabase, the document is created in the user's default mail database.

returns.name: notesDocument
returns.description: A document that contains a link to each document in the newsletter's collection; also contains the name of notesDatabase and the text of the original query that produced the collection, if any.

methods.description: The Body item of the returned notesDocument contains the following: If you want to save the newsletter, you must explicitly call the Save method on the NotesDocument. The document is saved to the notesDatabase you specify. If you want to mail the newsletter, you must explicitly call the Send method on the NotesDocument.
