methods.description: The notes for design elements are those regulated by the following properties. This method sets these properties True or False as indicated by selectorValue.

methods.name: SelectAllFormatElements

params.name: selectorValue
params.description: Boolean.

methods.description: The notes for format elements are those regulated by the following properties. This method sets these properties True or False as indicated by selectorValue.

methods.name: SelectAllIndexElements

params.name: selectorValue
params.description: Boolean.

methods.description: The notes for index elements are those regulated by the following properties. This method sets these properties True or False as indicated by selectorValue.

methods.name: SelectAllNotes

params.name: selectorValue
params.description: Boolean.

methods.description: Selects or deselects all notes for inclusion in the collection.

nodes.name: NotesOutline
nodes.description: Represents an outline in a database.

props.name: Alias
props.description: Read-write. Alias name of the outline, used for programmatic access.
props.syntax: To get: alias$ = notesOutline .Alias To set: notesOutline .Alias = alias$

props.name: Comment
props.description: Read-write. A brief description of the outline.
props.syntax: To get: comment$ = notesOutline .Comment To set: notesOutline .Comment = comment$

props.name: Name
props.description: Read-write. Programmatic name for accessing an outline.
props.syntax: To get: name$ = notesOutline .Name To set: notesOutline .Name = name$

props.name: ParentDatabase
props.description: Read-only. The parent database that contains an Outline object.

methods.name: AddEntry

params.name: newEntry
params.description: The entry you want to add to the outline. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

params.name: refEntry
params.description: Optional. The reference entry before or after which you want to insert the new entry. If you omit this parameter the entry is added to the end of the outline. Specify to add the first entry to an empty outline.

params.name: moveAfter
params.description: Optional. Specify True to move the new entry after the reference entry, and False to move the entry before the reference entry.

params.name: AsChild
params.description: Optional. Specify True if you want the new entry to be added as a child to the reference entry, and False if you want the new entry to be added as a sibling entry. Default is False.

methods.description: You must call the Save method to save the change to the outline.
methods.syntax: Call notesOutline. AddEntry( newEntry [, refEntry ] [, moveAfter ] [, AsChild ] )

methods.name: CreateEntry

params.name: name$
params.description: The name of the entry you want to create.

params.name: refentry
params.description: Optional. The reference entry before or after which you want to insert the new entry. If you omit this parameter the entry is added to the end of the outline after the last top level entry.

params.name: addAfter
params.description: Optional. Specify True to place the new entry after the reference entry, and False to place the entry before the reference entry.

params.name: asChild
params.description: Optional. Specify True if you want the new entry to be added as a child to the reference entry, and False if you want the new entry to be added as a sibling entry. The default is False.

returns.name: notesOutlineEntry
returns.description: NotesOutlineEntry. The new entry that is automatically added to the outline.

methods.description: You must Save the outline or the effect of this method is lost when the program exits.
methods.syntax: Set notesOutlineEntry = notesOutline. CreateEntry( name$ [, refEntry ] [, addAfter ] [, asChild ] )

methods.name: CreateEntryFrom

params.name: entry
params.description: The entry to be copied.

params.name: refentry
params.description: Optional. The reference entry before or after which you want to insert the new entry. If you omit this parameter the entry is added to the end of the outline after the last top level entry.

params.name: addAfter
params.description: Optional. Specify True to place the new entry after the reference entry, and False to place the entry before the reference entry.

params.name: asChild
params.description: Optional. Specify True if you want the new entry to be added as a child to the reference entry, and False if you want the new entry to be added as a sibling entry. The default is False.

returns.name: notesOutlineEntry
returns.description: NotesOutlineEntry. The new outline entry.

methods.description: You must Save the outline or the effect of this method is lost when the program exits. Example
methods.syntax: Set notesOutlineEntry = notesOutline .CreateEntryFrom( entry [, refentry ] [, addAfter ][, asChild ] )

methods.name: GetChild

params.name: entry
params.description: The parent entry.

returns.name: notesOutlineEntry
returns.description: NotesOutlineEntry. The child of the specified entry, or nothing if the specified entry has no sub-entries.

methods.description: Returns the child of the specified entry.

methods.name: GetFirst

returns.name: notesOutlineEntry
returns.description: NotesOutlineEntry. The first entry of the outline, or nothing if no entry exists.

methods.description: Gets the first entry of an outline.

methods.name: GetLast

returns.name: notesOutlineEntry
returns.description: NotesOutlineEntry. The last entry of the outline, or nothing is no entry exists.

methods.description: Gets the last entry of an outline.

methods.name: GetNext

params.name: entry
params.description: The current entry in the outline. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesOutlineEntry
returns.description: NotesOutlineEntry. The entry following the specified entry in the outline, or nothing if no entries follow the specified entry.

methods.description: Given an entry in an outline, returns the entry immediately following it.

methods.name: GetNextSibling

params.name: entry
params.description: The current entry in the outline. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesOutlineEntry
returns.description: NotesOutlineEntry. The entry following the specified entry in the outline at the same level, or nothing if no sibling entries follow the specified entry.

methods.description: Given an entry in an outline, returns the entry immediately following it at the same level.

methods.name: GetParent

params.name: entry
params.description: The current entry in the outline.

returns.name: notesOutlineEntry
returns.description: NotesOutlineEntry. The parent entry of the specified entry in the outline, or nothing if the entry specified has no parent.

methods.description: Given a response entry in an outline, returns its parent entry.

methods.name: GetPrev

params.name: entry
params.description: The current entry in the outline. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesOutlineEntry
returns.description: NotesOutlineEntry. The entry preceding the specified entry in the outline, or nothing if no entry precedes the entry specified.

methods.description: Given an entry in an outline, returns the entry immediately preceding it.

methods.name: GetPrevSibling

params.name: entry
params.description: The current entry in the outline. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesOutlineEntry
returns.description: NotesOutlineEntry. The entry preceding the specified entry in the outline at the same level, or nothing if no sibling entry precedes the entry specified.

methods.description: Given an entry in an outline, returns the entry immediately preceding it at the same level.

methods.name: MoveEntry

params.name: currentEntry
params.description: The entry that needs to be moved within the outline. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

params.name: refEntry
params.description: The reference entry before or after which you want to insert the current entry.

params.name: moveAfter
params.description: Optional. Specify True to move the current entry after the reference entry, and False to move the current entry before the reference entry. Default is True.

params.name: AsChild
params.description: Optional. Specify True if you want the new entry to be added as a child to the reference entry, and False if you want the new entry to be added as a sibling entry. The default is False.

methods.description: You must Save the outline or the effect of this method is lost when the program exits. Moving an entry to the beginning of the outline makes it a top-level (level 0) entry.
methods.syntax: Call notesOutline .MoveEntry( currentEntry , refEntry [, moveAfter ] [, AsChild ] )

methods.name: RemoveEntry

params.name: currentEntry
params.description: The entry being removed. Cannot be Nothing.

methods.description: You must Save the outline or the effect of this method is lost when the program exits. An attempt to access entries removed with this method generates an error.

methods.name: Save
methods.description: You must save the outline before the program exits or the effect of a method that changes the outline is lost. These methods include AddEntry, CreateEntry, MoveEntry, and RemoveEntry.

nodes.name: NotesOutlineEntry
nodes.description: Represents an entry within an outline. For a detailed explanation of outline and outline entry, see Application Development with Domino® Designer.

props.name: Alias
props.description: Read-write. Alias name of the outline entry, used for programmatic access.
props.syntax: To get: alias$ = notesOutlineEntry .Alias To set: notesOutlineEntry .Alias = alias$

props.name: Database
props.description: Read-only. The database that is the resource link for an outline entry, or null.

props.name: Document
props.description: Read-only. The document that is the resource link for an outline entry, or null. You create a document resource link by performing a Copy as document link operation on a document and pasting it as the resource for an outline entry.

props.name: EntryClass
props.description: Read-only. Class of outline entry.

props.name: Formula
props.description: Read-only. Formula of an action outline, or an empty string.

props.name: FrameText
props.description: Read-write. The name of the target frame in which you want the entry to appear.
props.syntax: To get: frame$ = notesOutlineEntry .FrameText To set: notesOutlineEntry .FrameText = frame$

props.name: HasChildren
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether an entry contains child entries.

props.name: HideFormula
props.description: Read-write. A formula that determines when an entry is hidden, or an empty string.
props.syntax: To get: image$ = notesOutlineEntry .HideFormula To set: notesOutlineEntry .HideFormula = image$

props.name: ImagesText
props.description: Read-write. The name of the image file you would use to add an icon to your entry.
props.syntax: To get: image$ = notesOutlineEntry .ImagesText To set: notesOutlineEntry .ImagesText = image$

props.name: IsHidden
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether an entry is hidden in the current user context (read) or hides an entry in all contexts (write).
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesOutlineEntry .IsHidden To set: notesOutlineEntry .IsHidden = flag

props.name: IsHiddenFromNotes
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether an entry is hidden in the context of the Notes® client.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesOutlineEntry .IsHiddenFromNotes To set: notesOutlineEntry .IsHiddenFromNotes = flag

props.name: IsHiddenFromWeb
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether an entry is hidden in the context of a Web browser.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesOutlineEntry .IsHiddenFromWeb To set: notesOutlineEntry .IsHiddenFromWeb = flag

props.name: IsInThisDb
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether an entry refers to an element within the current database.

props.name: IsPrivate
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether an entry is specific to an individual.

props.name: KeepSelectionFocus
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether an entry keeps selection focus.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesOutlineEntry .KeepSelectionFocus To set: notesOutlineEntry .KeepSelectionFocus = flag

props.name: Label
props.description: Read-write Label for an entry.
props.syntax: To get: text$ = notesOutlineEntry .Label To set: notesOutlineEntry .Label = text$

props.name: Level
props.description: Read-only. Indicates the level of this entry.

props.name: NamedElement
props.description: Read-only. The named element referenced by an outline entry (or the alias name if available), or an empty string.

props.name: Parent
props.description: Read-only. The outline that contains an OutlineEntry object.

props.name: Type
props.description: Read-only. Type of outline entry.

props.name: URL
props.description: Read-only. The URL of an outline entry, or an empty string.

props.name: UseHideFormula
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether an entry is hidden when the hide formula is True.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesOutlineEntry .UseHideFormula To set: notesOutlineEntry .UseHideFormula = flag

props.name: View
props.description: Read-only. The view that is the resource link for an outline entry, or null.

methods.name: SetAction

params.name: formula$
params.description: A Domino® formula.

methods.description: This method sets the outline entry Type property to OUTLINE_TYPE_ACTION.

methods.name: SetNamedElement

params.name: notesDatabase
params.description: The database containing the named element.

params.name: elementName$
params.description: The name of a database element.

params.name: entryclass&
params.description: The entry class for the named element.

methods.description: This method sets the outline entry Type property to OUTLINE_TYPE_NAMEDELEMENT and the EntryClass property depending on the named element. See the EntryClass property.

methods.name: SetNoteLink

params.name: notesDatabase
params.description: The database that is the resource link.

params.name: notesView
params.description: The view that is the resource link.

params.name: notesDocument
params.description: The document that is the resource link.

params.name: Obj
params.description: The database, view, or document that is the resource link.

methods.description: Only one object should be specified: either the notesDatabase, the notesView, or the notesDocument. If you are specifying a notesView as the resource link for an outline entry, the notesDatabase parameter must be passed in as an empty object. If you are specifying a notesDocument, the notesDatabase and notesView parameters must be passed in as empty objects. This method sets the outline entry Type property to OUTLINE_TYPE_NOTELINK and the EntryClass property to OUTLINE_CLASS_DATABASE, OUTLINE_CLASS_DOCUMENT, or OUTLINE_CLASS_VIEW depending on the type of object passed to the method.
methods.syntax: flag = notesOutlineEntry .SetNoteLink( notesDatabase, notesView, notesDocument ) Call notesOutlineEntry .SetNoteLink( Obj )

methods.name: SetURL

params.name: url$
params.description: A URL.

methods.description: This method sets the outline entry Type property to OUTLINE_TYPE_URL.

nodes.name: NotesProperty
nodes.description: Represents a single property in the Composite Applications properties of a Domino® application. Properties are data transmitted to or from the Property Broker, which routes your application's communication with other components in a composite application. Properties are defined in a Wiring Properties design element.

props.name: Description
props.description: Read-only. Returns the description of the NotesProperty.

props.name: Isinput
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether this property is an input property.

props.name: Name
props.description: Read-only. Returns the name of the NotesProperty.

props.name: NameSpace
props.description: Returns the namespace of the datatype associated with the NotesProperty.

props.name: Title
props.description: Read-only. Returns the title of the NotesProperty.

props.name: Typename
props.description: Returns the type name of the NotesProperty.

props.name: Values
props.type: Variant array of type String, Integer, Real (Double), or NotesDateTime. Input accepts a variant or a homogenious array of type String, Integer, Real (Double) or NotesDateTime.
props.description: Read-write. Returns array of notesProperty values.
props.syntax: To get: array = notesProperty .Values To set: notesProperty.Values = variant notesProperty. Values = array

methods.name: Clear
methods.description: If the NotesProperty is an input property, this method will have no effect.

methods.name: Publish
methods.description: After setting the value of a NotesProperty property, you must call this method for the changes to take effect. If you do not call Publish, your changes are lost. ECL_FLAG_PROPERTY_PUT checks are performed on this method.

nodes.name: NotesPropertyBroker
nodes.description: Mediates communication between components of a composite application, allowing communication between multiple Notes® components, or between Notes® and Java™ components.

props.name: InputPropertyContext
props.description: Read-only. Returns array of input NotesProperties.

methods.name: ClearProperty

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the property which will be purged.

methods.description: If the specified NotesProperty is an input property, this method will have no effect. All NotesPropertyBroker methods are inactive when called by applications running on the Domino® server, or running on the Notes® 8 basic configuration without Notes® 8 standard configuration.

methods.name: GetProperty

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the property that will be returned.

methods.description: All NotesPropertyBroker methods are inactive when called by applications running on the Domino® server, or running on the Notes® 8 basic configuration without Notes® 8 standard configuration.

methods.name: GetPropertyValue

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the property whose value will be returned.

returns.name: Variant array of type String, Integer, Real (Double), or NotesDateTime.

methods.description: This method is only valid for input properties. Therefore, this method is only of use within the context of an executing action. The variant array must be homogenious. All NotesPropertyBroker methods are inactive when called by applications running on the Domino® server, or running on the Notes® 8 basic configuration without Notes® 8 standard configuration.

methods.name: HasProperty

params.name: Name
params.description: The property name.

methods.description: This method is valid for both input and output properties. All NotesPropertyBroker methods are inactive when called by applications running on the Domino® server, or running on the Notes® 8 basic configuration without Notes® 8 standard configuration.

methods.name: Publish
methods.description: After setting the value of a NotesPropertyBroker property, using the SetPropertyValue method, you must call this method for the changes to take effect. If you do not call Publish, your changes are lost.

methods.name: SetPropertyValue

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the property whose value will be set.

params.name: value
params.description: The new value of the property.

methods.description: The property must be defined by WSDL. If it does not exist, it will be created. Otherwise, the existing value will be replaced. Input properties cannot be set. All NotesPropertyBroker methods are inactive when called by applications running on the Domino® server, or running on the Notes® 8 basic configuration without Notes® 8 standard configuration.

nodes.name: NotesQueryResultsProcessor
nodes.description: Aggregates, computes, sorts, and formats collections of Documents.

props.name: MaxEntries
props.description: To prevent runaway processing during ExecuteToJSON or ExecuteToView, throws an error if the number of documents fetched across all input collections exceeds the MaxEntries limit.
props.syntax: To get: Dim maxentries as Longmaxentries = qrp.MaxEntries To get: Dim maxentries as Longmaxentries = qrp.MaxEntries To set: qrp.MaxEntries = <value>

props.name: TimeOutSec
props.description: To prevent runaway processing during ExecuteToJSON or ExecuteToView, throws an error if execution time exceeds the TimeOutSec limit.
props.syntax: To get: Dim timeoutsec Longtimeoutsec = qrp.TimeOutSec To get: Dim timeoutsec Longtimeoutsec = qrp.TimeOutSec To set: qrp.TimeOutSec = <value>

methods.name: AddCollection

params.name: Collection
params.description: A NotesDocumentCollection object or a NotesViewEntryCollection object.

params.name: ReferenceName
params.description: A unique name (to the QueryResultsProcessor instance) of the input collection. This name is used in returned entries when the origin of results is desired and in addFormula method calls to override the data used to create sorted column values.

methods.description: Adds a NotesDocumentCollection or NotesViewEntryCollection to the Documents being processed.

methods.name: AddColumn

params.name: Name
params.type: The unique programmatic name of the column within a QueryResults Processor instance
params.description: If there is no override using the addFormula method call, the name provided is treated as a field name in each database involved in the QueryResultsProcessor object.

params.name: Title
params.description: Optional. The display title of the column. Used only in generated views, the title is the UI column header.

params.name: SortOrder
params.description: Optional. A constant indicating the direction of the sort. Default is SORT_UNORDERED. Legal values are:SORT_UNORDEREDSORT_ASCENDINGSORT_DESCENDING

params.name: Hidden
params.description: Optional. Specify this to sort by a column value but not return it. If true, the column cannot have a SortOrder of SORT_UNORDERED and cannot have Categorized set to True.

params.name: Categorized
params.description: Optional. Categorized columns have a single entry returned for each unique value with entries containing that value nested under it. In JSON results, these nested entries are represented in arrays under each categorized unique value.

params.name: Col1
params.description: Col2categorized

params.name: @@avg(col3)
params.description: @@sum(col4)

params.name: Col5
params.description: Col6categorized

params.name: @@avg(col7)
params.description: Col8

methods.description: Creates a single column of values to be returned when QueryResultsProcessor is executed.
methods.syntax: Call notesQueryResultsProcessor.AddColumn( String Name, Optional String Title, Optional String Formula, Optional Integer SortOrder, Optional Boolean Hidden, Optional Boolean Categorized)

methods.name: AddDominoQuery

params.name: Query
params.type: created and ready-to-execute NotesDominoQuery class instance
params.description: See the documentation for NotesDominoQuery for details on its parameters.

params.name: QueryString
params.description: The Domino Query Language query to be executed. It can include substitution variables and all legal DQL syntax.

params.name: ReferenceName
params.description: A unique name (to the NotesQueryResultsProcessor instance) of the input set of documents. This name is used in returned entries when the origin of results is desired and in addFormula method calls to override the data used to create sorted column values.

methods.description: Executes and adds the output set of documents from a NotesDominoQuery to those included in NotesQueryResultsProcessor instance. All NotesDominoQuery parameters remain in force for the DQL query execution.
methods.syntax: Call notesQueryResultsProcessor.AddDominoQuery( NotesDominoQuery Query, String QueryString, String ReferenceName)

methods.name: AddFormula

params.name: Formula
params.description: Formula language string to be evaluated in order to supply the values for a sort column.

params.name: ColumnName
params.description: String value responding the programmatic name of the sort column whose values are to be generated using the formula language supplied.

params.name: ReferenceName
params.description: Used to specify the input collection names to which will use the formula language to generate sort column values. Wildcards may be specified to map to multiple input collection names.

methods.description: Provides Domino formula language to override the data used to generate values for a particular sort column and an input collection or set of collections. Since input collections can be created from different databases, design differences can be adjusted using addFormula to produce homogenous values in the output.
methods.syntax: Call notesQueryResultsProcessor.AddFormula(String Formula, String ColumnName, String ReferenceName)

methods.name: ExecuteToJSON
methods.description: Processes the input collections in the manner specified by the Sort Columns, overriding field values with formulas specified via addFormula calls, and returns JSON output to a NotesJSONNavigator object.
methods.syntax: Set jsonnavigatorobj = notesQueryResultsProcessor.ExecuteToJSON() The JSON syntax produced by NotesQueryResultsProcessor execution conforms to JSON RFC 8259. All results are output under the “StreamedResults” top element key. For categorized results, all nested details are output under the “Documents” key. Special keys “@nid” for NoteID and “@DbPath” are output so results can be acted upon on a document basis. Fields that are lists on documents (multiply-occurring) are output as JSON arrays of like type.

methods.name: ExecuteToView
methods.description: Results views created using the ExecuteToView method have the following distinctive characteristics. To open and manipulate results views using the HCL Notes® client or to write application code that utilizes it, it's important to understand these characteristics. Results views are created and persist in a database that you choose. Using a separate, non-production database is recommended. Doing so avoids unnecessary, routine database processing and also avoids user confusion over the views, which are not standard views. Domino processing of results views is otherwise typical.
methods.syntax: Set myview = qrp.ExecuteToView( Byval name As String, Optional Byval Expirehours As Long, Optional Readers as Variant, Optional DesignSrcDB As NotesDatabase, Optional byval DesignSrcViewName As String) As NotesView

nodes.name: NotesRegistration
nodes.description: Represents the creation or administration of an ID file.

props.name: AltOrgUnit
props.description: Read-write. Alternate names for the organizational unit to use when creating ID files.
props.syntax: To get: stringArray = notesRegistration.AltOrgUnit To set: notesRegistration.AltOrgUnit = stringArray

props.name: AltOrgUnitLang
props.description: Read-write. Alternate name language for the organizational unit to use when creating ID files.
props.syntax: To get: stringArray = notesRegistration.AltOrgUnitLang To set: notesRegistration.AltOrgUnitLang = stringArray

props.name: CertifierIDFile
props.description: Read-write. The complete file specification of the certifier ID to use when creating IDs.
props.syntax: To get: string$ = notesRegistration.CertifierIDFile To set: notesRegistration. CertifierIDFile = string$

props.name: CertifierName
props.description: Optionally set this property before calling CrossCertify, Recertify, RegisterNewCertifier, RegisterNewServer, or RegisterNewUser. If UseCertificateAuthority is true, this property must specify the name of a CA-enabled certifier. If you are working in a hosted environment and registering a user to a hosted organization, use this property to specify a certifier created for the hosted organization.
props.syntax: To get: string$ = notesRegistration.CertifierName To set: notesRegistration. CertifierName = string$

props.name: CreateMailDb
props.description: Set this property before calling RegisterNewUser.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesRegistration.CreateMailDb To set: notesRegistration.CreateMailDb = flag

props.name: EnforceUniqueShortName
props.description: Optionally set this property before calling RegisterNewUser.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesRegistration.EnforceUniqueShortName To set: notesRegistration.EnforceUniqueShortName = flag

props.name: Expiration
props.description: Read-write. The expiration date to use when creating ID files.
props.syntax: To get: date = notesRegistration.Expiration To set: notesRegistration.Expiration = date

props.name: GroupList
props.description: Optionally set this property before calling RegisterNewUser.
props.syntax: To get: stringArray = notesRegistration.GroupList To set: notesRegistration. GroupList = stringArray

props.name: IDType
props.description: Read-write. The type of ID file to create.
props.syntax: To get: type% = notesRegistration.IDType To set: notesRegistrationID .IDType = type%

props.name: IsNorthAmerican
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether an ID file is NorthAmerican.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesRegistration.IsNorthAmerican To set: notesRegistration.IsNorthAmerican = flag

props.name: IsRoamingUser
props.description: Optionally set this property before calling RegisterNewUser. If this property is True, you can apply the following properties: RoamingCleanupPeriod, RoamingCleanupSetting, RoamingServer, and RoamingSubdir.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesRegistration.IsRoamingUser To set: notesRegistration.IsRoamingUser = flag

props.name: MailACLManager
props.description: Optionally set this property before calling RegisterNewUser. The mail database owner may or may not be a Manager depending on MailOwnerAccess.
props.syntax: To get: string$ = notesRegistration.MailACLManager To set: notesRegistration. MailACLManager = string$

props.name: MailCreateFTIndex
props.description: Optionally set this property before calling RegisterNewUser.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesRegistration.MailCreateFTIndex To set: notesRegistration. MailCreateFTIndex = flag

props.name: MailInternetAddress
props.description: Optionally set this property before calling RegisterNewUser. If this property remains an empty string, the registration process generates an Internet name.
props.syntax: To get: string$ = notesRegistration.MailInternetAddress To set: notesRegistration. MailInternetAddress = string$

props.name: MailOwnerAccess
props.description: Optionally set this property before calling RegisterNewUser.
props.syntax: To get: integer% = notesRegistration.MailOwnerAccess To set: notesRegistration. MailOwnerAccess = integer%

props.name: MailQuotaSizeLimit
props.description: Optionally set this property before calling RegisterNewUser. A value of 0 (default) sets no size limit.
props.syntax: To get: long& = notesRegistration.MailQuotaSizeLimit To set: notesRegistration. MailQuotaSizeLimit = long&

props.name: MailQuotaWarningThreshold
props.description: Optionally set this property before calling RegisterNewUser. A value of 0 (default) sets no warning threshold.
props.syntax: To get: long& = notesRegistration.MailQuotaWarningThreshold To set: notesRegistration. MailQuotaWarningThreshold = long&

props.name: MailReplicaServers
props.description: Optionally set this property before calling RegisterNewUser. This property applies only to clustered servers.
props.syntax: To get: variant = notesRegistration.MailReplicaServers To set: notesRegistration. MailReplicaServers = variant

props.name: MailSystem
props.description: Optionally set this property before calling RegisterNewUser.
props.syntax: To get: integer% = notesRegistration.MailSystem To set: notesRegistration. MailSystem = integer%

props.name: MailTemplateName
props.description: Optionally set this property to the relative path/filename of the template on the server where the user's primary mail database is to be created before calling RegisterNewUser. If this property remains an empty string, a standard template is used. For Release 8 the standard template is MAIL8.NTF.
props.syntax: To get: string$ = notesRegistration.MailTemplateName To set: notesRegistration. MailTemplateName = string$

props.name: MinPasswordLength
props.description: Read-write. The strength assigned to a password in an ID file.
props.syntax: To get: strength% = notesRegistration.MinPasswordLength To set: notesRegistrationID .MinPasswordLength = strength%

props.name: NoIDFile
props.description: Optionally set this property before calling RegisterNewUser. If you set this property True, leave StoreIDInAddressBook False.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesRegistration.NoIDFile To set: notesRegistration. NoIDFile = flag

props.name: OrgUnit
props.description: Read-write. The organizational unit to use when creating ID files.
props.syntax: To get: string$ = notesRegistration.OrgUnit To set: notesRegistration.OrgUnit = string$

props.name: PolicyName
props.description: Optionally set this property before calling RegisterNewUser.
props.syntax: To get: string$ = notesRegistration.PolicyName To set: notesRegistration. PolicyName = string$

props.name: RegistrationLog
props.description: Read-write. The log file to use when creating IDs.
props.syntax: To get: string$ = notesRegistration.RegistrationLog To set: notesRegistration.RegistrationLog = string$

props.name: RegistrationServer
props.description: Read-write. The server to use when creating IDs and performing other registration functions.
props.syntax: To get: string$ = notesRegistration.RegistrationServer To set: notesRegistration.RegistrationServer = string$

props.name: RoamingCleanupPeriod
props.description: Optionally set this property before calling RegisterNewUser. IsRoamingUser must be True. RoamingCleanupSetting must be REG_ROAMING_CLEANUP_EVERY_NDAYS.
props.syntax: To get: integer% = notesRegistration.RoamingCleanupPeriod To set: notesRegistration. RoamingCleanupPeriod = integer%

props.name: RoamingCleanupSetting
props.description: Optionally set this property before calling RegisterNewUser. IsRoamingUser must be True.
props.syntax: To get: integer% = notesRegistration.RoamingCleanupSetting To set: notesRegistration. RoamingCleanupSetting = integer%

props.name: RoamingServer
props.description: Optionally set this property before calling RegisterNewUser. IsRoamingUser must be True. If this property is an empty string, the roaming server defaults to the user's mail server.
props.syntax: To get: string$ = notesRegistration.RoamingServer To set: notesRegistration. RoamingServer = string$

props.name: RoamingSubdir
props.description: Optionally set this property before calling RegisterNewUser. IsRoamingUser must be True.
props.syntax: To get: string$ = notesRegistration.RoamingSubdir To set: notesRegistration. RoamingSubdir = string$

props.name: ShortName
props.description: Optionally set this property before calling RegisterNewUser. By default the short name is the first letter of the first name followed by the last name.
props.syntax: To get: string$ = notesRegistration.ShortName To set: notesRegistration. ShortName = string$

props.name: StoreIDInAddressBook
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether the ID file is stored in the server's Domino® Directory.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesRegistration.StoreIDInAddressBook To set: notesRegistration.StoreIDInAddressBook = flag

props.name: StoreIDInMailfile
props.description: Optionally set this property before calling RegisterNewUser. This property applies only to Domino® Web Access and allows Notes® users to read their encrypted mail while using Domino® Web Access. The MailSystem property must be set to REG_MAILSYSTEM_INOTES (3) prior to calling RegisterNewUser or the Notes® ID will not be stored in the user's mail file.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesRegistration.StoreIDInMailfile To set: notesRegistration.StoreIDInMailfile = flag

props.name: SynchInternetPassword
props.description: Optionally set this property before calling RegisterNewUser. The Internet password is in the user's Person document in the Domino® Directory. If the user changes the password for the Notes® client ID, the Internet password automatically (but not immediately) changes to match it.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesRegistration.SynchInternetPassword To set: notesRegistration. SynchInternetPassword = flag

props.name: UpdateAddressBook
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether the server entry in the Domino® Directory is updated when the ID file is created.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesRegistration.UpdateAddressBook To set: notesRegistration.UpdateAddressBook = flag

props.name: UseCertificateAuthority
props.description: Set this property before calling CrossCertify, Recertify, RegisterNewCertifier, RegisterNewServer, or RegisterNewUser. If this property is True, the certifier ID and password are not needed for registration. Registration will be carried out by the Certificate Authority listed by the CertifierName property of the NotesRegistration class. The signer of the agent must be listed as an RA (Registration Authority) to use the Certificate Authority if the LotusScript® code is run in a notes client. If the code is run on the server (as by a scheduled agent) then the server that the code is scheduled on must be listed as an RA to use the CA. This is true even if the server itself is the CA.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesRegistration.UseCertificateAuthority To set: notesRegistration. UseCertificateAuthority = flag

This method occurs automatically with RegisterNewCertifier if UpdateAddressBook is True.

methods.description: This method requires that CertifierIDFile be set. Expiration defaults to 2 years from now. This method acts on the Domino® Directory (names.nsf) for the server specified by RegistrationServer, or names.nsf in the local Domino® data directory if this property is not set.

methods.name: RegisterNewCertifier

params.name: organization$
params.description: The organization to which the new certifier ID belongs.

params.name: idfile$
params.description: The ID file to be created; specify the full path; for example, c:\notes\data\cert.id.

params.description: A password for the certifier ID file.

params.name: country$
params.description: A value for the country field in the Domino® Directory record.

methods.description: Before calling this method, set CertifierIDFile, Expiration, IDType, and MinPasswordLength. Expiration defaults to 100 years from now for organizations and organizational units, and cannot be changed for organizations. Set UpdateAddressBook True to add a Certifier record to the Domino® Directory. Set StoreIDInAddressBook True to attach the ID to the Domino® Directory record. These operations can be performed separately with AddCertifierToAddressBook. Set OrgUnit to add an organizational unit to the ID name.
methods.syntax: variant = notesRegistration.RegisterNewCertifier( organization$, idfile$ [, certpw$ [, country$ ]] )

Set UpdateAddressBook True to add a Server record to the Domino® Directory. Set StoreIDInAddressBook True to attach the ID to the Domino® Directory record. These operations can be performed separately with AddServerToAddressBook. Set OrgUnit to add an organizational unit to the ID name.
methods.syntax: variant = notesRegistration.RegisterNewServer( server$, idfile$, domain$ [, servpw$ [, certpw$ [, location$ [, comment$ [, network$ [, adminname$ [, title$]]]]]]] )

params.name: lastname$

params.name: idfile$
params.description: The ID file to be created; specify the complete path, for example, c:\notes\data\user.id.

params.name: mailserver$
params.description: The canonical name of the server containing the user's mail file.

params.name: firstname$
params.description: A first name for the user.

params.name: middle$
params.description: A middle initial for the user.

params.name: certpw$
params.description: The password of the certifier ID file.

params.name: location$
params.description: A value for the location field in the Domino® Directory record.

params.name: comment$
params.description: A value for the comment field in the Domino® Directory record.

params.name: maildbpath$
params.description: The path of the user's mail file relative to the mail directory; for example, mail\jones.nsf.

params.name: fwddomain$
params.description: The forwarding domain for the user's mail file.

params.name: NOTES_FULL _CLIENT (176)

Set UpdateAddressBook True to add a Server record to the Domino® Directory. Set StoreIDInAddressBook True to attach the ID to the Domino® Directory record. These operations can be performed separately with AddUserToAddressBook. Set OrgUnit to add an organizational unit to the ID name. Optionally set AltOrgUnit and AltOrgUnitLang.
methods.syntax: variant = notesRegistration.RegisterNewUser( lastname$ , idfile$ , mailserver$ [, firstname$ [, middle$ [, certpw$ [, location$ [, comment$ [ , maildbpath$ [ , fwddomain$ [ , userpw$ [, usertype% [, altname [ , altnamelang ]]]]]]]]]]] )

nodes.name: NotesReplication
nodes.description: Represents the replication settings of a database.

props.name: Abstract
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether large documents should be truncated and attachments removed during replication.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesReplication.Abstract To set: notesReplication.Abstract = flag

props.name: CutoffDelete
props.description: After setting this property, you must call Save for the changes to take effect. If you do not call Save, your changes are lost.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesReplication.CutoffDelete To set: notesReplication.CutoffDelete = flag

props.name: CutoffInterval
props.description: After setting this property, you must call Save for the changes to take effect. If you do not call Save, your changes are lost.
props.syntax: To get: interval = notesReplication.CutoffInterval To set: notesReplication.CutoffInterval = interval

props.name: Disabled
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether replication is disabled.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesReplication.Disabled To set: notesReplication.Disabled = flag

props.name: DontSendLocalSecurityUpdates
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether local security (encryption) updates are sent.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesReplication.DontSendLocalSecurityUpdates To set: notesReplication.DontSendLocalSecurityUpdates = flag

props.name: IgnoreDeletes
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether outgoing deletions should not be replicated to other databases.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesReplication.IgnoreDeletes To set: notesReplication.IgnoreDeletes = flag

props.name: IgnoreDestDeletes
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether incoming deletions should not be replicated to the current database.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesReplication.IgnoreDestDeletes To set: notesReplication.IgnoreDestDeletes = flag

props.name: Priority
props.description: Read-write. Replication priority.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesReplication. Priority To set: notesReplication.Priority = flag

methods.name: ClearHistory
methods.description: After calling this method, you must call Save for the changes to take effect. If you do not call Save, your changes are lost.

methods.name: GetEntries

returns.name: notesReplicationEntryArray
returns.description: Array of type NotesReplicationEntry.

methods.description: A replication entry describes the replication settings for a pair of servers, of which one is the source and the other is the destination.

methods.name: GetEntry

params.name: source$
params.description: Name of the source server ("Receives from").

params.name: destination$
params.description: Name of the destination server ("When computer").

params.name: createflag
params.description: Optional.

returns.name: notesReplicationEntry
returns.description: NotesReplicationEntry. The replication entry if it exists or the create flag is True, or Nothing.

methods.description: A replication entry describes the replication settings for a pair of servers, of which one is the source and the other is the destination. The source and destination names serve as strings to identify the replication entry and must be specified exactly as they appear. A hyphen (-) means any server. Creating an entry for a new destination where the source is not any server results in the creation of an additional entry for that destination whose source is any server.
methods.syntax: Set notesReplicationEntry = notesReplication.GetEntry( source$ , destination$ [ , createflag ] )

methods.name: Reset
methods.description: After calling this method, you must call Save for the changes to take effect. If you do not call Save, your changes are lost.

methods.name: Save
methods.description: If you don't call the Save method before closing the database, any changes to the replication properties are lost.

nodes.name: NotesReplicationEntry
nodes.description: Represents the replication settings for a pair of servers in a database.

props.name: Destination
props.description: Read-only. Name of the destination server.

props.name: Formula
props.description: Read-write. The replication formula.
props.syntax: To get: formula$ = notesReplicationEntry .Formula To set: notesReplicationEntry .Formula = formula$

props.name: IsIncludeACL
props.description: Read-write. Includes the ACL in replications.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesReplicationEntry .IsIncludeACL To set: notesReplicationEntry .IsIncludeACL = flag

props.name: IsIncludeAgents
props.description: Read-write. Includes agents in replications.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesReplicationEntry .IsIncludeAgents To set: notesReplicationEntry .IsIncludeAgents = flag

props.name: IsIncludeDocuments
props.description: Read-write. Includes documents in replications.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesReplicationEntry .IsIncludeDocuments To set: notesReplicationEntry .IsIncludeDocuments = flag

props.name: IsIncludeForms
props.description: Read-write. Includes forms in replications.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesReplicationEntry .IsIncludeForms To set: notesReplicationEntry .IsIncludeForms = flag

props.name: IsIncludeFormulas
props.description: Read-write. Includes the replication formula in replications.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesReplicationEntry .IsIncludeFormulas To set: notesReplicationEntry .IsIncludeFormulas = flag

props.name: Source
props.description: Read-only. The name of the source server.

props.name: Views
props.description: Read-write. The views in the source database that are replicated when replicating a subset of views and folders.
props.syntax: To get: views$ = notesReplicationEntry .Views To set: notesReplicationEntry .Views = views$

methods.name: Remove
methods.description: Removes the replication entry from the database.

methods.name: Save
methods.description: If you don't call the Save method before closing the database, any changes to the replication entry are lost.

methods.description: To get the style, use HotSpotTextStyle.

nodes.name: NotesRichTextItem
nodes.description: Represents an item of type rich text.

props.name: EmbeddedObjects
props.description: Read-only. All the embedded objects, object links, and file attachments contained in a rich text item.

methods.name: New

params.name: notesDocument
params.description: The document on which to create a new rich text item.

params.name: name$
params.description: The name of the new rich text item.

methods.description: Given a document, New creates a rich text item on the document, with the name you specify.
methods.syntax: Dim variableName As New NotesRichTextItem( notesDocument , name$ ) Set notesRichTextItem = New NotesRichTextItem( notesDocument , name$ )

methods.name: AddPageBreak

params.name: notesRichTextParagraphStyle
params.description: NotesRichTextParagraphStyle. Starts the new page with the specified paragraph style.

methods.description: By default the insertion occurs at the end of the item. You can change the insertion point with BeginInsert and EndInsert. This method may be called with or without the RichTextParagraphStyle object as a parameter. If the optional parameter is supplied, the new page begins with the paragraph style. In an open document in edit mode (the NotesDocument via NotesUIDocument.Document), changes made to rich text will not appear on screen immediately as they would with fields of other types. There is no method to cause this update to occur. You must close and reopen the document to see changes.

methods.name: AppendParagraphStyle

params.name: notesRichTextParagraphStyle
params.description: The paragraph style to be appended.

methods.description: By default the insertion occurs at the end of the item. You can change the insertion point with BeginInsert and EndInsert. In an open document in edit mode (the NotesDocument via NotesUIDocument.Document), changes made to rich text will not appear on screen immediately as they would with fields of other types. There is no method to cause this update to occur. You must close and reopen the document to see changes.

methods.name: AppendTable

params.name: rows%
params.description: Number of rows in the table.

params.name: columns%
params.description: Number of columns in the table.

params.name: labels
params.description: Optional. Text of labels for a tabbed table. The number of array elements must equal the number of rows. Omitting this parameter appends a basic table. Including this parameter appends a tabbed table.

params.name: leftMargin&
params.description: Optional. Left margin of the table in twips. Defaults to 1440. The following constants are available:

params.name: rtpsStyleArray
params.description: Optional. Creates a table with fixed-width columns and style attributes as specified. Omitting this parameter creates an auto-width table. The array must contain one element for each column in the table in sequence. Explicitly set the first line left margin and left margin, which control the start of text relative to the start of the column, and the right margin, which controls column width.

methods.description: By default the insertion occurs at the end of the item. You can change the insertion point with BeginInsert and EndInsert. The NotesRichTextTable class represents a table. In an open document in edit mode (the NotesDocument via NotesUIDocument.Document), changes made to rich text will not appear on screen immediately as they would with fields of other types. There is no method to cause this update to occur. You must close and reopen the document to see changes.

methods.name: BeginInsert

params.name: element
params.type: NotesEmbeddedObject, NotesRichTextDocLink, NotesRichTextNavigator, NotesRichTextRange, NotesRichTextSection, or NotesRichTextTable
params.description: The element. A NotesRichTextNavigator element means the element at the current position of the navigator. Other elements mean the specified element.

params.name: after
params.description: Optional.

methods.description: In an open document in edit mode (the NotesDocument via NotesUIDocument.Document), changes made to rich text will not appear on screen immediately as they would with fields of other types. There is no method to cause this update to occur. You must close and reopen the document to see changes.

methods.name: BeginSection

params.name: title
params.description: The title of the section.

params.name: titleStyle
params.description: Optional. The style of the title of the section.

params.name: barColor
params.description: Optional. The color of the section bar.

params.name: expand
params.description: Optional.

methods.description: By default the insertion occurs at the end of the item. You can change the insertion point with BeginInsert and EndInsert. You cannot create a section containing existing rich text. You must start the section with BeginSection, append rich text, and end the section with EndSection. In an open document in edit mode (the NotesDocument via NotesUIDocument.Document), changes made to rich text will not appear on screen immediately as they would with fields of other types. There is no method to cause this update to occur. You must close and reopen the document to see changes.

methods.name: Compact
methods.description: In rare cases, editing a rich text item may introduce extraneous space. This method eliminates any extraneous space. This method invalidates the positions of navigators and is best used at the end of editing. In an open document in edit mode (the NotesDocument via NotesUIDocument.Document), changes made to rich text will not appear on screen immediately as they would with fields of other types. There is no method to cause this update to occur. You must close and reopen the document to see changes.

methods.name: Update
methods.description: Operations on a rich text item are queued for efficiency. The order and time of completion are not predictable. Use this method to ensure that processing is complete at a certain point. This method is useful when interacting with the front-end classes. In an open document in edit mode (the NotesDocument via NotesUIDocument.Document), changes made to rich text will not appear on screen immediately as they would with fields of other types. There is no method to cause this update to occur. You must close and reopen the document to see changes.

methods.description: For purposes of navigation, this method finds an element of type RTELEM_TYPE_TEXTRUN. All navigation markers in the RTitem, including ranges, are invalidated after a FindAndReplace operation. nodes.description: Processes input XML as a series of events using a SAX (Simple API for XML) parser.

events.name: SAX_Characters
events.description: Signals the occurrence of text in the input XML.

events.name: SAX_EndDocument
events.description: Signals the end of the input XML.

events.name: SAX_EndElement
events.description: Signals the end of the specified element in the input XML.

events.name: SAX_Error
events.description: Signals that an error occurred when processing the input XML.

events.name: SAX_FatalError
events.description: Signals that a fatal error occurred when processing the input XML.

events.name: SAX_IgnorableWhiteSpace
events.description: Signals ignorable white space found in the input XML.

events.name: SAX_NotationDecl
events.description: Signals a notation declaration found in the input XML. Used for special handling of non-XML documents.

events.name: SAX_ProcessingInstruction
events.description: Signals a processing instruction found in the input XML.

events.name: SAX_ResolveEntity
events.description: Signals an entity was found.

events.name: SAX_StartDocument
events.description: Signals the beginning of the input XML.

events.name: SAX_StartElement
events.description: Signals the start of the specified element in the input XML.

events.name: SAX_UnparsedEntityDecl
events.description: Signals an unparsed external entity declaration found in the input XML. Used for special handling of non-XML documents.

events.name: SAX_Warning
events.description: Signals that a warning occurred when processing the input XML.

nodes.name: NotesSession
nodes.description: Represents the environment of the current script, providing access to environment variables, Address Books, information about the current user, and information about the current Notes® platform and release number.

props.name: AddressBooks
props.description: Read-only. The Domino® Directories and Personal Address Books, including directory catalogs, known to the current session.

props.name: CommonUserName
props.description: Read-only. The common name portion of the current user's name.

props.name: ConvertMime
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether items of type MIME_PART are converted to rich text upon NotesDocument instantiation.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesSession.ConvertMIME To set: notesSession.ConvertMIME = flag

props.name: CurrentAgent
props.description: Read-only. The agent that's currently running.

props.name: CurrentDatabase
props.description: Read-only. The database in which the current script resides. This database may or may not be open.

props.name: DocumentContext
props.description: Read-only. The in-memory document when an agent starts.

props.name: EffectiveUserName
props.description: The identity of the person under whose identity the program is running depends on whether you are running an agent, an XPage, a servlet, or a stand alone program. For an agent, selecting 'run as web user' will make this property use the identity of the logged in web user. If 'run as web user' is not selected, this property will use the identity of the agent signer. For everything else the identity is the identity established by the Session or automatically assigned by the environment.

props.name: HttpURL
props.description: Read-only. The Domino® URL of a session when HTTP protocols are in effect.

props.name: International
props.description: Read-only. The international (regional) settings for your operating environment.

props.name: IsOnServer
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether a script is running on a server.

props.name: LastExitStatus
props.description: Read-only. The exit status code returned by the Agent Manager the last time the current agent ran.

props.name: LastRun
props.description: Read-only. The date when the current agent was last executed.

props.name: NotesBuildVersion
props.description: Read-only. The Notes® build version in which a selected document resides.

props.name: NotesURL
props.description: Read-only. The Domino® URL of a session when Notes® protocols are in effect.

props.name: NotesVersion
props.description: Read-only. The release of Notes® in which the current script is running.

props.name: OrgDirectoryPath
props.description: Read-only. The default directory of the current user in a hosted organization.

props.name: Platform
props.description: Read-only. The name of the platform on which the session is running.

props.name: SavedData
props.description: Read-only. A document that an agent script uses to store information in between invocations. The script can use the information in this document the next time the script runs.

props.name: ServerName
props.description: Read-only. The full name of the server that the session is running on.

props.syntax: To get: Set notesDatabase = notesSession.URLDatabase Example

props.name: UserGroupNameList
props.description: Read-only. The groups to which the current user belongs.

props.name: UserName
props.description: Read-only. The current user's name.

props.name: UserNameList
props.description: Read-only. The name of the user or server that created the session, and the alternate name if it exists.

props.name: UserNameObject
props.description: Read-only. The name, in the form of a NotesName object, of the user or server that created the session.

methods.name: New
methods.description: To access the current session, use New.
methods.syntax: Dim variableName As New NotesSession Set notesSession = New NotesSession To access the current session from a NotesDatabase object, use the Parent property in NotesDatabase. To access the current session through COM, see "Creating a session object" and "Initializing a session."Examples To access the current session through OLE, see "Using OLE."

methods.name: Evaluate

params.name: formula$
params.description: The formula.

params.name: doc
params.description: The formula context. Cannot be null.

returns.name: variant
returns.description: The result of the evaluation. A scalar result is returned.

methods.description: The formula takes the field from the specified document. @Functions that affect the user interface do not work in the Evaluate method. These include: @Command, @DbManager, @DbName, @DbTitle, @DDEExecute, @DDEInitiate, @DDEPoke, @DDETerminate, @DialogBox, @PickList, @PostedCommand, @Prompt, and @ViewTitle. You cannot change a document with the Evaluate method; you can only get a result. To change a document, write the result to the document with a method such as NotesDocument.ReplaceItemValue.

methods.name: FileOpBegin

params.name: filePath$
params.description: The path to the file in the browser filesystem

params.name: fileExists
params.description: Whether or not the file exists. If False and the file exists, or True and the file is missing, this method throws an error.

methods.description: This method is used in Nomad Web when calling COM from LotusScript where the COM application will need access to a file contained within Notes. When you have a file in the browser virtual file system(like a file obtained via NotesEmbeddedObject.extractfile), FileOpBegin will export the file to a temporary location on your machine's physical drive, and this file will automatically be used when you pass the virtual file to COM commands. FileOpEnd must be called later if the file updates should be saved to the browser file system.

methods.name: FileOpEnd

params.name: filePath$
params.description: The path to the file in the browser filesystem.

methods.description: This method is used in Nomad Web when calling COM from LotusScript where the COM application will need access to a file contained within Notes. When you have a file in the browser virtual file system(like a file obtained via NotesEmbeddedObject.extractfile), FileOpBegin will export the file to a temporary location on your machine's physical drive, and this file will automatically be used when you pass the virtual file to COM commands. Calling FileOpEnd will copy the file from the temporary location back to the browser file system.

methods.name: FreeResourceSearch

params.name: Start
params.description: The start time of resource.

params.name: End
params.description: The end time of resource.

params.name: Site$
params.description: Site name information.

params.name: Maxresults
params.description: The maximum number of results you want returned from this search. Default is set to 1.

params.name: User$
params.description: Optional. The user who is searching for the resource.The default is the current user.

params.name: Mincapacity%
params.description: Option. The minimum capacity limit of the resource. Set this to 0 to ignore the capacity limit. The default is 0.

params.name: Apptunid$
params.description: Optional. The UNID for a specific appointment. If you search a resource for a reschedule, this parameter is useful. The default is NULL.

params.name: Server$
params.description: Optional. The name of the server which includes the directory and busy-time for your search. The default is "".

params.name: Outputversion
params.description: Optional. Function version number. For example, AVAILABLERESOURCESLOOKUP_VERSION_0(0)

returns.name: Variant
returns.description: Array of strings. The format of each string is: ResourceName;Capacity;Email. This represents a sample string: CN=Room 2001/O=Boston Building 1;50;bos1-room2001@swg.usma.ibm.com.

methods.description: Searches for free resource slots for calendaring and scheduling.
methods.syntax: Set variant = NotesSession.FreeResourceSearch( Start , End , Site$, Type%, Maxresults, [User$], [Mincapacity], [Apptunid$], [Server$], [Outputversion])

methods.name: FreeTimeSearch

params.name: window
params.description: Starting and ending times within which to search for a match. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

params.name: duration
params.description: The length in minutes of the free-time interval you want to schedule.

params.name: names
params.type: String or an array of strings
params.description: The name or names of the people or groups whose free time you want to schedule. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

params.name: firstfit
params.description: True if you just want the first matching date range. False if you want all matching times. Defaults to False.

returns.name: notesDateRange
returns.description: Array of NotesDateRange objects, or an array of empty objects if there are no matches.

methods.description: Searches for free time slots for calendaring and scheduling.
methods.syntax: Set notesDateRange = notesSession.FreeTimeSearch( window , duration , names [ , firstfit ] )

params.name: maildatabase
