returns.name: NotesCalendar
returns.description: The newly created NotesCalendar object.

methods.description: Creates a new NotesCalendar object.

methods.name: GetDatabase

params.name: server$
params.description: The name of the server on which the database resides. Use an empty string ("") to indicate a database on the current computer: if the script runs on the workstation, the empty string indicates a local database; if the script runs on a server, it indicates a database on that server.

params.name: dbfile$
params.description: The file name and location of the database within the Notes® data directory. Use a full path name if the database is not within the Notes® data directory. Use empty strings for both dbfile$ and server$ if you want to create a Database object not yet associated with a database.

params.name: createonfail
params.description: Optional. Specify True (default) to create a Database object even if the specified database cannot be opened. Specify False to return Nothing (the Database object is not created) if the database cannot be opened.

returns.name: notesDatabase
returns.description: A NotesDatabase object that can be used to access the database you've specified, or Nothing if the database cannot be opened and createonfail is False. If the database cannot be opened and createonfail is True or omitted, IsOpen is false for the NotesDatabase object.

methods.description: Creates a NotesDatabase object that represents the database located at the server and file name you specify, and opens the database, if possible.
methods.syntax: Set notesDatabase = notesSession.GetDatabase( server$ , dbfile$ [, createonfail ] )

methods.name: GetDbDirectory

params.name: serverName$
params.description: The name of the server whose database files you want to navigate. Use an empty string ("") to indicate the current computer.

returns.name: notesDbDirectory
returns.description: The directory on the server you specified.

methods.description: Creates a new NotesDbDirectory object using the name of the server you want to access. When used with OLE automation, this method allows you to create a NotesDbDirectory object without using New.

methods.name: GetDirectory

params.name: serverName$
params.description: Optional. The name of the server whose database files you want to navigate. Use an empty string ("") to indicate the current computer. If no server name is specified, the local directory as indicated by the UseContextServer property is used.

returns.name: notesDirectory
returns.description: The directory on the server you specified.

methods.description: If this method is run on a server, and the serverName is the name of a different server, the current server must have a Trusted relationship with the specified server.

methods.name: GetEnvironmentString

params.name: name$
params.description: The name of the environment variable to get.

params.name: system
params.description: Optional. If True, the method uses the exact name$ of the environment variable. If False, the method prepends "$" to the name$ before retrieving its value. If you omit this parameter it defaults to False.

returns.name: String
returns.description: String. The value of the environment variable.

methods.description: If the script runs on a workstation, this method retrieves the environment variable from the current user's notes.ini (or Notes® Preferences) file. If the script runs on a server, this method retrieves the environment variable from the server's notes.ini file, subject to administrative restrictions (see "Restricting agents on servers" in Administration Help or Administering the Domino® System).

methods.name: GetEnvironmentValue

params.name: name$
params.description: The name of the environment variable to get.

params.name: system
params.description: Optional. If True, the method uses the exact name$ of the environment variable. If False, the method prepends "$" to the name$ before retrieving its value. If you omit this parameter, it defaults to False.

returns.name: valueV
returns.description: Variant. The value of the environment variable.

methods.description: User must be listed in the server document/agent manager section in order to access environment variables (notes.ini file) through LotusScript® or Java™. If the script runs on a workstation, this method retrieves the environment variable from the current user's notes.ini (or Notes® Preferences) file. If the script runs on a server, this method retrieves the environment variable from the server's notes.ini file, subject to administrative restrictions. For more information, see "Restricting agents on servers" in Administration Help or Administering the Domino® System.

methods.name: GetOIDCAccessToken

params.name: server$
params.description: Name of Domino Server to contact.

params.name: Clientid$
params.description: The client ID for your OAuth application.

params.name: issuer$
params.description: Your issuer (Domino OIDC provider if empty string).

params.name: resource$
params.description: Requested resource (First configured audience for client if empty string)

params.name: scope$
params.description: Requested scopes (Configured scopes for client if empty string)

returns.name: String
returns.description: String. The access token.

methods.description: It is used to acquire an access token from a Domino OIDC Provider for the current user as identified and authenticated by their ID file.
methods.syntax: token$ = session.GetOIDCAccessToken(server$, clientid$, issuer$, resource$, scope$)

methods.name: GetPropertyBroker

returns.name: propertybroker
returns.description: NotesPropertyBroker. The property broker associated with the current composite application.

methods.description: This method is inactive when called by applications running on Domino® servers, or running on clients with Notes® 8 basic configuration without Notes® 8 standard configuration. ECL_FLAG_PROPERTY_GET checks are performed on this method.

methods.name: GetUserPolicySettings

params.name: server$
params.description: The name of the server containing the policy. An empty string ("") means the local computer.

params.name: name$
params.description: The name of the effective user of the policy. The name must be fully qualified.

params.name: type%
params.description: The type of policy.

params.name: explicitPolicy$
params.description: Optional. The policy namespace, with slash (/) prefix, that contains the settings. If this parameter is specified, name$ is not used.

params.name: reserved$
params.description: Optional. Not used.

returns.name: notesDocument
returns.description: NotesDocument. A read-only document that represents the effective or explicit policy. Returns Nothing if the specified policy is not available.

methods.description: See "Policy Documents" in Domino® Administrator Help.

methods.name: HashPassword

params.name: password$
params.description: The string to be hashed.

returns.name: String
returns.description: String. The hashed string.

methods.description: A field of type "Password" is not secure. Even though it displays as asterisks, its value can be read programmatically or by looking at the field in the Properties box. Use HashPassword to convert such a value to gibberish. Use VerifyPassword to examine a hashed value.

methods.name: Initialize

params.name: password$
params.description: Optional. The password must match the user ID password. If you do not specify a password, you will be prompted for a password as required and as the software permits. If the software does not support prompting (for example, VBScript under ASP/IIS), you must supply the password or the user ID must not have one.

methods.description: This method can be used on a computer with a Notes® Client or Domino® Server. This method assumes that you trust the local Domino® or Notes® installation. Access to the Domino® Objects is as a client if the user ID is not specified as a server in the local Domino® Directory or Notes® Directory. Otherwise, access is as a server. As a client, you can access any server that accepts your user ID. As a server, you can access only the local server.

methods.name: InitializeUsingNotesUserName

params.name: name$
params.description: The user name. Can be an empty string. If you specify a name, this method looks it up in the local Domino® Directory and permits access to the local server depending on the "Server Access" and "COM Restrictions" settings. If you do not specify a name, access is granted if the server permits Anonymous access.

params.name: password$
params.description: Optional. The password must match the Internet password associated with the name.

methods.description: This method can be used only on a computer with a Domino® server. You can access only the local server through the Domino® Objects. This method assumes that you trust the local Domino® installation.

methods.name: ResetUserPassword

params.name: servername
params.description: Name of the server or servers to execute the agent. or NULL if the agent containing this method is executed on the local server. The server does not have to be a vault server. The server must be running Domino® 8.5 or later.

params.name: username
params.description: Name of user whose IDpassword or download count will be set.

params.name: password
params.description: New password for username's ID. This parameter can be an empty string if download count is being changed.

params.name: downloadcount
params.description: Optional. If this user's effective policy setting document has "Allow automatic ID downloads" set to "No", then this parameter specifies how many downloads the user can now perform. If "Allow automatic ID downloads" set to "Yes" this setting should be zero or omitted.

methods.description: This method is only supported on the server. If you needed to invoke this method from the Notes® Client, use RunOnServer method to invoke another agent containing method ResetUserPassword. This method is only supported in agents. The signer of the agent has to be an authorized password resetter, as does the server or servers on which the agent runs. To designated password resetters use Administration client tools "Create Id Vault" or "Manage Id Vault." For more information, see Domino® Administrator Help. In addition the agent signer has to have rights to run agents specified in the Programmability Section of the Security section in Domino® Directory.
methods.syntax: Call notesSession. ResetUserPassword( servername, username, password[, downloadcount ] )

methods.name: Resolve

params.name: url$
params.description: A URL that addresses a Domino® object.

returns.name: unknown
returns.description: A NotesDatabase, NotesView, NotesForm, NotesDocument, or NotesAgent object.

methods.description: The general forms of URLs that address Domino® objects are as follows: protocol :// host / database ?OpenDatabase protocol :// host / database / view ?OpenView

methods.name: SendConsoleCommand

params.name: serverName$
params.description: The name of the server. The user must have administrative access to the server. Use an empty string ("") to indicate the current computer.

params.name: consoleCommand$
params.description: The console command. Must be a valid console command.

returns.name: String
returns.description: String. The server's response to the command. This value is the same as would be sent to a remote console.

methods.description: This method can be used in a scheduled agent on a server to send console commands to another server. The signer of the agent must have privilege to "Run unrestricted methods and operations" on the server receiving the console command. If this method is executed on a client, the current user must have privilege under "Full Remote Console Administrators" on the server receiving the console command. If a console command abruptly closes the network connection to the server (for example, "drop all"), subsequent commands to the server fail with a network timeout until the connection is reestablished.

methods.name: SetEnvironmentVar

params.name: name$
params.description: The name of the environment variable to set.

params.name: valueV
params.description: The value of the environment variable. Date values are converted to strings.

params.name: issystemvar
params.description: Optional. If True, no dollar sign character ("$") is prepended to the variable name. Defaults to False.

methods.description: User must be a member, or a member of a group that is explicitly stated in the list of who can run unrestricted LotusScript® or Java™ agents. This list can be found on the security tab in the server's server document which is contained in the server's Name and Address book. If an environment variable called name$ already exists, it gets the new value. Otherwise, a new environment variable is added to the notes.ini (or Notes® Preferences) file. SetEnvironmentVar prepends a dollar sign character ("$") to the name$ before setting the value of the environment variable, unless you specify issystemvar as True or have already included a "$" as the first character of the name$. When you examine the environment variable in the notes.ini (or Notes® Preferences) file, you see the "$" character. When retrieving environment variables with the GetEnvironmentString or GetEnvironmentValue methods, you have the option of including or omitting the "$" from the variable name.

methods.name: UpdateProcessedDoc

params.name: notesDocument
params.description: The document to be marked as processed.

methods.description: You use this method in conjunction with the UnprocessedDocuments property, the UnprocessedFTSearch method, the UnprocessedFTSearchRange method, and the UnprocessedSearch method in NotesDatabase in an agent that runs on all new and modified documents, newly received mail documents, newly modified documents, and pasted documents. This method marks a document so subsequent invocations of the same agent recognize the document as processed. You must explicitly mark a document with this method. No implicit marking occurs in a LotusScript® agent. If a marked document is modified, it is unmarked and will be processed by the next invocation of the agent.

methods.name: VerifyPassword

params.name: password$
params.description: The string to be hashed.

params.name: hashedPassword$
params.description: The hashed string.

returns.name: flag
returns.description: Boolean.

methods.description: Use HashPassword to create a hashed value.

nodes.name: NotesStream
nodes.description: Represents a stream of binary or character data.

props.name: Bytes
props.description: Read-only. The size of the stream in bytes.

props.name: Charset
props.description: Read-only. Character set used by the external data source.

props.name: IsEOS
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether the selected position is at the end of the stream.

props.name: IsReadOnly
props.description: Read-only. Indicates that the stream is associated with a read-only file.

props.name: Position
props.description: Read-write. Offset from the beginning of the stream in bytes.
props.syntax: To get: offset& = notesStream .Position To set: notesStream .Position = offset&

methods.name: Close
methods.description: It is recommended that you close streams explicitly using this method, although they close implicitly at the end of their scope. Closing a stream with zero bytes deletes the associated file.

methods.name: Open

params.name: pathname$
params.description: The full path name of a file. Must be valid for the current platform.

params.name: charset$
params.description: Optional. The character set that describes the file content. Defaults to "System."

returns.name: flag
returns.description: Boolean.

methods.description: This method creates the file if it does not exist. When a stream is opened, property values are: It is recommended that you call Close when you are finished processing the stream.

methods.name: Read

params.name: length&
params.description: The number of bytes to read to a maximum of 65535. Defaults to the number of bytes in the stream starting at the current position to a maximum of 65535.

returns.name: buffer
returns.description: Variant array. The bytes read. The lower bound of the array is 0, except that if the number of bytes read is greater than 32767, the lower bound is -32768.

methods.description: This method starts at Position and reads the number of bytes specified or the number of bytes left in the stream.

methods.name: ReadText

params.name: oneLine&
params.description: Constant of type Long.

params.name: eol&
params.description: Constant of type Long. End-of-line character(s) within the text. The default is EOL_CRLF.

returns.name: String
returns.description: String. The text read.

methods.description: This method starts at Position and reads text until end of line, including the end-of-line character, or end of stream.

methods.name: Truncate
methods.description: This method raises an error if the stream is read-only. See IsReadOnly. When a stream is truncated, property values are: Closing a stream with zero bytes deletes the associated file.

methods.name: Write

params.name: buffer
params.description: The bytes to write, to a maximum of 65535.

returns.name: Long
returns.description: Long. The number of bytes written.

methods.description: This method appends the bytes to the end of the stream. This method raises an error if the stream is read-only. See IsReadOnly. When a stream is written, property values are:

methods.name: WriteText

params.name: text$
params.description: The text to write, to a maximum of 2GB bytes.

params.name: eol&
params.description: End-of-line character(s) appended to the text. The default is EOL_NONE.

returns.name: Long
returns.description: Long. The number of characters written.

methods.description: This method appends the text to the end of the stream. This method raises an error if the stream is read-only. See IsReadOnly. When a stream is written, property values are:

nodes.name: NotesTimer
nodes.description: Represents a mechanism for triggering an event every fixed number of seconds.

props.name: Comment
props.description: Read-write. Any comment.
props.syntax: To get: string = notesTimer .Comment To set: notesTimer .Comment = string

props.name: Enabled
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether the NotesTimer object is active.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesTimer .Enabled To get: notesTimer .Enabled = flag

props.name: Interval
props.description: Read-write. The interval in seconds at which the handler for the Alarm event is called.
props.syntax: To get: interval% = notesTimer .Interval To set: notesTimer .Interval = interval%

methods.name: New

params.name: interval%
params.type: The interval in seconds for calling the event handler for the timer
params.description: This parameter sets the Interval property. The interval is not guaranteed; other activities on the computer involving script may delay the event.

params.name: comment$
params.description: Any comment. This parameter sets the Comment property.

methods.description: New creates a timer object.
methods.syntax: Dim variableName As New NotesTimer( interval [, comment] ) Set notestimer = New NotesTimer( interval [, comment] )

events.name: Alarm
events.description: Occurs for an enabled NotesTimer object at the specified interval.

props.name: Documents

params.name: navigatorName$

events.name: PostDocumentDelete
events.description: Occurs after the command is given to delete (clear or cut) a document, but before it is actually deleted.

events.name: QueryDocumentDelete

events.name: QueryDragDrop

events.name: QueryDropToArchive

nodes.name: NotesUIDocument
nodes.description: Represents the document that's currently open in the Notes® workspace.

props.name: AutoReload
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether or not the current document should be refreshed whenever the corresponding back-end document changes. Refreshing the current document updates its representation in memory, and visually on the workspace, to reflect the changes that have been made to the back-end document.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesUIDocument.AutoReload To set: notesUIDocument.AutoReload = flag

props.name: CurrentField
props.description: Read-only. The name of the field where the cursor is.

props.name: DialogBoxCanceled
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether a user cancels a dialog.

props.name: Document
props.description: Read-only. The back-end document that corresponds to the currently open document.

props.name: EditMode
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether a document is in Edit mode.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesUIDocument.EditMode To set: notesUIDocument.EditMode = flag

props.name: FieldHelp
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether field Help for a document is displayed.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesUIDocument.FieldHelp To set: notesUIDocument.FieldHelp = flag

props.name: HiddenChars
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether the hidden characters in a document (such as tabs and carriage returns) are displayed.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesUIDocument.HiddenChars To set: notesUIDocument.HiddenChars = flag

props.name: HorzScrollBar

methods.name: Categorize

methods.name: Clear
methods.description: This method is valid only when the document is in Edit mode. The selection is not copied to the clipboard. If you want to remove the selection and place it on the clipboard, use the Cut method.

methods.name: Close

params.name: immediate
params.description: Optional. If True, the document is immediately closed. If False, closing the document may be delayed. The default is False.

methods.description: If the user has edited the document, Notes® displays a dialog box asking if the user would like to save the changes.

methods.name: CollapseAllSections
methods.description: A section can be on a form or in a rich-text field in a document. When you call this method, each section collapses regardless of the expand and collapse settings it has on the form or document.

methods.name: Copy
methods.description: If the user has something on the Clipboard, it is lost when you call this method.

methods.name: CreateObject

params.name: name$
params.description: Optional. The name you want to use to refer to the object.

params.name: type$
params.description: Optional. The type of object that you want to create. The type that you specify must correspond to an entry in the OLE registry and is case-sensitive. The method creates an empty instance of the specified object type. If you specify a type, you must omit the filePath$ parameter.

params.name: filePath$
params.description: Optional. The path and file name of the source document that you want to use to create an object. The method creates an object from the file. If you specify a file path, you must specify an empty string ("") for the type$ parameter.

returns.name: handleV
returns.description: Variant. A handle to the OLE object.

methods.description: You must specify either a type$ or a filePath$, but not both. If you specify a filePath$, you create the object in a rich-text field. You must, however, code a GetObject statement to give LotusScript® a handle on the object. The current document must be open in Edit mode and the cursor must be in an editable rich-text field. With no parameters, this method displays the Create Object dialog box, which allows the user to select the object to create.
methods.syntax: Set handleV = notesUIDocument.CreateObject( [ name$ [ , type$ _ [ , filePath$ ] ] ] )

methods.name: Cut
methods.description: This method is valid only when the document is in Edit mode. If the user has something on the Clipboard, it is lost when you call this method.

methods.name: DeleteDocument
methods.description: The document is not actually deleted from the database until the user refreshes the view or closes the database, and chooses to delete the marked document. If the current document is already marked for deletion, this method closes the document, leaving the deletion mark in place.

methods.name: DeselectAll
methods.description: If there is nothing selected, this method raises the error "Document command is not available."

methods.name: ExpandAllSections
methods.description: A section can be either on a form or in a rich-text field in a document. When you call this method, each section expands, regardless of the expand and collapse settings it has on the form or document.

methods.name: FieldAppendText

params.name: fieldName$
params.description: The field into which you want to append text$. The field must be editable. Specify an empty string ("") to append the text to the current field.

params.name: text$
params.description: The value you want to append to the field.

methods.description: This method is valid only when the document is in Edit mode. If there is already some text in the field, the new text is added directly after the last character of the existing text.

methods.name: FieldClear

params.name: fieldName$
params.description: Optional. The name of the field you want to clear. The field must be editable. If you don't specify a fieldName$, the current field is cleared.

methods.description: This method is valid only when the document is in Edit mode.

params.name: fieldName$
params.description: The name of the field you want to check. Specify an empty string ("") to check the contents of the current field.

params.name: textValue$
params.description: The value to look for.

returns.name: flag
returns.description: Boolean. Returns True if the field contains the textValue$. Otherwise returns False.

methods.description: In an open document, checks if a field contains a specific text value.

params.description: Optional. The name of the field that has the contents you want. If you don't specify a fieldName$, the method returns the contents of the current field.

returns.name: String
returns.description: String. The contents of the field you specified.

methods.description: All values are displayed for a multi-value field. The values are separated by the multi-value separator as specified for display purposes in the field properties.

methods.name: FieldSetText

params.name: fieldName$
params.description: The name of the field with the value you want to set. The field must be editable. Specify an empty string ("") to set the value of the current field.

params.name: textValue$
params.description: The value you want to give to the field. If the fieldName$ is not of type text, this value is converted from text to the appropriate data type when you save the document.

methods.description: This method is valid only when the document is in Edit mode. To enter multiple values for a multi-value field, separate the values with the input separator as specified in the field properties.

methods.name: FindFreeTimeDialog

params.name: reqPeopleItem$
params.description: Name of a Text field in the current document that contains the names of people required at the meeting.

params.name: optPeopleItems$
params.description: Name of a Text field in the current document that contains the names of people whose attendance is optional.

params.name: reqRoomsItems$
params.description: Name of a Text field in the current document that contains the names of rooms required for the meeting.

params.name: optRoomsItems$
params.description: Name of a Text field in the current document that contains the names of optional rooms.

params.name: reqResourcesItems$
params.description: Name of a Text field in the current document that contains the names of resources required for the meeting.

params.name: optResourcesItems$
params.description: Name of a Text field in the current document that contains the names of optional resources.

params.name: removedPeopleItems$
params.description: Name of a Text field in the current document that contains the names of people to remove from the attendance list.

params.name: startDateItem$
params.description: Name of a Date/Time field in the current document that contains the start time for the meeting. You must specify the StartDateTime.

params.name: endDateItem$
params.description: Name of a Date/Time field in the current document that contains the end time for the meeting. You must specify the EndDateTime. The EndDateTime must be later than the StartDateTime.

methods.description: This method is valid only when the document is in Edit mode. If you don't need a parameter, specify it as an empty string. The meeting time should not span midnight.
methods.syntax: Call notesUIDocument.FindFreeTimeDialog( reqPeopleItems$ , optPeopleItems$ , reqRoomsItems$ , optRoomsItems$ , reqResourcesItems$ , optResourcesItems$ , removedPeopleItems$ , startDateItem$ , endDateItem$ )

methods.name: FindFreeTimeDialogEx

params.name: reqPeopleItems$
params.description: Names of one or more multi-value Text fields in the current document that contain the names of people required at the meeting.

params.name: optPeopleItems$
params.description: Names of one or more multi-value Text fields in the current document that contain the names of people whose attendance is optional.

params.name: reqRoomsItems$
params.description: Names of one or more multi-value Text fields in the current document that contain the names of rooms required for the meeting.

params.name: optRoomsItems$
params.description: Names of one or more multi-value Text fields in the current document that contain the names of optional rooms.

params.name: reqResourcesItems$
params.description: Names of one or more multi-value Text fields in the current document that contain the names of resources required for the meeting.

params.name: optResourcesItems$
params.description: Names of one or more multi-value Text fields in the current document that contain the names of optional resources.

params.name: removedPeopleItems$
params.description: Names of one or more multi-value Text fields in the current document that contain the names of people to remove from the attendance list.

params.name: startDateItem$
params.description: Name of a Date/Time field (item) in the current document that contains the start time for the meeting. You must specify the StartDateTime.

params.name: endDateItem$
params.description: Name of a Date/Time field (item) in the current document that contains the end time for the meeting. You must specify the EndDateTime. The EndDateTime must be later than the StartDateTime.

returns.name: flag
returns.description: Boolean. Returns True if the user selects OK, False if the user selects Cancel.

methods.description: This method is valid only when the document is in Edit mode. If you don't need a parameter, specify it as an empty string. The meeting time should not span midnight.
methods.syntax: flag = notesUIDocument.FindFreeTimeDialogEx( reqPeopleItems$ , optPeopleItems$ , reqRoomsItems$ , optRoomsItems$ , reqResourcesItems$ , optResourcesItems$ , removedPeopleItems$ , startDateItem$ , endDateItem$ )

methods.name: FindString

params.name: text$
params.description: The text string that you want to look for.

params.name: wholeword
params.description: Optional. Specify True to match the whole word. Default is True.

params.name: backwards
params.description: Optional. Specify True to search backwards. Default is False.

params.name: caseSensitive
params.description: Optional. Specify True to perform a case-sensitive search. Default is False.

params.name: wildCards
params.description: Optional. Specify True to use wild cards such as (*) in the search. Default is False.

params.name: accentSensitive
params.description: Optional. Specify True to perform an accent-sensitive search. Default is False.

params.name: searchDocument
params.description: Optional. Specify True to search the current document. Default is True.

params.name: findNext
params.description: Optional. Specify True to find the next match. Default is True.

params.name: wrapAround
params.description: Optional. Specify True to perform a wrap search. Default is False.

params.name: ignoreDiacritics
params.description: Optional. Specify True to ignore diacritics. Default is False.

methods.description: Performs the equivalent of Edit - Find (CTRL+F or CTRL+G) that is done in the User Interface without prompting the user.
methods.syntax: Call notesUIDocument.FindString(text$ [, wholeword ] [, backwards ] [, caseSensitive ] [, wildCards ] [, accentSensitive ] [, searchDocument ] [, findNext ] [, wrapAround ] [, ignoreDiacritics ])

methods.name: Refresh

params.name: IncludeRichTextItems
params.description: Optional. Specify True to update all rich-text items in the back-end document to match the data in the front end. Defaults to False.

methods.description: This method is valid only when the document is in Edit mode. This method is useful when the value of a computed field depends upon the contents of another field in the document whose value may have changed, and you want to update the computed field. If you only need to update Hide When formulas, see RefreshHideFormulas. This call is the same as if the user chooses View - Refresh.

methods.name: RefreshHideFormulas
methods.description: Use this method when you want to recalculate hide-when formulas without recalculating all the formulas on the current document form. This method is an expensive operation, and may affect performance.

methods.name: Reload
methods.description: This method is valid only when the document is in Edit mode. This method reloads the back-end document associtated with the editing session, which can be acquired through the Document property. It does not reload the base back-end document saved on disk. You must close and reopen the document to reload the base back-end document. This method is useful only when the AutoReload property is False. By default this property is True and modifications to the back-end document appear immediately in the front-end. Where reloading on every modification is slow, you might set the AutoReload property to False, make the modifications, and call Reload.

methods.name: Save
methods.description: This method is valid only when the document is in Edit mode. You must call Save if you want changes to the document to be written to disk.

methods.name: SaveNewVersion
methods.description: This method is valid only when the document is in Edit mode. To use this method, the Versions options on the document's form must be set as follows. The form must have one of the following versioning options. The versioning option determines what kind of document you create when you call the SaveNewVersion method.

methods.name: SelectAll
methods.description: In a document in Edit mode, selects the entire contents of the current field. In a document in Read mode, selects the entire contents of the document.

methods.name: Send
methods.description: The document must contain a SendTo field indicating the recipients. There are two kinds of fields that affect the mailing of the document:

methods.name: SpellCheck

events.name: OnBlur

events.name: OnFocus
events.description: Occurs when the subwindow displaying the document receives a focus notification.

events.name: OnHelp
events.description: Occurs after the user chooses Help or presses F1.

events.name: OnLoad
events.description: Occurs after the current document is opened, but before the user has input focus.

events.name: OnSize
events.description: Occurs when document window is resized.

events.name: OnSubmit
events.description: Occurs just before the current document is saved.

events.name: OnUnload
events.description: Occurs just before the current document is closed.

events.name: PostModeChange
events.description: Occurs after the document has changed modes (from Read to Edit mode, or from Edit to Read mode) but before the user has been given input focus.

events.name: PostOpen
events.description: Occurs after the current document is opened, but before the user has input focus.

events.name: PostRecalc
events.description: Occurs just after the current document is recalculated (after all the formulas on the document form have executed).

events.name: PostSave
events.description: Occurs just after the current document is saved.

events.name: PostSend
events.description: Occurs just after the current document is sent (mailed).

events.name: QueryClose
events.description: Occurs just before the current document is closed.

events.name: QueryModeChange
events.description: Occurs just before the current document changes modes (from Read to Edit mode, or from Edit to Read mode).

events.name: QueryOpen
events.description: Occurs just before the current document is opened.

events.name: QueryRecalc
events.description: Occurs just before the current document is recalculated (before all the formulas on the document execute).

events.name: QuerySave
events.description: Occurs just before the current document is saved.

events.name: QuerySend
events.description: Occurs just before the current document is sent (mailed).

events.name: OnBlur
events.description: Occurs when the subwindow displaying the view receives a loss-of-focus notification.

events.name: OnFocus
events.description: Occurs when the subwindow displaying the view receives a focus notification.

events.name: OnSelect
events.description: This event occurs when a view is opened, since a row is highlighted at that time. This event also occurs when a document is selected or deselected using the checkmark "selection margin" or when the user clicks the already-selected row. The user changes the current highlight (or caret location) by a mouse click or pressing the up and down arrows. Exactly one document or category is highlighted in a view. Use CaretNoteID to get the Note ID of the document currently highlighted. If there are no documents in the view, this property has a value of "0". If a category row is highlighted, CaretNoteID returns a note ID with the high-order bit set. You can determine whether the current row contains a category or a document either by using GetDocumentByID to try to retrieve the document (which will return Nothing for a category entry), or by testing the high-order bit (e.g. Source.CaretNoteID Like "NT[89A-F]*").

nodes.name: NotesUIWorkspace
nodes.description: Represents the current Notes® workspace window.

props.name: CurrentCalendarDateTime
props.description: Read-only. The date and time of the current (the selected) region in a calendar view.

props.name: CurrentDatabase
props.description: Returns a NotesUIDatabase object representing the database that's currently open.

props.name: CurrentDocument
props.description: Returns a NotesUIDocument object representing the document in the window that currently has focus.

props.name: CurrentView
props.description: Returns a NotesUIView object representing the view that's currently open.

props.name: IsEmbeddedInsideWCT
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether the applicaton is running in the Workplace Client.

props.name: IsInCompositeApp
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether the component is running within a composite application.

props.name: IsUsingJavaElement
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether the current component is a Java-based view or outline.

methods.name: New
methods.description: New creates a NotesUIWorkspace object representing the current workspace.

methods.name: AddDatabase

params.name: server$
params.description: The name of the server where the database resides. If this parameter is omitted or is an empty string (""), the database is opened on the local computer.

params.name: filename$
params.description: The path and file name of the database.

methods.description: Adds a database to the workspace and highlights the icon, or highlights the icon if the database is already on the workspace.

methods.name: CheckAlarms
methods.description: You use this method when creating a new alarm from a template. Any time a template creates a new appoint event that contains an alarm, the template script calls CheckAlarms, causing the daemon to check for new alarms.

methods.name: ComposeDocument

params.name: server$
params.description: Optional. The name of the server where the database resides. If this parameter is omitted or is an empty string (""), the database is opened on the local computer. If both server$ and file$ are omitted or empty strings, the current database is used.

params.name: file$
params.description: Optional. The file name of the database where you want to compose a document. If this parameter is omitted or is an empty string (""), a document is composed in the current database.

params.name: form$
params.description: Optional. The name of the form you want to use to compose the document. If this parameter is omitted or is an empty string (""), the Create Other dialog box is displayed and the user can select a form name.

params.name: windowWidth#
params.description: Optional. The width in inches of the document window. If this parameter is omitted, the window is the width of the current window.

params.name: windowHeight#
params.description: Optional. The height in inches of the document window. If this parameter is omitted, the window is the height of the current window.

params.name: returnNotesUIDocument
params.description: Optional. If True (default), this method attempts to return the NotesUIDocument object; if False, this method does not attempt to return the object. See the usage note on lsERR_LSXU13_ANCESTOR_TARGET_FRAME.

returns.name: notesUIDocument
returns.description: The document that was just created and opened.

methods.description: If the ComposeDocument method is used in a view action, the form formula of the view overrides the form specified in the method. To avoid this problem, add the following line to the form formula: This method raises the error lsERR_LSXU13_ANCESTOR_TARGET_FRAME (4426) if it attempts to return a NotesUIDocument object for a target frame that is the ancestor of the frame containing the code. You can suppress returning the object by specifying returnNotesUIDocument as False. The document opens in the target frame but you do not have access to its NotesUIDocument object. Calling this method as a subroutine does not suppress returning the object; you must specify returnNotesUIDocument as False.
methods.syntax: Set notesUIDocument = notesUIWorkspace .ComposeDocument( [ server$ [, file$ [, form$ [, windowWidth# [, windowHeight# [, returnNotesUIDocument]]]]]] )

methods.name: DialogBox

params.name: form$
params.description: The name of the form.

params.name: autoHorzFit
params.description: Optional. Specify True to scale the dialog box horizontally to fit the first layout region or table on the form. Otherwise, the dialog box is not scaled horizontally.

params.name: autoVertFit
params.description: Optional. Specify True to scale the dialog box vertically to fit the first layout layout region or table on the form. Otherwise, the dialog box is not scaled vertically.

params.name: noCancel
params.description: Optional. Specify True to not display the Cancel button. Otherwise, the dialog box contains the Cancel button.

params.name: noNewFields
params.description: Optional. Specify True to not add fields that are on the dialog box form but not already contained in the underlying document. Otherwise, all dialog box fields are passed to the underlying document.

params.name: noFieldUpdate
params.description: Optional. Specify True to not pass any edits from the dialog box to the underlying document (for example, if you're passing the edits somewhere else in a Queryclose script for the dialog box form). Otherwise, the edits are passed to the underlying document.

params.name: readOnly
params.description: Optional. Specify True to prohibit writing to the dialog box (for example, if you are using the dialog box to display a help screen). Otherwise, the dialog box is read-write. This option makes noCancel true.

params.name: title$
params.description: Optional. A title for the dialog box. Defaults to "Notes®."

params.name: notesDocument
params.description: Optional. A document to be opened instead of the current document.

params.name: sizeToTable
params.description: Optional. Specify True to apply autoHorzFit and autoVertFit to the first table on the form. Otherwise, they are applied to the first layout region on the form.

params.name: noOkCancel
params.description: Optional. Specify True to not display the OK button. Otherwise, the dialog box contains the OK button. Using this parameter prevents any changes made in the dialog box from being reflected in the current document. Use the RefreshParentNote method if you want changes to be reflected in the current document but don't want an Ok button to display.

params.name: okCancelAtBottom
params.description: Optional. Specify True to display the OK and/or Cancel buttons side-by-side at the end of the dialog box. If you specify False or omit this parameter, the buttons appear stacked at the beginning.

returns.name: flag
returns.description: Boolean. Returns True if the user clicks OK in the dialog box. Returns False if the user clicks Cancel.

methods.description: The autoHorzFit and autoVertFit parameters allow you to display an entire layout region (sizeToTable is False) or table (sizeToTable is True) in a dialog box without displaying the rest of the form. If the form has more than one layout region or table, the first is used. For best results: If autoHorzFit and autoVertFit are both False, the entire form is used and no sizing takes place. If the form contains no layout region (sizeToTable is False) or table (sizeToTable is True), the entire form is used and no sizing takes place.

methods.name: EditDocument

params.name: editMode
params.description: Optional. Specify True (default) to open the document in Edit mode. Specify False to open the document in Read mode or to put the document in the given Edit mode.

params.name: notesDocument
params.description: Optional. Opens the specified document instead of the currently selected document.

params.name: notesDocumentReadOnly
params.description: Optional. If True, sets notesDocument to read-only mode, which prevents the user from later switching to read-write mode. Defaults to False. This parameter does not open the document in Read mode; you must set editMode to False. This parameter has no effect if you do not specify notesDocument.

params.name: documentAnchor$
params.description: Optional. Opens the document to an anchor link within the document. This parameter specifies the text of the anchor link. The edit mode (parameter 1) must be False if this parameter is specified.

params.name: returnNotesUIDocument
params.description: Optional. If True (default), attempts to return the NotesUIDocument object; if False, does not attempt to return the object. See the usage note on lsERR_LSXU13_ANCESTOR_TARGET_FRAME.

params.name: newInstance
params.description: Optional. If True (default), opens a new instance of notesDocument (parameter 2) in the UI. If False, changes focus to an existing instance of notesDocument if one exists, or to a new instance if one does not exist. This parameter does not apply if notesDocument is not specified or the document has a target frame.

returns.name: notesUIDocument
returns.description: The document that's just been opened. Does not attempt to return this object if returnNotesUIDocument is False.

methods.description: The current document may be either of the following: This method raises the error lsERR_LSXU13_ANCESTOR_TARGET_FRAME (4426) if it attempts to return a NotesUIDocument object for a target frame that is the ancestor of the frame containing the code. You can suppress returning the object by specifying returnNotesUIDocument as False. The document opens in the target frame but you do not have access to its NotesUIDocument object. Calling this method as a subroutine does not suppress returning the object; you must specify returnNotesUIDocument as False.

methods.name: EditProfile

params.name: profileName$
params.description: The name or an alias of the Profile document, or the form name to use to display the Profile document.

params.name: uniqueKey$
params.description: Optional. The key associated with the Profile document.

methods.description: You can efficiently store information in Profile documents. You can access them quickly and use them in templates to store specific information, or to share variables across scripts within a database. Only one profile of a given form or name can exist per database per key. If you create a profile without a key, Notes® assumes it's the only Profile document of that type in the database. You need at least author access to create a profile document. Documents saved with EditProfile are hidden.

methods.name: EnableAlarms

params.name: enable
params.description: True sets the $EnableAlarms notes.ini variable. False stops the alarm daemon if it is running.

methods.description: Starts or stops the alarm daemon.

methods.name: Folder

params.name: foldername$
params.description: Optional. Name of the folder to which the current document is to be moved. If you omit this parameter or specify it as an empty string, this method displays the "Move To Folder" dialog box.

params.name: move
params.description: True moves the document from the current folder; False copies it. This parameter has no effect in a view -- the document is always copied. This parameter has no effect if the first parameter is not specified or is an empty string -- the document is copied or moved depending on the user's selection in the dialog box.

methods.description: Copies or moves the current document to a folder.

methods.name: GetComponentViewPreference

returns.name: preferenceValue
returns.description: Text list. Value(s) of the specified preference.

methods.description: This method is only valid in the Notes® client. If the PreferenceID cannot be found, an error is returned. The Error string is "Specified view preference is unavailable". The following are some common component view preference IDs.

methods.name: GetCurrentDatabase

returns.name: notesUIDatabase
returns.description: Represents the current database.

methods.description: This method supersedes the CurrentDatabase property.

methods.name: GetListOfTunes

returns.name: variant
returns.description: An array of strings that represent the sounds on the system.

methods.description: Returns the known sounds on your system.

methods.name: OpenDatabase

params.name: server$
params.description: The name of the server where the database resides.

params.name: file$
params.description: The path and file name of the database.

params.name: view$
params.description: Optional. The name of the view you want to open. If you omit this parameter, the database opens to its default view.

params.name: key$
params.description: Optional. Indicates to which document you want to scroll when the view is opened. The key is a value that appears in the first sorted column of view$. If you omit this parameter, the caret will be placed at its default location for that view (which might be the beginning, the end, or a memorized position from your last visit).

params.name: newInstance
params.description: Optional. Specify True if you want the view to open in a new window, even if there's already a window open for the database. If you specify False or omit this parameter, the new window is opened only when it's actually needed.

params.name: temp
params.description: Optional. Specify True if you only want the database opened on a temporary basis for browsing, without adding the database to the user's workspace. If you specify False or omit this parameter, the database is added to the user's workspace.

methods.description: Opens a database to a view that you specify.
methods.syntax: Call notesUIWorkspace .OpenDatabase( server$, file$[, view$][, key$][, newInstance][, temp] )

methods.name: OpenFileDialog

params.name: multipleSelection
params.description: Boolean.

params.name: title$
params.description: Optional. Title for the dialog box. Defaults to "Open."

params.name: filters$

params.name: initialDirectory$
params.description: Optional. The directory to which the dialog box is opened. Otherwise, determined by the system.

params.name: initialFile$
params.description: Optional. Default text for the selection box. Otherwise, no default text for the selection box. This parameter does not apply to platforms such as the Macintosh that do not have a selection box.

returns.name: stringArray
returns.description: An array of type String.

methods.description: This method returns names. It does not open or perform any other action on files. See SaveFileDialog for a variation.

methods.description: Opens the specified frameset within the current target frame.

params.name: pagename$
params.description: Name of the page defined for the current database.

methods.description: Opens the specified page within the current target frame.

methods.name: OutlineReload

params.name: outlinename$
params.description: The name of the outline to be reloaded.

params.name: databasefilename$
params.description: The path and file name of the database. The current server is used, or incorporate the server name at the beginning of the path. Use a null string, "", to indicate the current database.

methods.description: This method can be used to to redisplay a modified outline after you have edited its design programmatically using the NotesOutline class. Otherwise, you would have to close and reopen the application to see these changes.

methods.name: PickListCollection

params.name: type%
params.description: This parameter is restricted to PICKLIST_CUSTOM (3).

params.name: multipleSelection
params.description: Optional. Specify True if you want to select multiple documents. Defaults to False.

params.name: server$
params.description: Name of the server where the database resides.

params.name: databaseFileName$
params.description: The filename of the database that contains the view.

params.name: viewName$
params.description: Name of the view you want displayed in the dialog box.

params.name: title$
params.description: Title for the dialog box.

params.name: prompt$
params.description: The prompt that you want to appear inside the dialog box.

params.name: Singlecategory$
params.description: Optional. Displays the specified category in the view. The view should be categorized in order to use this parameter.

returns.name: This method returns a NotesDocumentCollection object containing the selected documents. If the user clicks Cancel, the collection is empty and the Count property is 0.
returns.description: Example

methods.description: Creates a NotesDocumentCollection object from a list selected by the user. You specify a view within a database from which the user can select one or more documents.
methods.syntax: Set notesDocumentCollection = notesUIWorkspace .PickListCollection( type% [, multipleSelection ], server$, databaseFileName$, viewName$, title$, prompt$ [, Singlecategory$ ] )

methods.name: PickListStrings

params.name: type%
params.description: The type of view that you specify. The legal values for this parameter are as follows:

params.name: PICKLIST_NAMES (0)
params.description: Can only be used in the first form of the method.

params.name: PICKLIST_ROOMS (1)
params.description: Can only be used in the first form of the method.

params.name: PICKLIST_RESOURCES (2)
params.description: Can only be used in the first form of the method.

params.name: PICKLIST_CUSTOM (3)
params.type: Displays the dialog box for the view that you specify
params.description: Can only be used in the second form of the method.

params.name: multipleSelection
params.description: Optional. Specify True if you want to select multiple documents. Defaults to False.

params.name: server$
params.description: Name of the server where the database resides.

params.name: databaseFileName$
params.description: The filename of the database that contains the view.

params.name: viewName$
params.description: Name of the view that you want displayed in the dialog box.

params.name: title$
params.description: Title for the dialog box.

params.name: prompt$
params.description: The prompt that you want to appear inside the dialog box.

params.name: column%
params.description: The column value that you want returned.

params.name: categoryname$
params.description: Optional. Displays the specified category in the view. The view should be categorized in order to use this parameter.

returns.name: This method returns a variant containing a string array if you click OK, or it returns EMPTY if you click Cancel.

methods.description: Creates a string array from a list selected by the user.
methods.syntax: stringArray = notesUIWorkspace .PickListStrings( type% [, multipleSelection ] ) stringArray = notesUIWorkspace .PickListStrings( type% [, multipleSelection ], server$, databaseFileName$, viewName$, title$, prompt$, column% [, categoryname$ ] )

params.name: tuneName$
params.description: Could be the name of the sound from the GetListOfTunes method or a complete file path.

methods.description: Plays the specified tune.

methods.name: Prompt

params.name: type%
params.description: Indicates the type of dialog box that you want to display. May be any of the following:

params.name: PROMPT_CHOOSEDATABASE (13)
params.description: PROMPT_OK (1)

params.name: PROMPT_OKCANCELCOMBO (5)
params.description: PROMPT_OKCANCELEDIT (3)

params.name: PROMPT_OKCANCELEDITCOMBO (6)
params.description: PROMPT_OKCANCELLIST (4)

params.name: PROMPT_OKCANCELLISTMULT (7)
params.description: PROMPT_PASSWORD (10)

params.name: PROMPT_YESNO (2)
params.description: PROMPT_YESNOCANCEL (11)

params.name: title$
params.description: The text that you want displayed in the dialog box title bar. Required for all type%s, although you can specify a null string with "". The maximum number of characters is 65.

params.name: prompt$
params.description: The text that you want displayed within the dialog box. Required for all type%s, although you can specify a null string with "". The maximum number of characters is 255.

params.name: default
params.type: The value that will be used as your default input value
params.description: The input section of the dialog box is primed with the value; you can accept it by selecting OK, replace it with another value, or Cancel.

params.name: values
params.type: The values that you want displayed in the dialog box list box
params.description: You can select one of these values as the input. Required only with type%s PROMPT_OKCANCELLIST, PROMPT_OKCANCELCOMBO, PROMPT_OKCANCELEDITCOMBO, and PROMPT_OKCANCELLISTMULT.

returns.name: PROMPT_OK
returns.description: Variant of type Integer. Always returns 1.

methods.description: Displays a dialog box and returns a value based on your actions in the dialog box.
methods.syntax: variant = notesUIWorkspace .Prompt( type%, title$, prompt$ [, default ] [, values ] )

methods.name: RefreshParentNote
methods.description: This method is used mainly to create your own buttons to dismiss a dialog box. Mostly used in the QueryClose event. If your button calls this method and then closes the dialog (e.g. with NotesUIDocument.Close), the NotesUIWorkspace.DialogBox method in the caller will return True, as if the user had clicked OK.

methods.name: ReloadWindow
methods.description: Reloads the contents of the current window.

params.name: directoriesOnly
params.description: Boolean.

params.name: title$
params.description: Optional. Title for the dialog box. Defaults to "Save As."

params.name: filters$
params.description: Optional. User-defined string of filters that can limit the files displayed. For example, "Notes® Databases|*.nsf|Notes Templates|*.ntf" displays only files with the suffixes .nsf and .ntf. Undefined if parameter 1 is True.

params.name: initialDirectory$
params.description: Optional. The directory to which the dialog box is opened. Otherwise, determined by system.

params.name: initialFile$
params.description: Optional. Default text for the selection box. Otherwise, no default text for the selection box.

returns.name: stringArray
returns.description: An array of type String.

methods.name: SetCurrentLocation

params.name: location$
params.description: Optional. Name of location to switch to. If location$ is "" or omitted, this method brings up the Change Location dialog box.

methods.description: This method fails if the Advanced option "User ID to switch to" for the location is set to other than the current user.

methods.name: SetTargetFrame

params.name: frame$
params.description: The name of the frame that a view, page, frameset, or document should open into. You could also specify a path to the nested frame.

methods.description: Use SetTargetFrame method before opening the view, page, or frameset, or before composing or editing a document. The following methods use the frame specified in the SetTargetFrame method:

methods.name: URLOpen

params.name: Url$
params.description: Optional. The URL for the Web page you want to open, for example, http://www.acme.com/. If you omit this parameter, you see the Open URL dialog box where the user can enter the URL for the Web page.

params.name: reload%
params.description: Optional. Enter 1 (True) to reload the page from its Internet server. Enter 0 (False) to load the page from the Internet only if it is not already in the Web Navigator database. Enter 2 to reload the page only if it has been modified on its Internet server. The default value is 0.

params.name: urlList%
params.description: Optional. Web pages can contain URL links to other Web pages. This value specifies that the Web Navigator should save the URLs in a field called URLLinksn in the document. (The Web Navigator creates a new URLLinksn field each time the field size reaches 64K. For example, the first URLLinks field would be URLLinks1, the second would be URLLinks2, and so on.)

params.name: charset$
params.description: Optional. Enter the MIME character set (for example, ISO-2022-JP for Japanese or ISO-8859-1 for United States) that you want the Web Navigator to use when processing the Web page. Use this parameter only when the Web Navigator misdetects the correct MIME character set of the URL contents.

params.name: webusername$
params.description: Optional. Some Internet servers require you to obtain a username before you can access their pages. This parameter allows you to enter the username that you previously obtained from the Internet server.

params.name: webpassword$
params.description: Optional. Some Internet servers require you to obtain a password before you can access their pages. This parameter allows you to enter the password that you previously obtained from the Internet server.

params.name: proxywebusername$
params.description: Optional. Some proxy servers require that you specify a user name in order to connect through them. This parameter allows you to enter the user name for the proxy server. See your administrator for the user name required by the proxy.

params.name: proxywebpassword$
params.description: Optional. Some proxy servers require that you specify a password in order to connect through them. This parameter allows you to enter the password for the proxy server. See your administrator for the password required by the proxy.

params.name: notesRetriever
params.description: Optional. If set to False or omitted, the default browser opens the URL. If present, or set to True, the Notes® Web Navigator opens the URL.

methods.description: Retrieves a World Wide Web page specified by its URL.
methods.syntax: Call notesUIWorkspace .URLOpen( Url$ , reload% , urlList% , charset$ , webusername$ , webpassword$ , proxywebusername$ , proxywebpassword$, notesRetriever )

methods.name: UseLSX

params.name: lsxLibraryName$
params.description: The lsx file to load, either a name prepended with an asterisk or the full path name of the file. If you specify a name prepended with an asterisk (for example, "*lsxodbc"), the file is determined by searching the registry, initialization file, or preferences file, depending on the client platform. The Windows95 registry, for example, might contain an entry for HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE, SOFTWARE, Lotus®, Components, LotusScriptExtensions, 2.0, LSXODBC, whose value is "c:\notes95\nlsxodbc.dll."

methods.description: LotusScript® registers the Public classes defined in the LSX file for use in the module containing the UseLSX statement. Other modules that use the containing module can also access the Public classes.

methods.name: ViewRebuild

params.name: viewrebuild
params.description: Defaults to True. Rebuilds the view design, for example, to capture a change in a column formula.

params.name: loadpreferences
params.description: Defaults to False. In a calendar view, reloads the view properties (preferences). This parameter has no effect in a non-calendar view.

methods.description: You do not have to use this method to update the view. ViewRefresh is more efficient. If a view is in the active window, this method rebuilds that view. If a document is in the active window, this method rebuilds the view associated with the document when the view's window is activated.

methods.name: ViewRefresh
methods.description: If a view is in the active window, this method refreshes that view. If a document is in the active window, this method refreshes the view associated with the document when the view's window is activated. This method first refreshes the associated back-end view unless the code containing the method is running in a UI document. To refresh a UI view from within a document, first call Refresh of NotesView.

nodes.name: NotesUserID
nodes.description: The NotesUserID class represents the Notes ID file or a Notes ID when it resides within the Domino IDVault. This object is primarily used to obtain the names of the private encryption keys which are available for use within the UserID object.

params.name: keys
params.type: Array of strings. Encryption keys of the specified user ID

returns.name: keys
returns.description: Array of strings. Encryption keys of the specified user ID.

methods.description: Gets the encryption keys of a given userID

methods.name: GetUserName

returns.name: name
returns.description: User name of this user ID.

methods.description: Gets the user name of a given user ID.

nodes.name: NotesView
nodes.description: Represents a view or folder of a database and provides access to documents within it.

props.name: Aliases
props.description: Read-write. The aliases of a view.
props.syntax: To get: aliases = notesView .Aliases To set: notesView .Aliases = aliases

props.name: AllEntries
props.description: Read-only. All document entries in a view.
props.syntax: To get: Set notesViewEntryCollection = notesView .AllEntries Usage A view entry collection contains only document entries (no categories or totals). If a view is filtered by FTSearch, this property returns the entries in the filtered view. Use NotesViewNavigator (ViewNavigator in Java™) to get all view entries including categories and totals.

props.name: AutoUpdate
props.description: Read-write. Indicates whether a view is automatically refreshed by the currently running code when a navigation method touches an update (addition, deletion, or change) to the database that occurred after view creation or the last refresh.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesView .AutoUpdate To set: notesView .AutoUpdate = flag

props.name: BackgroundColor
props.description: Read-write. The background color of a view.
props.syntax: To get: color% = notesView .BackgroundColor To set: notesView .BackgroundColor = color%

props.name: ColumnCount
props.description: Read-only. The number of columns in a view.

props.name: ColumnNames
props.description: Read-only. The names of the columns in a view.

props.name: Columns
props.description: Read-only. All the columns in a view.

props.name: Created
props.description: Read-only. The date/time a view was created.

props.name: EntryCount
props.description: Read-only. The number of documents in a view.

props.name: HeaderLines
