methods.name: FTSearch

params.name: query$
params.description: The full-text query. See below.

params.name: maxDocs%
params.description: The maximum number of documents you want returned from the search. If you want to receive all documents that match the query, specify 0.

returns.name: Long
returns.description: Long. The number of documents in the view after the search. Each of these documents matches the query$.

methods.description: After calling FTSearch, you can use the regular NotesView methods to navigate the result, which is a subset of the documents in the view. If the database is not full-text indexed, the documents in the subset are in the same order as they are in the original view. However, if the database is full-text indexed, the documents in the subset are sorted into descending order of relevance. GetFirstDocument returns the first document in the subset, GetLastDocument returns the last document, and so on. Use the Clear method to clear the full-text search filtering. The NotesView methods now navigate to the full set of documents in the view. If the database is not full-text indexed, this method works, but less efficiently. To test for an index, use the IsFTIndexed property. To create an index on a local database, use the UpdateFTIndex method.
methods.syntax: To search for a word or phrase, enter the word or phrase as is, except that search keywords must be enclosed in quotes. Remember to escape quotes if you are inside a literal. Wildcards, operators, and other syntax are permitted. For the complete syntax rules, see "Refining a search query using operators" in Notes® Help. Search for "query syntax" in the Domino® Designer Eclipse help system or information center (for example, FTSearch (NotesDatabase - JavaScript)), both of which include Notes®.

methods.name: FTSearchSorted

params.name: query
params.description: The full-text query or the intersection of multiple queries. See below for the query syntax.

params.name: maxdocs%
params.description: Optional. The maximum number of documents you want returned from the search. If you want to receive all documents that match the query, specify 0. Defaults to 0.

params.name: column
params.description: Optional. The name or 0-based index of a sorted column. A specification of NotesView.VIEW_FTSS_RELEVANCE_ORDER (512) returns results in relevance order while honoring the use of the extended flags for exact case, variants, and fuzzy search.

params.name: ascending
params.description: Optional. Sorts column data in ascending order if true, descending order if false. Defaults to true. Ignored if NotesView.VIEW_FTSS_RELEVANCE_ORDER is in effect. The availability of a column to be sorted in ascending or descending order is determined by "Click on column header to sort" on the Sorting tab of the column properties. The relevant options are Ascending, Descending, and Both. Trying to sort a column in an unsupported direction throws an NOTES_ERR_INVALIDVIEWRESORT exception.

params.name: exact
params.description: Optional. Applies the EXACTCASE filter to the search if true. Defaults to false.

params.name: variants
params.description: Optional. Returns word variants in the search results if true. Defaults to false.

params.name: fuzzy
params.description: Optional. Returns misspelled words in the search results if true. Defaults to false.

returns.name: Long
returns.description: The number of documents in the view after the search. Each of these documents matches the query.

methods.description: After calling this method, you can use the regular NotesView methods to navigate the result, which is a subset of the documents in the view. When passing in just the query argument, the documents in the subset are sorted using the default collation of 0. To retrieve the documents with a different collation pass in the name or index of the column you wish to sort on along with any optional search flags. The method GetFirstDocument returns the first document in the subset, GetLastDocument returns the last document, and so on. Use the Clear method to clear the full-text search filtering. The NotesView methods now navigate to the full set of documents in the view. If the database is not full-text indexed, this method will issue an error. To test for an index, use the IsFTIndexed property. To create an index on a local database, use the UpdateFTIndex method.
methods.syntax: To search for a word or phrase, enter the word or phrase as is, except that search keywords must be enclosed in quotes. Remember to escape quotes if you are inside a literal. Wildcards, operators, and other syntax are permitted. For the complete syntax rules, see "Refining a search query using operators" in Notes® Help. Search for "query syntax" in the Domino® Designer Eclipse help system or information center (for example, FTSearch (NotesDatabase - JavaScript)), both of which include Lotus Notes®. The query is of type Variant which can either be a single String or a Array of String which will be combined together with an AND during the search.

methods.name: GetAllDocumentsByKey

params.name: keyArray
params.type: String (variable-length only), integer, long or double value, or array of string, number, DateTime, or DateRange objects
params.description: Each element in the array is compared to a sorted column in the view. The first element in the array is compared to the first sorted column in the view; the second element is compared to the second sorted column; and so on.

params.name: exactMatch%
params.description: Optional. Specify True if you want to find an exact match. All documents that match the key exactly are returned. If you specify False (the default) or omit this parameter, a partial match succeeds. A partial match returns all documents that match the initial characters of the key.

returns.name: notesDocumentCollection
returns.description: A NotesDocumentCollection. All documents in the view whose column values match each of the values in the keyArray. If no documents match, the collection is empty and the count is zero.

methods.description: For the GetAllDocumentsByKey method to work, you must have at least one sorted column for each key. If this method is used under COM, with a keyArray parameter of an array, it must be defined as an array of type Variant. This method returns all the documents whose column values match the keys you indicate. To locate just the first document, use GetDocumentByKey.

methods.name: GetAllEntriesByKey

params.name: keyArray
params.type: String (variable-length only), integer, long or double value, or array of string, number, DateTime, or DateRange objects
params.description: Each element in the array is compared to a sorted column in the view. The first element in the array is compared to the first sorted column in the view; the second element is compared to the second sorted column; and so on.

params.name: exactMatch%
params.description: Optional. Specify True if you want to find an exact match. All entries that match the key exactly are returned. If you specify False (the default) or omit this parameter, a partial match succeeds. A partial match returns all entries that match the initial characters of the key.

returns.name: notesViewEntryCollection
returns.description: A NotesViewEntryCollection. A collection of all entries in the view whose column values match each of the values in the keyArray. If no entries match, the collection is empty and the count is zero.

methods.description: For the GetAllEntriesByKey method to work, you must have at least one sorted column for each key. If this method is used under COM, with a keyArray parameter of an array, it must be defined as an array of type Variant. This method returns all the view entries of type document whose column values match the keys. To locate just the first entry, use the GetEntryByKey method.

methods.name: GetAllReadEntries

params.name: username
params.description: Optional. If present, the method returns a NotesViewEntryCollection containing all read entries on behalf of the given name. If omitted, the method returns a NotesViewEntryCollection containing all read entries on behalf of the current user ID.

returns.name: notesViewEntryCollection
returns.description: A NotesViewEntryCollection. If no entries are read, the collection is empty and the count is zero.

methods.description: If the database does not track unread marks, all documents associated with entries are considered read.

methods.name: GetAllUnreadEntries

params.name: username
params.description: Optional. If present, the method returns a NotesViewEntryCollection containing all unread entries on behalf of the given name. If omitted, the method returns a NotesViewEntryCollection containing all unread entries on behalf of the current user ID.

returns.name: notesViewEntryCollection
returns.description: A NotesViewEntryCollection. If no entries are unread, the collection is empty and the count is zero.

methods.description: If the database does not track unread marks, all documents associated with entries are considered read.

methods.name: GetChild

params.name: document
params.description: Any document in the view. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesDocument
returns.description: A NotesDocument. The first response document to the specified document. Returns Nothing if there are no responses to the document.

methods.description: To find additional response documents, use GetNextSibling. The combination of GetChild and GetNextSibling lets you access a document's responses, sorted in the same order as they appear in a view. To get all of a document's immediate responses, unsorted, use the Responses property in NotesDocument. If you've filtered the view with the FTSearch method, GetChild returns the next document in the view.

methods.name: GetColumn

params.name: columnNumber&
params.description: A column number where 1 is the first column. Cannot be less than 1 or greater than the number of columns in the view.

returns.name: notesViewColumn
returns.description: A NotesViewColumn object. The specified column.

methods.description: Returns a specified column in a view.

methods.name: GetDocumentByKey

params.name: keyArray
params.type: String (variable-length only), integer, long or double value, or array of string, number, DateTime, or DateRange objects
params.description: Each element in the array is compared to a sorted column in the view. The first element in the array is compared to the first sorted column in the view; the second element is compared to the second sorted column; and so on.

params.name: exactMatch%
params.description: Optional. Specify True if you want to find an exact match. The first document that matches the key exactly is returned. If you specify False (the default) or omit this parameter, a partial match succeeds. A partial match returns the first document that matches the initial characters of the key.

returns.name: notesDocument
returns.description: A NotesDocument. The first document in the view whose column values match each of the values in the keyArray. If there is no matching document, the return value is Nothing.

methods.description: For the GetDocumentByKey method to work, you must have at least one sorted column for each key. If any columns are formatted with both categories and subcategories in the same column (using the "\\" special character), the method will not find the document. If this method is used under COM, with a keyArray parameter of an array, it must be defined as an array of type Variant.

methods.name: GetEntryByKey

params.name: keyArray
params.type: String (variable-length only), integer, long or double value, or array of string, number, DateTime, or DateRange objects
params.description: Each element in the array is compared to a sorted column in the view. The first element in the array is compared to the first sorted column in the view; the second element is compared to the second sorted column; and so on.

params.name: exactMatch%
params.description: Optional. Specify True if you want to find an exact match. The first entry that matches the key exactly is returned. If you specify False (the default) or omit this parameter, a partial match succeeds. Partial matches return the first entry that matches the initial characters of the key.

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: A NotesViewEntry object. The first entry in the view whose column values match each of the values in the keyArray. Returns Nothing if there is no matching entry.

methods.description: For the GetAllDocumentsByKey method to work, you must have at least one sorted column for each key. If this method is used under COM, with a keyArray parameter of an array, it must be defined as an array of type Variant. If any columns are formatted with both categories and subcategories in the same column (using the "\\" special character), the method will not find the entry.

returns.name: notesDocument
returns.description: A NotesDocument. The first document in the view.

methods.description: GetFirstDocument is useful when:

returns.name: notesDocument
returns.description: A NotesDocument. The last document in the view.

methods.name: GetNextDocument

params.name: document
params.description: Any document in the view. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesDocument
returns.description: A NotesDocument. The document following the specified document in the view. Returns Nothing if there are no more documents in the view.

methods.description: GetNextDocument returns the next document in the view regardless of what type of document it is (document, response, or response-to-response). If you want the next sibling document in the view, use GetNextSibling. When processing documents in a loop, do not delete the document, or modify it in a way that causes it to disappear from the view or for its position to change. In that situation, GetNextDocument method may not return a useful result because the document that was the next document, is now at a different position in the view. The most common symptom of this problem is that documents are skipped when you loop through them. To address this issue, change your logic so that you call GetNextDocument before making any change to the document. Or, use NotesView.AutoUpdate (= False) to prevent re-indexing of the view while you're using it.

methods.name: GetNextSibling

params.name: document
params.description: Any document in the view. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesDocument
returns.description: A NotesDocument. The document following the specified document, at the same level in the view. Returns Nothing if there are no more siblings.

methods.description: You can use GetNextSibling to: If the specified view is different from the current view, the GetNextSibling method returns the following error: "Document not found in <view>." That is, you can only navigate using the Document parameters that are from the same view in which you are navigating. If you have filtered the view with FTSearch, GetNextSibling returns the next document in the view, regardless of level.

methods.name: GetNthDocument

params.name: index&
params.description: A number indicating the document to return. Use 1 to indicate the first document in the view, 2 to indicate the second document, and so on.

returns.name: notesDocument
returns.description: A NotesDocument. The document in the specified position in the view. If there is no document at the specified position, returns Nothing.

methods.description: This method accesses only top-level (main) documents in a view; response documents are excluded. For categorized views, this method returns the first document in each main category only. If you do not have Reader access to a document in a view, this method returns the next document that you have reader access to.

methods.name: GetParentDocument

params.name: document
params.description: Any document in the view. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesDocument
returns.description: A NotesDocument. The parent of the specified document. If you have filtered the view using FTSearch, GetParentDocument simply returns the previous document in the view. Returns Nothing if the specified documentis not a response document.

methods.description: The document returned by GetParentDocument may be a main document, a response, or a response-to-response. To find a document's parent outside a view, get its ParentDocumentUNID and use the NotesDatabase method GetDocumentByUNID.

methods.name: GetPrevDocument

params.name: document
params.description: Any document in the view. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesDocument
returns.description: A NotesDocument. The document preceding the specified document. Returns Nothing if there is no preceding document.

methods.description: Given a document in a view, returns the document immediately preceding.

methods.name: GetPrevSibling

params.name: document
params.description: Any document in the view. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesDocument
returns.description: A NotesDocument. The document preceding the specified document, at the same level. Returns Nothing if there is no previous sibling in the view.

methods.description: You can use GetPrevSibling to move from one main document to the next, skipping any response documents in between. If you have filtered the view using FTSearch, GetPrevSibling returns the previous document in the view, regardless of level.

methods.name: Lock

params.name: name
params.description: Optional. The names of the lock holders. Each lock holder must be a user or group. Defaults to one lock holder: the effective user. The empty string ("") is not permitted in the array.

params.name: provisionalOK
params.description: Optional.

returns.name: flag
returns.description: Boolean.

methods.description: IsDesignLockingEnabled in NotesDatabase must be True or this method raises an error. This method: The following actions occur depending on the current lock status:

methods.name: LockProvisional

params.name: name
params.description: Optional. The names of the lock holders. Each lock holder must be a user or group. Defaults to one lock holder: the effective user. The empty string ("") is not permitted in the array.

returns.name: flag
returns.description: Boolean.

methods.description: IsDesignLockingEnabled in NotesDatabase must be True or this method raises an error. The following actions occur depending on the current lock status: If the view is modified by another user before the lock can be placed, this method raises an error.

methods.name: MarkAllRead

params.name: username
params.description: Optional. If present, the method marks all documents as read on behalf of the given name. If omitted, the method marks all documents as read on behalf of the current user ID.

methods.description: If the database does not track unread marks, all documents are considered read, and this method has no effect.

methods.name: MarkAllUnread

params.name: username
params.description: Optional. If present, the method marks all documents as unread on behalf of the given name. If omitted, the method marks all documents as unread on behalf of the current user ID.

methods.description: If the database does not track unread marks, all documents are considered read, and this method has no effect.

methods.name: Refresh
methods.description: By default, refresh is automatic when view navigation touches an update. See AutoUpdate. After a refresh, existing navigators and entries based on this NotesView object may contain invalid information. The typical error message is "Entry not found in index." This method does not refresh the front-end view that the interactive user sees, nor does it rebuild the front-end view. See ViewRefresh and ViewRebuild in NotesUIWorkspace. Use ViewRebuild if there is some possibility that the existing view entries for unmodified documents are incorrect. This can happen if the view selection formula or column formulas are something like @ToTime("today"), preventing the view indexer from realizing that the formula value can change even if the document does not. It may also make sense to use Rebuild if you know that nearly all the documents in it have changed.

methods.name: Remove
methods.description: When a view has been removed, all subsequent calls to any of its methods or properties will generate an error. Also, all subsequent calls to any methods or properties of any ViewNavigators created from the view will generate an error.

methods.name: RemoveColumn

params.name: columnNameOrIndex
params.description: The title or position of the column. Defaults to the last column.

methods.description: Positioning is 1-based. This differs from the array returned by Columns, which is 0-based.

methods.name: ResortView

params.name: columnName
params.description: The name of the column on which to sort. Defaults to the first column.

params.name: ascendingFlag
params.description: True sorts columnName ascending. False sorts columnName descending. Defaults to True.

methods.description: If no parameters are supplied, the view is resorted to the first column ascending.

methods.name: SetAliases

params.name: alias
params.description: The new aliases.

returns.name: Long
returns.description: Long. Returns 0.

methods.description: These aliases replace any existing aliases. Use the vertical bar to delineate multiple aliases. For example, "Alias1|Alias2" specifies two aliases. See Aliases to get and set aliases as an array of strings.

methods.name: UnLock
methods.description: IsDesignLockingEnabled in NotesDatabase must be True or this method raises an error. This method raises an error if the current user is not one of the lock holders and does not have lock breaking authority.

props.syntax: To get: flag = notesViewColumn.IsResize To set: notesViewColumn. IsResize = flag

props.name: IsSortDescending

props.syntax: To get: flag = notesViewColumn.IsSortDescending To set: notesViewColumn. IsSortDescending = flag

props.syntax: To get: flag = notesViewColumn.IsSorted To set: notesViewColumn. IsSorted = flag

props.description: Read-only. The programmatic name of a column, which is usually the field (item) name for a column based on a field.

props.name: ListSep
props.description: Read-write. List (multi-value) separator for values in a column.
props.syntax: To get: sep% = notesViewColumn.ListSep To set: notesViewColumn. ListSep = sep%

props.name: NumberAttrib

props.syntax: To get: attrib% = notesViewColumn.NumberAttrib To set: notesViewColumn. NumberAttrib = attrib%

props.syntax: To get: digits% = notesViewColumn.NumberDigits To set: notesViewColumn. NumberDigits = digits%

props.name: NumberFormat

props.syntax: To get: format% = notesViewColumn.NumberFormat To set: notesViewColumn. NumberFormat = format%

props.name: Parent
props.description: Read-only. The view that contains a column.

props.name: Position
props.description: Read-only. The position of a column in its view. Columns are numbered from left to right, starting with 1.

props.name: ResortToViewName
props.description: Read-write. The name of the target view for a user-sorted column that allows the user to change to another view.
props.syntax: To get: viewName = notesViewColumn.ResortToViewName To set: notesViewColumn. ResortToViewName = viewName

props.name: SecondaryResortColumnIndex
props.description: Read-write. The index of the secondary sorting column of a user-sorted column that allows a secondary sorting column.
props.syntax: To get: columnIndex = notesViewColumn.SecondaryResortColumnIndex To set: notesViewColumn. SecondaryResortColumnIndex = columnIndex

props.syntax: To get: fmt% = notesViewColumn.TimeDateFmt To set: notesViewColumn.TimeDateFmt = fmt%

props.syntax: To get: fmt% = notesViewColumn.TimeFmt To set: notesViewColumn.TimeFmt = fmt%

props.name: TimeZoneFmt
props.description: Read-write. The time zone format in time-date data in a column.
props.syntax: To get: fmt% = notesViewColumn.TimeZoneFmt To set: notesViewColumn.TimeZoneFmt = fmt%

props.name: Title
props.description: Read-write. The title of a column, if any.
props.syntax: To get: title$ = notesViewColumn.Title To set: notesViewColumn.Title = title$

props.name: Width

props.syntax: To get: width% = notesViewColumn.Width To set: notesViewColumn.Width = width%

nodes.name: NotesViewEntry
nodes.description: Represents a view entry. A view entry represents a row in a view.

props.name: ChildCount
props.description: Read-only. The number of immediate children belonging to the current view entry.

props.name: ColumnIndentLevel
props.description: Read-only. The columns indent level of a view entry.

props.name: ColumnValues
props.description: Read-only. The value of each column in the view entry.

props.name: DescendantCount
props.description: Read-only. The number of descendants belonging to the current view entry.

props.name: Document
props.description: Read-only. The document associated with the view entry.

props.name: FTSearchScore
props.description: Read-only. The full-text search score of a view entry, if it was retrieved as part of a full-text search.

props.name: IndentLevel
props.description: Read-only. The indent level of a view entry.

props.name: IsCategory
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether a view entry is a category.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesViewEntry .IsCategory Legal values

props.name: IsConflict
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether a view entry contains a document on which a replication or save conflict occurred.

props.name: IsDocument
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether a view entry is a document.

props.name: IsTotal
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether a view entry is a total.

props.name: IsValid
props.description: Read-only. Indicates whether a view entry is a valid entry.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesViewEntry .IsValid Legal values

props.name: NoteID
props.description: Read-only. The prefix "NT" followed by the note ID of a view entry.

props.name: Parent
props.description: Read-only. The parent view, view entry collection (COM only), or view navigator (COM only) of a view entry.

props.name: SiblingCount
props.description: Read-only. The number of siblings belonging to the current view entry.

props.name: UniversalID
props.description: Read-only. The universal ID of a document, associated with a view entry. The ID is a 32-character combination of hexadecimal digits (0-9, A-F) that uniquely identifies a document across all replicas of a database.

methods.name: GetPosition

params.name: separator$
params.description: The separator in the return value.

returns.name: String
returns.description: String. A series of integers and separators. The integers indicate the positions of the view entry at each level, where 1 is the first position. The first integer indicates the first level, and so on.

methods.description: Returns the position of the entry in the view hierarchy; for example, "2.3" for the third document of the second category.

methods.name: GetRead method

params.name: username
params.description: Text. If supplied, the username who has or has not read the entry. Otherwise, defaults to the name of the current user.

returns.name: flag
returns.description: Boolean. True if the current entry has been read by username. False if it has not been read by username.

methods.description: Returns true if the current entry has been read, otherwise false.

nodes.name: NotesViewEntryCollection
nodes.description: Represents a collection of view entries, selected according to specific criteria. This class provides you mainly with a collection of documents in a view-sorted order.

props.name: Count
props.description: Read-only. The number of entries in a collection.

props.name: Parent
props.description: Read-only. The view that contains a view entry collection.

props.name: Query
props.description: Read-only. The text of the query that produced a view entry collection, if the collection results from a full-text or other search.

methods.name: AddEntry

params.name: addentry
params.type: ViewEntry object representing the entry to be added
params.description: If the specified entry is a Category or Total entry, an error is returned. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

params.name: checkDups
params.description: Optional. Specify True to check for duplicate entries. The default is False.

methods.description: For sorted collections, the entry is appended. For unsorted collections, the entry is inserted using an internal algorithm. Sorted collections include all ViewEntryCollections and DocumentCollections created from FTSearch (including DocumentCollections modified by calling the FTSearch method on the collection). All other DocumentCollections are unsorted.

methods.name: Clone
methods.description: Returns a NotesViewEntryCollection object which is a copy of the original collection.

methods.name: Contains

params.name: inputNotes
params.type: String, NotesDocument, NotesDocumentCollection, NotesViewEntry or NotesViewEntryCollection
params.description: The NotesDocument, NotesDocuments, NotesViewEntry, or NotesViewEntries that the method determines are or are not contained in the NotesViewEntryCollection. A single NotesDocument can be specified by note ID (a String value) or NotesDocument object. Multiple NotesDocuments must be specified as a NotesDocumentCollection. A single NotesViewEntry object can be specified, as long as the NotesViewEntry object has a NotesDocument associated with it. Multiple NotesViewEntry objects that have NotesDocuments associated with them, can be specified as a NotesViewEntryCollection.

methods.description: The document or documents whose containment this method determines (or the view entries associated with those documents) must be in the same database as the original collection. Otherwise, the method will return the error "the specified note or notes do not exist in the database" or, if a noteID was passed to the method that matches a noteID in the original collection's database, the method will use the unintended document. If inputNotes is an empty collection, this method will return True.

methods.name: DeleteEntry

params.name: deleteentry
params.description: The view entry to be deleted. If the specified entry is a Category or Total entry, an error is returned. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

methods.description: Deletes an entry from a collection.

methods.name: FTSearch

params.name: query$
params.description: The full-text query. See the following for the syntax.

params.name: maxDocs%
params.description: The maximum number of entries you want returned from the query. Set this parameter to 0 to receive all matching entries.

methods.description: This method moves the current pointer to the first document in the collection. The collection of documents that match the full-text query is sorted by relevance with highest relevance first. You can access the relevance score of each document in the collection using the FTSearchScore property in NotesDocument. If the database is not full-text indexed, this method works, but less efficiently. To test for an index, use the IsFTIndexed property in NotesDatabase. To create an index on a local database, use the UpdateFTIndex method.
methods.syntax: To search for a word or phrase, enter the word or phrase as is, except for search keywords, which must be enclosed in quotes. Remember to escape quotes if you are inside a literal. Wildcards, operators, and other syntax are permitted. For the complete syntax rules, see "Refining a search query using operators" in Notes® Help. Search for "query syntax" in the Domino® Designer Eclipse help system or information center (for example, FTSearch (NotesDatabase - JavaScript)), both of which include Notes®.

methods.name: GetEntry

params.name: entry
params.type: ViewEntry or Document object representing the entry to be retrieved
params.description: If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: The view entry to be retrieved. If the specified entry is not in the collection, returns Nothing.

methods.description: The effect of this method is to move the current pointer.

methods.name: GetFirstEntry

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: The first view entry in the collection. If there are no entries in the collection, returns Nothing.

methods.description: Gets the first entry in a view collection.

methods.name: GetLastEntry

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: The last view entry in the collection. If there are no entries in the collection, returns Nothing.

methods.description: Gets the last entry in a view collection.

methods.name: GetNextEntry

params.name: currententry
params.description: An entry in the view collection. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: If no parameter, the entry following the current entry. If a parameter, the entry following the specified entry. If there is no next entry, returns Nothing.

methods.description: Gets the entry immediately following the current one or a specified entry in a view collection.

methods.name: GetNthEntry

params.name: index&
params.description: A number indicating the entry to return. Use 1 to indicate the first entry in the collection, 2 to indicate the second entry, and so on.

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: The entry in the nth position in the view collection. If there is no entry at the specified position, returns Nothing.

methods.description: View collections are renumbered when deletions occur so that the positions of entries after the deleted entry change. Using getNthEntry to iterate through a loop is strongly discouraged for performance reasons. See GetFirstEntry, GetLastEntry, GetNextEntry, and GetPrevEntry for the preferred loop structure.

methods.name: GetPrevEntry

params.name: currententry
params.description: An entry in the view collection. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: If no parameter, the entry preceding the current entry. If a parameter, the entry preceding the specified entry. If there is no previous entry, returns Nothing.

methods.description: Gets the entry immediately preceding the current one or a specified entry in a view collection.

methods.name: Intersect

params.name: inputNotes
params.type: String, NotesDocument, NotesDocumentCollection, NotesViewEntry or NotesViewEntryCollection
params.description: The NotesDocument, NotesDocuments, NotesViewEntry, or NotesViewEntries that the method will intersect with the NotesViewEntryCollection. A single NotesDocument can be specified by note ID (a String value) or NotesDocument object. Multiple NotesDocuments must be specified as a NotesDocumentCollection. A single NotesViewEntry object can be specified, as long as the NotesViewEntry object has a NotesDocument associated with it. Multiple NotesViewEntry objects that have NotesDocuments associated with them, can be specified as a NotesViewEntryCollection.

params.name: maintainOrder
params.description: Specifies that after the Intersect operation is complete, whatever order the originating view was in when the NotesEntryCollection was created will remain in force for subsequent entry processing. That is, either the default view order will be used or that last set by calling ResortView.

methods.description: The document or documents (or the view entries and their associated documents) being intersected must be in the same database as the original document collection. Otherwise, the method will return the error "the specified note or notes do not exist in the database" or, if a noteID was passed to the method that matches a noteID in the original collection's database, the method will use the unintended document.

methods.name: MarkAllRead

params.name: username
params.description: Optional. If present, the method marks all of the documents in the collection as read on behalf of the given name. If omitted, the method marks all of the documents in the collection as read on behalf of the current user ID.

methods.description: If the database does not track unread marks, all documents are considered read, and this method has no effect.

methods.name: MarkAllUnread

params.name: username
params.description: Optional. If present, the method marks all of the documents in the collection as unread on behalf of the given name. If omitted, the method marks all of the documents in the collection as unread on behalf of the current user ID.

methods.description: If the database does not track unread marks, all documents are considered read, and this method has no effect.

methods.name: Merge

params.name: inputNotes
params.type: String, NotesDocument, NotesDocumentCollection, NotesViewEntry or NotesViewEntryCollection
params.description: The NotesDocument, NotesDocuments, NotesViewEntry or NotesViewEntries that the method will merge with the NotesViewEntryCollection. A single NotesDocument can be specified by note ID (a String value) or NotesDocument object. Multiple NotesDocuments must be specified as a NotesDocumentCollection. A single NotesViewEntry object can be specified, as long as the NotesViewEntry object has a NotesDocument associated with it. Multiple NotesViewEntry objects that have NotesDocuments associated with them, can be specified as a NotesViewEntryCollection.

methods.description: The document or documents (or the view entries and their associated documents) being merged by this method must be in the same database as the original collection. Otherwise, the method will return the error "the specified note or notes do not exist in the database" or, if a noteID was passed to the method that matches a noteID in the original collection's database, the method will use the unintended document. This method performs a union of the two collections.

methods.name: PutAllInFolder

params.name: foldername$
params.description: The name of the folder in which to place the documents. The folder may be personal if the script is running on the workstation. If the folder is within another folder, specify a path to it, separating folder names with backward slashes, for example, "Vehicles\Bikes".

params.name: createonfail
params.description: Optional. Specify True so that a folder will be created if it does not exist.

methods.description: If a document is already inside the folder you specify, PutAllInFolder does nothing for that document. If you specify a path to a folder, and none of the folders exist, the method creates all of them for you.

methods.name: RemoveAll

params.name: force
params.description: If True, a document is removed even if another user modifies the document after it is retrieved. If False, a document is not removed if another document modifies it first.

methods.description: Resets current pointer to the beginning of the collection.

methods.name: RemoveAllFromFolder

params.name: foldername$
params.description: The name of the folder from which to remove the documents. The folder may be personal if the script is running on a workstation. If the folder is within another folder, specify a path to it separating folder names with backward slashes, for example, "Vehicles\Bikes".

methods.description: The method does nothing for documents not in the folder you specify. This method does nothing if the folder you specify does not exist.

methods.name: StampAll

params.name: itemname$
params.description: The name of the item.

params.name: value
params.description: A value appropriate for the item type.

methods.description: If the item does not exist, it is created. The item values are immediately written to the documents on the server. You do not have to use the Save method of NotesDocument after StampAll. However, any documents modified by your script must be saved before calling StampAll. This method does not modify existing NotesDocument objects. Documents must be retrieved again to see the changes.

methods.name: StampAllMulti

params.name: document
params.description: The document contains multiple items, each with values appropriate for the item type.

methods.description: If an item does not exist, it is created. If the item is of a different data type, the existing item will be deleted and a new item of the new data type created. The item values are immediately written to the documents on the server. You do not have to use the Save method of NotesDocument after StampAllMulti. However, any documents modified by your script must be saved before calling StampAllMulti. This method does not modify existing NotesDocument objects. Documents must be retrieved again to see the changes.

methods.name: Subtract

params.name: inputNotes
params.type: String, NotesDocument, NotesDocumentCollection, NotesViewEntry or NotesViewEntryCollection
params.description: The NotesDocument, NotesDocuments, NotesViewEntry, or NotesViewEntries that the method will subtract from the NotesViewEntryCollection. A single NotesDocument can be specified by note ID (a String value) or NotesDocument object. Multiple NotesDocuments must be specified as a NotesDocumentCollection. A single NotesViewEntry object can be specified, as long as the NotesViewEntry object has a NotesDocument associated with it. Multiple NotesViewEntry objects that have NotesDocuments associated with them, can be specified as a NotesViewEntryCollection.

params.name: maintainOrder
params.description: Specifies that after the Subtract operation is complete, whatever order the originating view was in when the NotesEntryCollection was created will remain in force for subsequent entry processing. That is, either the default view order will be used or that last set by calling ResortView.

methods.description: The document or documents (or the view entries and their associated documents) being subtracted must be in the same database as the original document collection. Otherwise, the method will return the error "the specified note or notes do not exist in the database" or, if a noteID was passed to the method that matches a noteID in the original collection's database, the method will use the unintended document.

methods.name: UpdateAll
methods.description: See the UpdateProcessedDoc method in NotesSession for a description of the update process.

nodes.name: NotesViewNavigator
nodes.description: Represents a view navigator. A view navigator provides access to all or a subset of the entries in a view.

props.name: CacheSize
props.description: Read-write. The size of the navigator cache in view entries.
props.syntax: To get: size& = notesViewNavigator .CacheSize To set: notesViewNavigator .CacheSize = size&

props.name: Count
props.description: Read-only. The number of entries represented by the navigator.

props.name: MaxLevel
props.description: Read-write. The maximum level of navigation for the navigator.
props.syntax: To get: maxlevel& = notesViewNavigator .MaxLevel To set: notesViewNavigator .MaxLevel = maxlevel&

props.name: ParentView
props.description: Read-only. The parent view of a navigator.

methods.name: GetChild

params.name: entry
params.description: An entry in the view. Defaults to the current entry. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: NotesViewEntry. The first child of the current or specified entry. Returns Nothing if there are no children.

methods.description: To find additional response documents, use GetNextSibling. The combination of GetChild and GetNextSibling lets you access document responses, sorted in the same order that they appear in a view. To get all of the immediate responses to a document, unsorted, use the Responses property in NotesDocument.

methods.name: GetCurrent

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: NotesViewEntry. The current entry.

methods.description: If the navigator is empty, the NotesViewEntry return value is not set, nor is an error raised. However, an error is raised when you attempt to use the NotesViewEntry value. You must insert your own code to handle this condition.

methods.name: GetEntry

params.name: entry
params.description: The name of an entry in the view. Could be a NotesDocument or a NotesViewEntry object.

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: NotesViewEntry. The specified entry of the navigator. Returns Nothing if there are no entries.

methods.description: This method moves the current pointer to the retrieved entry unless the return value is Nothing. Unlike all the other ViewNavigator methods, the entry need not come from the current navigator. For instance, you can access an entry in the navigator from a document created via a document collection navigation. If the specified entry is a document, or came from a ViewEntryCollection, GetEntry method returns the first entry in the view for that entry. The entry may appear in multiple categories.

methods.name: GetFirst

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: NotesViewEntry. The first entry of the navigator. Returns Nothing if there are no entries.

methods.description: This method moves the current pointer to the retrieved entry, unless the return value is Nothing.

methods.name: GetFirstDocument

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: NotesViewEntry. The first document entry of the navigator. Returns Nothing if there are no entries.

methods.description: This method moves the current pointer to the retrieved entry unless the return value is Nothing.

methods.name: GetLast

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: NotesViewEntry. The last entry of the navigator. Returns Nothing if there are no entries.

methods.description: This method moves the current pointer to the retrieved entry unless the return value is Nothing.

methods.name: GetLastDocument

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: NotesViewEntry. The last document entry of the navigator. Returns Nothing if there are no entries.

methods.description: This method moves the current pointer to the retrieved entry unless the return value is Nothing.

methods.name: GetNext

params.name: entry
params.description: An entry in the view. Defaults to the current entry. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: NotesViewEntry. The entry following the current or specified entry. Returns Nothing if there is no next entry.

methods.description: This method moves the current pointer to the retrieved entry unless the return value is Nothing.

methods.name: GetNextCategory

params.name: entry
params.description: An entry in the view. Defaults to the current entry. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: NotesViewEntry. The category entry following the current or specified entry. Returns Nothing if there is no next category entry.

methods.description: This method moves the current pointer to the retrieved entry unless the return value is Nothing.

methods.name: GetNextDocument

params.name: entry
params.description: An entry in the view. Defaults to the current entry. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: NotesViewEntry. The document entry following the current or specified entry. Returns Nothing if there is no next document entry.

methods.description: This method moves the current pointer to the retrieved entry unless the return value is Nothing. When processing documents in a loop, do not delete the document, or modify it in a way that causes it to disappear from the view or for its position to change. In that situation, GetNextDocument method may not return a useful result because the document that was the next document, is now at a different position in the view. The most common symptom of this problem is that documents are skipped when you loop through them. To address this issue, change your logic so that you call GetNextDocument before making any change to the document. Or, use NotesView.AutoUpdate (= False) to prevent re-indexing of the view while you're using it.

methods.name: GetNextSibling

params.name: entry
params.description: An entry in the view. Defaults to the current entry. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: NotesViewEntry. The sibling entry following the current or specified entry. Returns Nothing if there is no next sibling entry.

methods.description: This method moves the current pointer to the retrieved entry unless the return value is Nothing.

methods.name: GetNth

params.name: index&
params.description: Position of the entry, where 1 is the first entry.

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: NotesViewEntry. The specified top-level entry. Returns Nothing if there is no such entry.

methods.description: This method moves the current pointer to the retrieved entry unless the return value is null. If you do not have Reader access to a document in a view, this method returns the next document to which you have reader access. Using GetNth to iterate through a loop is strongly discouraged for performance reasons. See GetFirst, GetFirstDocument, GetLast, GetLastDocument, GetNext, GetNextCategory, GetNextDocument, GetNextSibling, GetPrev, GetPrevCategory, GetPrevDocument, and GetPrevSibling for the preferred loop structure.

methods.name: GetParent

params.name: entry
params.description: An entry in the view. Defaults to the current entry. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: NotesViewEntry. The parent entry of the current or specified entry. Returns Nothing if there is no parent entry.

methods.description: This method moves the current pointer to the retrieved entry unless the return value is Nothing.

methods.name: GetPos

params.name: position$
params.description: A position in decimal format; for example, "1.2.3" is the third response to the second response to the first entry, using the period as a level separator.

params.name: separator$
params.description: The separator between position levels.

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: NotesViewEntry. The entry at the specified position. Returns Nothing if there is no entry at that position.

methods.description: This method moves the current pointer to the retrieved entry unless the return value is Nothing.

methods.name: GetPrev

params.name: entry
params.description: An entry in the view. Defaults to the current entry. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: NotesViewEntry. The entry preceding the current or specified entry. Returns Nothing if there is no previous entry.

methods.description: This method moves the current pointer to the retrieved entry unless the return value is Nothing.

methods.name: GetPrevCategory

params.name: entry
params.description: An entry in the view. Defaults to the current entry. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: NotesViewEntry. The category entry preceding the current or specified entry. Returns Nothing if there is no previous category entry.

methods.description: This method moves the current pointer to the retrieved entry unless the return value is Nothing.

methods.name: GetPrevDocument

params.name: entry
params.description: An entry in the view. Defaults to the current entry. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: NotesViewEntry. The document entry preceding the current or specified entry. Returns Nothing if there is no previous document entry.

methods.description: This method moves the current pointer to the retrieved entry unless the return value is Nothing.

methods.name: GetPrevSibling

params.name: entry
params.description: An entry in the view. Defaults to the current entry. If you specify Nothing, this method generates an error.

returns.name: notesViewEntry
returns.description: NotesViewEntry. The sibling entry preceding the current or specified entry. Returns Nothing if there is no previous sibling entry.

methods.description: This method moves the current pointer to the retrieved entry unless the return value is Nothing.

methods.name: GotoChild

params.name: entry
params.description: An entry in the view.

methods.description: If there is no child, the current pointer stays where it is. No exception is raised. You must insert your own code (for example, comparing positions) to detect this condition. A child of a category entry can be either a category or document entry.

methods.name: GotoEntry

params.name: objUnknown
params.description: A NotesDocument or NotesViewEntry object.

methods.description: The Document or ViewEntry object provided, as the parameter does not need to come from the current view navigator. The gotoEntry method searches for the existence of the entry in the current view navigator. If there is no entry for the document or view entry, the current pointer stays where it is. No exception is raised. You must insert your own code (for example, comparing positions) to detect this condition.

methods.name: GotoFirst
methods.description: Moves the current pointer to the first entry in a view navigator.

methods.name: GotoFirstDocument
methods.description: Moves the current pointer to the first document entry in a view navigator, skipping any category entries.

methods.name: GotoLast
methods.description: Moves the current pointer to the last entry in a view navigator.

methods.name: GotoLastDocument
methods.description: Moves the current pointer to the last document entry in a view navigator, skipping any totals and category entries.

methods.name: GotoNext

params.name: entry
params.description: An entry in the view.

methods.description: If there is no next entry, the current pointer stays where it is. No exception is raised. You must insert your own code (for example, comparing positions) to detect this condition.

methods.name: GotoNextCategory

params.name: entry
params.description: An entry in the view.

methods.description: If there is no next category entry, the current pointer stays where it is. No exception is raised. You must insert your own code (for example, comparing positions) to detect this condition.

methods.name: GotoNextDocument

params.name: entry
params.description: An entry in the view.

methods.description: If there is no next document entry, the current pointer stays where it is. No exception is raised. You must insert your own code (for example, comparing positions) to detect this condition.

methods.name: GotoNextSibling

params.name: entry
params.description: An entry in the view.

methods.description: If there is no next sibling entry, the current pointer stays where it is. No exception is raised. You must insert your own code (for example, comparing positions) to detect this condition.

methods.name: GotoParent

params.name: entry
params.description: An entry in the view.

methods.description: If there is no next parent entry, the current pointer stays where it is. No exception is raised. You must insert your own code (for example, comparing positions) to detect this condition.

methods.name: GotoPos

params.name: pos$
params.description: A position in decimal format; for example, "1.2.3" is the third child to the second child to the first entry, using the period as a level separator.

params.name: separator$
params.description: The separator between position levels.

methods.description: If there is no entry at the specified position, the current pointer stays where it is. No exception is raised. You must insert your own code (for example, comparing positions) to detect this condition. This method raises the error lsERR_NOTES_VIEWNAV_BAD_POS 4393 ("Invalid position string") if the position parameter syntax is incorrect.

methods.name: GotoPrev

params.name: entry
params.description: An entry in the view.

methods.description: If there is no previous entry, the current pointer stays where it is. No exception is raised. You must insert your own code (for example, comparing positions) to detect this condition.

methods.name: GotoPrevCategory

params.name: entry
params.description: An entry in the view.

methods.description: If there is no previous category entry, the current pointer stays where it is. No exception is raised. You must insert your own code (for example, comparing positions) to detect this condition.

methods.name: GotoPrevDocument

params.name: entry
params.description: An entry in the view.

methods.description: If there is no previous document entry, the current pointer stays where it is. No exception is raised. You must insert your own code (for example, comparing positions) to detect this condition.

methods.name: GotoPrevSibling

params.name: entry
params.description: An entry in the view.

methods.description: If there is no previous sibling entry, the current pointer stays where it is. No exception is raised. You must insert your own code (for example, comparing positions) to detect this condition.

methods.name: MarkAllRead

params.name: username
params.description: Optional. If present, the method marks all of the documents in the navigator as read on behalf of the given name. If omitted, the method marks all of the documents in the navigator as read on behalf of the current user ID.

methods.description: If the database does not track unread marks, all documents are considered read, and this method has no effect.

methods.name: MarkAllUnread

params.name: username
params.description: Optional. If present, the method marks all of the documents in the navigator as unread on behalf of the given name. If omitted, the method marks all of the documents in the navigator as unread on behalf of the current user ID.

methods.description: If the database does not track unread marks, all documents are considered read, and this method has no effect.

nodes.name: NotesXMLProcessor
nodes.description: A base class containing properties and methods common to all XML processing classes.

props.name: ExitOnFirstFatalError
props.description: Read-write. Indicates that processing should terminate if any fatal error is encountered.
props.syntax: To get: flag = notesXMLProcessor .ExitOnFirstFatalError To set: notesXMLProcessor .ExitOnFirstFatalError = flag

props.name: Log
props.description: Read-only. An XML representation of warnings, errors, and fatal errors generated by the processor.

props.name: LogComment
props.description: Read-write. A comment added to the top of the log.
props.syntax: To get: comment$ = notesXMLProcessor .LogComment To set: notesXMLProcessor .LogComment = comment$

methods.name: Process
methods.description: Before calling this method, you must specify the input and output for the conversion. To do this either use the processor's SetInput and SetOutput methods or use the parameters of one of the following create methods: The process method for NotesDOMParser raises the PostDOMParse event. Before calling this method for NotesDXLImporter, set ACLImportOption, DesignImportOption, and DocumentImportOption.

methods.name: SetInput

params.name: Input
params.description: For NotesDXLExporter this parameter is an object of the following type:

methods.description: This method overrides the input parameter of CreateDOMParser, CreateDXLExporter, CreateDXLImporter, CreateSAXParser, or CreateXSLTransformer. The SetInput parameter, or the input parameter to any of the preceding methods, must be explicitly declared and strongly typed (cannot be a variant). You cannot explicitly read or write a NotesStream object associated with a file prior to using it for XML input or output. For example, if you write to a file then use it for XML input, you must close and reopen the NotesStream object.

methods.name: SetOutput

params.name: output
params.description: For NotesDXLImporter this parameter is a NotesDatabase object.

methods.description: This method overrides the output parameter of CreateDOMParser, CreateDXLExporter, CreateDXLImporter, CreateSAXParser, or CreateXSLTransformer. The output medium need not be specified for a pipeline operation where this object is the input parameter to another XML process object. You cannot explicitly read or write a NotesStream object associated with a file prior to using it for XML input or output. For example, if you write to a file then use it for XML input, you must close and reopen the NotesStream object.

nodes.name: NotesXSLTransformer

props.name: InputValidationOption
props.description: Read-write. Indicates that if a DTD is specified in the XML declaration statement, it should be used to validate the input XML.
props.syntax: To get: option% = notesXSLTransformer .InputValidationOption To set: notesXSLTransformer .InputValidationOption = option%

props.name: Log
props.description: Read-only. An XML representation of warnings, errors, and fatal errors generated by the processor.

props.name: LogComment

props.syntax: To get: comment$ = notesXMLProcessor .LogComment To set: notesXMLProcessor .LogComment = comment$
